events.title: JOE DOMBROWSKI
events.description:
Recently named one of Variety ’s Top 10 Comics to Watch, Detroit native Joe Dombrowski first caught the world’s attention in 2017 with a viral April Fools’ Day spelling test prank that led to multiple appearances on The Ellen DeGeneres Show . Don’t get it twisted, though—it takes 20 years to be an overnight sensation! Joe’s been performing stand-up since his third-grade talent show, and he hasn’t stopped since.

Today, Joe tours internationally with material that highlights his marriage, being a new dad, his accidentally hilarious parents and unforgettable years as an elementary school teacher. His comedy has been featured on The Late Show with Stephen Colbert , the Just For Laughs Festival in Montreal, the New York Comedy Festival, and Netflix Is a Joke Fest.

Joe is also the co-host of the hit live comedy/game show My Straight Friends , running monthly at The World Famous Comedy Store in Los Angeles and Emerald City Comedy Club in Seattle. Joe currently resides in Seattle, Washington where he hopes to run for Mayor sometime. And by Mayor he means PTA president.

No refunds or exchanges.
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest. 
Ages 21+

events.title: [ONLINE] EE Business Networking @Lunch (free!)
events.description: Why go it alone? It only takes one person to change our lives personally or professionally. Who will you meet today at “EE“? See additional info and direct links below… Exceptional Entrepreneurs is one of the USA’s largest business networking organizations – and one of the best values in business networking. At EE: There is no membership required. There is no attendance requirement. There is no business profession or category exclusivity. ALL business professions are welcome to attend! Meeting Agenda: First 30 minutes: check-in & breakout rooms open. Remaining 60 minutes: structured agenda with everyone in main room, including introductions by participants + more! While meetings end on time (for those who need to go), we also open breakout rooms again for those wanting to meet with other attendees afterwards (at least 30 minutes, usually longer.. fyi). Interested in being the featured speaker for this event? Information and registration is at: http://EEspeaker.com Info to join this EE business networking meeting: ####################################### http://nroi.us/eeonline (the shortlink above will remain updated with the current Zoom link) Passcode: 7772021 ####################################### Limited to the first 100 participants. We also cross-livestream through the EE FB Group. No participant limit on FB, however it’s view-only mode. Please feel free to leave your contact info in the comments of the livestream when listening along: https://www.facebook.com/groups/networkingeesd The meetings (including chat) will be recorded to the cloud – and made available temporarily afterwards. Information on recordings will be provided to attendees on Zoom and Facebook. Exceptional Entrepreneurs was founded November, 2008 in San Diego, California USA by David & Gina Demangos. Since then, “EE” has become one of the USA’s largest business networking organizations, due to the Demangos’ vision and leadership to provide “exceptional” opportunities for connecting local professionals. David Demangos is an eXp real estate agent in San Diego. For more information on David, please see: Awesome San Diego Real Estate: http://www.awesomesandiegorealestate.com “Effective networking is a learned skill. Exceptional Entrepreneurs are highly trusted professionals who share ideas and know that in order to receive you must first be willing to give.” – David Demangos Thank you for your continuing support of “EE”! events.title: Nat at Night
events.description: On Fridays, the San Diego Natural History Museum is open until 11 PM just for you. Enjoy access to all open exhibitions and our rooftop deck for good views and great vibes. The rooftop is open to all visitors and serves beer, wine, non-alcoholic drinks, and food from Wolf in the Woods. Take a selfie with a megalodon shark, see scientists at work in our Paleo Center, admire glittering gems, get a close up look at spiders and snakes, and so much more. Best of all, get a look at our brand-new exhibition, Washed Ashore: Art to Save the Sea and grab a drink in the dive bar while chatting with a scientist. On select dates, channel your inner artist with Sketchparty, get competitive during trivia, or unwind while listening to the rooftop DJ. Hungry for more? The Craft Taco, located inside the Atrium, is a vibrant taco restaurant offering a delectable fusion of flavors and artisanal creations. They offer made-to-order tacos featuring signature blue corn tortillas, delectable burritos and bowls, and a variety of drinks and coffees. Admission is half-price after 5 PM, and parking is free after 6 PM. You can buy your tickets online in advance or buy them at the door the night of. As always, members of The Nat and Explorer pass holders enjoy free general admission. Fill your summer with wonder at The Nat. With weekly Nat at Night, brunches, weekend family programming, and so much more, membership just makes sense. Not to mention, you’re directly supporting nature conservation in our region. Join us today.

events.description: Modern is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards Cards from Core Set Eighth Edition and Mirrodin through today No maximum deck size, as long as you can shuffle your deck unassisted Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and Wastes), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age block Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forest are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block.

events.title: GEN X-RATED COMEDY
events.description:
The Gen X-Rated comedy showcase will feature some of the best San Diego comics who experienced the 80s and 90s firshand. These comics watched evolution unfold--from social cues to social media, from tape cassettes to playlists and from MTV to TikTok. Don't miss this opportunity to take a trip down memory lane with a generation that witnessed it all! These fine comics will deliver hilarious performances that will give you a history lesson that screams they were actually 'woke' back then too! They will make you laugh out loud as they riff on these trendsetting decades. You will also get the flipside and get a millennial's take on this old school generation that defined a moment in time. You do not want to miss this epic show!

No refunds or exchanges.

JOE DOMBROWSKI
events.description:
Recently named one of Variety ’s Top 10 Comics to Watch, Detroit native Joe Dombrowski first caught the world’s attention in 2017 with a viral April Fools’ Day spelling test prank that led to multiple appearances on The Ellen DeGeneres Show . Don’t get it twisted, though—it takes 20 years to be an overnight sensation! Joe’s been performing stand-up since his third-grade talent show, and he hasn’t stopped since.

Today, Joe tours internationally with material that highlights his marriage, being a new dad, his accidentally hilarious parents and unforgettable years as an elementary school teacher. His comedy has been featured on The Late Show with Stephen Colbert , the Just For Laughs Festival in Montreal, the New York Comedy Festival, and Netflix Is a Joke Fest.

Joe is also the co-host of the hit live comedy/game show My Straight Friends , running monthly at The World Famous Comedy Store in Los Angeles and Emerald City Comedy Club in Seattle. Joe currently resides in Seattle, Washington where he hopes to run for Mayor sometime. And by Mayor he means PTA president.

events.title: Tai Chi Ch’uan Lee Style
events.description: A class session consists of gentle limbering exercises that aid in coordination, balance, and the opening of various channels within the body to allow your Chi to flow freely. In addition, we incorporate respiration therapy such as our style’s form of Qigong, K’ai Men, otherwise known as Taoist Yoga and various specialized breathing exercises referred to as ‘Dao Yin’. Moreover, we include partner exercises that reinforce the underlying principles of being tenderly gentle, soft, and giving way to all force, provided that our style requires absolutely no physical strength. Furthermore, Taoist physical exercises include parts of the art of Tai Chi Ch’uan: The form of Tai Chi Ch’uan and Tai Chi Dance. The physical movements of Tai Chi Ch’uan are gentle, natural and an exceptionally light therapeutic exercise.

events.title: Online: Saturday Study Group
events.description: We are continuing our Study group online for the time being. We are now meeting online in the San Diego Python Discord server! Here is the link to join: https://discord.gg/8GpWuVvdbQ There is a “meetup” voice/video channel that we will be using! We may start meeting in person soon! This will be discussed at the meetup. We are considering having some online, some in-person. Decisions, decisions! Every Saturday we meet up with the larger Python community (including San Diego PyLadies (https://www.meetup.com/sd-pyladies), Open BioInformatics San Diego (https://www.meetup.com/Open-Bioinformatics-SanDiego/) and San Diego Artificial Intelligence (https://www.meetup.com/SanDiegoAI/)) to chat, learn, and hang out. Our study group is for everyone on their Python journey, whether you’re just starting out, an experienced pro, or anywhere in between. There’s no formal instruction, just a bunch of folks who like to code. Sometimes we talk about a specific subject, sometimes answer questions, whatever! Python Community Code of Conduct (http://www.pythonsd.org/coc): Python is much more than a software language. Python is a vibrant community made up of members from around the globe with a diverse set of skills, personalities, and experiences. It is through these differences that our community experiences great successes and continued growth. Overall, we’re good to each other. We contribute to this community not because we have to, but because we want to.

events.title: CEDH – Bronze Event
events.description: TopDeck Championship Series – Bronze Event A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition. *Event May be downgraded to based on attendance*

events.title: PAULIE CALAFIORE
events.description:
Paulie Calafiore came up on the soccer field and on reality TV long 
before comedy. First gaining notice on Big Brother Season 18 and later 
becoming one of The Challenge’s most talked-about competitors, he turned
real-life drama into must-watch TV. Before reality fame, the New Jersey
native captained his high school and Rutgers University soccer teams, 
then went on to train as a bobsledder for Team USA and chase every 
athletic edge he could find. Along the way he’s been admired, debated, 
and yes, accused of being a charmer - but lately he’s been rewriting his
own script, openly embracing his bisexual identity and bringing that 
honesty (and plenty of laughs) to every room he walks into. Now Paulie 
takes the mic to blend his world of reality TV, competitive highs, 
awkward lows, Jersey swagger, and sharp observations into a set that’s 
personal, unpredictable, and impossible to ignore.

events.title: Where is AI in 2026 and Where is it Going?
events.description: A free, public workshop — no technical background needed. ​In just a few years, AI has advanced at a pace that stunned even leading experts. These systems aren’t science fiction anymore — they’re reshaping work, relationships, and even crime in real time. This workshop explores where AI may be in the next 1–5 years and what that means for your daily life. ​Together, we’ll tackle urgent questions like: ​Jobs: Could AI lead to mass unemployment — or entirely new kinds of work? ​Crime: How might AI supercharge scams, hacking, and even physical crime? ​Companions: Will people form deep emotional bonds — or even romantic relationships — with AI assistants? ​Society: How do top researchers think these trends will shape our future? ​What you’ll get: ​A fast-paced, beginner-friendly overview of today’s most powerful AI systems ​Live demos that reveal both the breakthroughs and the risks ​An interactive forecasting exercise where you’ll test your own predictions about AI’s impact ​Come ready to explore a future arriving faster than most people realize — one that could transform not just how we work, but how we trust, connect, and even love.

events.title: Diving deeper into software development discussions
events.description: • What we’ll do Come join in discussions about Vim, Emacs, Linux, Elixir, Erlang, OTP, Lisp, Haskell, Ruby, Javascript, Functional Programming, Object Oriented Programming, Blockchain, HashGraph, Distributed Systems, Decentralized Systems, and whatever peaks your interest. • What to bring A laptop, good questions, exciting ideas, an open mind and a positive attitude. • Where to meet Meet by the corn hole game in the back and look for others with laptops. Ask for others that are there for the software discussion group. • Alternate location Some nights the Rabbit Hole has live music. I was not able to pin down an exact schedule, just sometimes we would show up and there would be a band but most times there was not. As an alternate location on live music nights the discussion group could be moved just down the street to Lestats coffee shop. They have great coffee and a nice atmosphere for hanging out and discussing software. • Important to know This is a self organizing open discussion group that is meant to be organic, with no main speaker or leader. It is just a time and a place for like minded people to come together to talk about software. • Background When I was staying in SD (Jan-Aug 2018) I was teaching software development at UCSD and the Meetup gave my cohort a space to dive further into topics that the curriculum did not allow time for. When the class was over and I went back to traveling full time, I thought it would be an interesting experiment to leave the Meetup going and see if people would still gather to discuss software without someone to lead it. I liked the idea of a group that did not require some one to always lead it. A lot off Meetups I have been to before were always one or two people doing a lot of work to organize and put on talks but everyone else just shows up to consume and doesn’t really participate that much. Kinda like going to a movie, in a way. The idea for this group is that it is what people make it. You get out of it what you put into it. It’s great that people are showing up still and I hope you have some great discussions. When I get back to San Diego (planning on a handful of times a year) and there is a meeting I will stop by and hang out and invite my cohort of previous students.

events.title: A Space for All of Us
events.description: Sensory-Friendly Quiet Mornings at the Museum The Museum’s sensory-friendly quiet hours are held every Sunday from 10 a.m. to 11 a.m. This time is dedicated to creating a calmer, quieter museum experience. We remain open to the public during this time, and all are welcome to visit the Museum. During this time, specific exhibit features in the main Museum building will be turned off to minimize audio and visual stimulation. Noise-blocking headphones are free for check-out at the main Admissions desk and the entrance to the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibition, Exhibit features in the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibit will remain on, and all exhibits will be fully turned on at 11 a.m. Admission purchased during sensory-friendly quiet hours is the same as standard admission and applies to the entire day. Please contact publicprograms@museumofus.org with any questions.

events.title: House of Denmark
events.description: The annual lawn program is held on the first Sunday in June each year. As the 4th of June is “Grundlovsdag” the day the Danish Constitution was signed and transferring the oldest Kingdom in the World from a Monarchy rule of law to a Parlamentary rule of law. The Danish Royal family is still extremely popular among the common Danes and has so far not experienced scandals like other European Royal families. Queen Margrethe is highly educated and is a great artist (designer and painter) and the King to be Crown Prince Frederik is also highly educated and a Danish “Navy Seal” and married to sweet Mary from Australia. Entertainment for this event is done by a storyteller, who is a member of the cottage who talks about fun and historical topics. A three-man band plays Danish-English favorites with singing. A dance group in their native costumes adds to the color and variety.

events.title: SHAWN HARRIS
events.description:
Standing
6'5, Shawn Harris's stage presence and larger than life comedic person 
grabs you from the moment he walks on stage. Shawn began his comedy 
career in 1994 by gracing comedy clubs throughout NY! In 1999, Shawn was
given the opportunity to perform on BET's Comic View and after 6 
performances on that network he's branched out to HBO, Comedy Central, 
NBC and others! Shawn's comedic prowess hasn't gone unnoticed by the 
entertainment industry and has garnered invitations to showcase for the 
Montreal Comedy Festival in 2005 and the Aspen Comedy Festival in 2007. 
Moreover, Shawn has had 7 different movies showcased in the Comedy Film 
Festivals nationwide and won "Best Comedic Performance in a Short Film" 
at the Boston Comedy Film Festival in 2009 for his role in "John The 
Angry Mover". His Guiding principle along the way is simple: "Be 
successful. Laugh Your Ass off. Comedy is Medicine!"

events.title: MAX AMINI
events.description:
Max Amini is an Iranian-American director, producer, and 
world-touring comedian. Born in Tuscon, AZ and raised on the east coast,
he graduated from UCLA’s School of Theater, Film and Television in 
2004. While still at school, Max began performing stand- up comedy at 
the LA Improv. His exceptional abilities in improvised and observational
comedy made him a standout performer and he quickly built a reputation 
as one of the fastest growing comedians in the Los Angeles comedy 
circuit. Impressed with Max’s popular performances and steady career 
growth, the world-famous Laugh Factory in Hollywood made him a resident 
comic in 2007.

Never setting aside his passion for film and 
television, Max continued to pursue directing and producing as his 
global comedy career progressed. In 2010, Max established his production
company and began creatively producing and directing television series,
comedy specials and films. More recently, Max directed a Netflix 
original stand-up special, Enissa Amani: Erhenwort, the film James The Second ,
and has sold to networks like Discovery, Voice of America and more. 
Amini has appeared on shows such as NBC’s Heroes, Netflix’s Real Rob 
with Rob Schneider, and Comedy Central’s Mind of Mencia, has garnered 
multiple hour-long comedy specials and continues to tour and perform 
around the world.

Ages 21+
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest.
Doors for open for seating approximately 30 minutes before late showtimes. Please note, doors are subject to prior show endtime and may change without notice, beyond our control.
No refunds or exchanges.

events.title: Mad House Open Mic! Free. Open to Anyone. Come watch stars be born and dreams die 5 min at a time :)
events.description: OPEN MIC INFO… Is it free? Yes. It’s free and there are no food or drink minimums ever for open mic shows at Mad House Comedy Club. Is it free for a reason? Yes. It’s an open mic, so it’s literally “open” to anyone in the world that might want to go on stage including people who are quite insane. Is it a fun show? It depends on your pain tolerance. There are lots of comedians who are very funny practicing new material. There are new comedians who are terrible but they’re new, so they’re supposed to be awful and it’s kind of cute to watch them slowly die onstage. There are people who will make you cringe and decide it’s a great time to use the restroom and then there are comics who are so incredibly bad that it’s actually hilarious. The open mic is my favorite show. If you’re the type of person who doesn’t want to know how the sausage is made, then it’s probably not for you. If you’re the type of person who’d like to know the shocking truth of how stand up comedy really starts… this is your new home. Bonus… you get to hang out in an amazing bar with funny people. We have an open mic 7 nights a week.

This new chapter is more than just a gathering. It is a chance to expand our movement of empowerment, inspiration, and support into the heart of San Diego. Whether you cannot always make it to Feel Good Fridays in Encinitas or you are ready to double down on connection by joining both, Tuesday Team Up was created for you. It is a place where authentic conversations spark new opportunities, where encouragement turns into action, and where people come together to lift each other higher both personally and professionally. Tuesday Team Up is another step toward that vision and we would love for you to be part of it.

events.description: June An is the visionary founder of The Group, a community born from the triumphs and trials of his own entrepreneurial journey. As a seasoned entrepreneur, June understands the personal and professional challenges that can often feel overwhelming. His journey has taken him through moments of helplessness and hopelessness, which fueled his passion to create a space where others don’t have to navigate these challenges alone. Inspired by his experiences, June founded The Group with a clear mission: to empower individuals to overcome obstacles and pursue their dreams with renewed hope and determination. The Group is more than just a community; it’s a place where dreams are nurtured, resilience is strengthened, and people come together to achieve their full potential—both personally and professionally. Let The Group inspire you to live your dreams with purpose and confidence. https://www.thegroupcoastal.com/

events.title: FilmNet’s Monthly Event
events.description: Happy New Year, everyone! Let’s march into 2026 with renewed energy and focus on our shared goals, to make this the year we really come together to make our dreams come true. This month the attention will be on the art of cinematography, on that special window into what our visions ought to look like on the screen. It is said that there are four directors: the screenwriter, the actual director, the cinematographer and the editor. Each brings their own skills and vision and each puts their own unique stamp on the final product. The January panel, moderated by producer/actor Anthony De La Cruz, includes Mike Logan of Spark Studios and Kraftwerks Productions, Jeff Arballo of Arballo Entertainment, Mauricio Navarro of Influence Films, and student filmmaker Daniel Hernandez. Panelists will discuss why they chose to be cinematographers as well as equipment they use and their current projects, followed by audience Q & A. We also have a SPECIAL ANNOUNCEMENT about a future FilmNet offering, so be sure to keep your antennae up and your ears unplugged! Back by popular demand . . . SUSHI and POKE BOWLS by celebrity Chef Mer! And as always, we’ll be enjoying a red carpet and photo stations c/o Tony Amat and our beloved partner R3 Productions, drinks for sale, a studio tour (get there at 4:30 pm!), more free headshots and lots of glitzy, campy, Hollywood-style fun. And of course, more exciting networking opportunities to give you the chance to make ALL the connections you really want to make. ********************************* Filmmakers Network San Diego’s Monthly Event is back!!! At long last we’re starting up monthly networking events again to support the local film community. It’s going to be even more hands-on, interactive and productive this time. Besides networking and hosting guest speakers, we will showcase short films and promo material, work on pitches and invite industry gatekeepers to hear them, put on fun games and contests as well as many more enriching activities. Networking includes supporting local producers, creators, actors, crew and film professionals in their effort to assemble teams and develop content and marketing opportunities, along with original FilmNet content and a documentary of our group, events, participants and their projects. Feel free to bring a guest or two! Below is the schedule for our first meeting: 4:30–6:30 / Studio tour, doors open for sign-up and seating 6:30–7:15 / Intros and success stories 7:15–8:15 / Cinematography panel, , Q & A 8:15–9:00 / Special announcement, networking Actors, feel free to bring headshots. Business cards or any promotional materials a plus. $5.00 admission at the event. Karl – 858-752-9985 (text)

events.title: CEDH
events.description: A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition.

events.title: Legacy
events.description: Legacy is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards No maximum deck size Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used Allows cards from all legal sets unless otherwise banned for power level reasons. With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, and Forest), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age blockâ€”Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forestâ€”are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block. Legacy decks may consist of cards from allÂ MagicÂ card sets, plus the following cards: Sewers of Estark, Windseeker Centaur, and Nalathni Dragon (Mana Crypt would also be included were it not currently bannedâ€”see below). Cards from expansions and special sets (likeÂ From the Vault,Â Magic: The Gatheringâ€”Commander,Â Duel Decks,Â Conspiracy, etc.) are legal in the Legacy format on the date of release of the expansion or special set.

events.title: Mad House Open Mic! Free. Open to Anyone. Come watch stars be born and dreams die 5 min at a time :)
events.description: OPEN MIC INFO… Is it free? Yes. It’s free and there are no food or drink minimums ever for open mic shows at Mad House Comedy Club. Is it free for a reason? Yes. It’s an open mic, so it’s literally “open” to anyone in the world that might want to go on stage including people who are quite insane. Is it a fun show? It depends on your pain tolerance. There are lots of comedians who are very funny practicing new material. There are new comedians who are terrible but they’re new, so they’re supposed to be awful and it’s kind of cute to watch them slowly die onstage. There are people who will make you cringe and decide it’s a great time to use the restroom and then there are comics who are so incredibly bad that it’s actually hilarious. The open mic is my favorite show. If you’re the type of person who doesn’t want to know how the sausage is made, then it’s probably not for you. If you’re the type of person who’d like to know the shocking truth of how stand up comedy really starts… this is your new home. Bonus… you get to hang out in an amazing bar with funny people. We have an open mic 7 nights a week.

events.title: VibeCraft Power Hour
events.description: VibeCraft is a recurring, collaborative working session where ideas turn into experiments and experiments turn into real builds. Each session is shaped by the group. We review what is emerging, explore new directions, and decide together what is worth building next. This is not a presentation or a passive meetup. It is an active, hands-on environment where members contribute ideas, react to each other’s work, and influence the direction of future sessions. Expect open discussion, live demos, shared whiteboards, and practical exploration. Sessions may include live walkthroughs of tools, automations, and AI agents, discussions around product and company design, rapid demos from members, and breakdowns of different approaches to building with modern AI systems. Topics evolve over time, but the focus remains the same: learning by doing, building in public, and exploring a high-tech, high-touch future grounded in curiosity, collaboration, and thoughtful design. Come ready to participate, share what you are building, and help shape what we explore next as a VibeCraft community.

events.title: 1 Million Cups
events.description: 1 Million Cups is a weekly event for entrepreneurs, by entrepreneurs — built on the belief that conversations over coffee can accelerate innovation. Each week, two founders take the stage to pitch their business for six minutes, followed by 20 minutes of honest, constructive feedback from a room full of fellow entrepreneurs, business professionals, students, and curious minds. This isn’t a pitch competition — it’s a collaborative environment where ideas are refined, challenges are unpacked, and next steps become clearer. Attendees are encouraged to ask thoughtful questions, offer insights, and share resources that help presenters grow. Whether you’re launching your first startup, scaling your next venture, or just looking to get plugged into your local entrepreneurial ecosystem, 1 Million Cups is where you’ll find the energy, perspective, and community you need to move forward. Come for the coffee. Stay for the conversation. Leave with a better business.

events.title: Coworking with some San Diego Digital Designers
events.description: Sometimes you just want to be surrounded by percolating design minds. At this gathering, bring your laptop and work on whatever’s on your plate that day — now surrounded by like minds. This is your chance to see Figma on the next screen over. To chat through a perplexing interaction design. To focus, inspired, on your portfolio. As with all SDDD events, this is a casual, personal, and quiet-ish meeting of designers. Introvert- and extrovert-friendly. Coworking, collaboration, and conversation, not networking. We’ve got wifi and power (duh), I’ll brew up a fresh batch of coffee, and we’ve got some tasty teas. What else do you need? (No, srsly, what else do you need?) Priority will be given to SDDD members (joining is easy — assuming you are a digital designer in San Diego — and free at sddd.org). Hit up the chat here in Meetup — or #events in Slack — with any questions. Limited capacity, so please RSVP accurately! 🙂 See you there!

Come and go as you please, but do remember to bring your laptop (charged as plugs are few). It’s free to join, but ideally grab some coffee or lunch & tip the servers. A note about RSVPs: This group aims to help people meet in a small group. The max amount of people for this event is 12 people. We close the RSVP list on Mondays if there is a waitlist, and if you can’t make it we do kindly ask that you change your status as soon as you know to give someone else a chance to attend. Those on the waitlist will receive a note on the day of the meetup letting them know if there is space or not. If you sign up, but do not attend, you will be marked as a No Show. events.title: Carlsbad Coworking
events.description: We are all writers. Whether you’re writing copy for your next launch, tackling those client emails, drafting your newsletter, or finally updating your resume – bring your laptop and get it done. We meet every other week, 2:30-4:30pm at Wildland in Carlsbad. Structure: – 30 min: Intros & set your intention (what are you working on today?) – 60 min: Focused work time (no distractions, everyone’s heads down) – 30 min: Share progress, get feedback, ask questions This is for anyone who writes as part of their work: founders, marketers, consultants, creatives, job searchers. If you’re tired of procrastinating alone, come procrastinate with us (and then actually get stuff done). Details: This event is FREE. Please support Wildland by grabbing a coffee, tea, or treat while you’re there. RSVP to secure your spot. Refund policy Paid tickets are non-refundable and all sales are final.

events.title: Member Night at the Museum
events.description: Wednesday, June 10 4:00–7:00 p.m. Members get special late-night access to two special exhibitions! Join us for Member Night at the Museum, a new after-hours event series just for members. Come for all or part of the event and enjoy a special evening look at two of the Museum’s special Centennial exhibitions as we celebrate 100 years of art and community with our members! Enjoy light refreshments while mingling with fellow members and Museum staff, plus spin our prize wheel for possible membership extensions or partner guest pass giveaways. Shop spotlight gifts from local artisans and exhibition-related gifts at The Museum Store. During Member Night, members receive an additional 10% discount (total discount of 20%) and every $10+ purchase in the Store enters members in for an opportunity drawing! One Night, Two Exhibitions Forging a Legacy: 15 Years of Landmark Acquisitions, our newest centennial exhibition, includes more than 150 standout works from the many thousands of important works of art added to our collection in recent history. The astounding quality of the additions to the collection are testament not only to the Museum’s directorial and curatorial vision, but also to the many generous individuals who have gifted art to share with this city and its diverse communities. It is a collection created by and for the people of San Diego and is shared internationally through publications, loans to other museums, and with touring exhibitions worldwide. Enjoy after-hours access and a guided docent tour of the exhibition. Cafés and Cabarets: the Spectacular Art of Toulouse-Lautrec showcases the revolutionary work of French artist Henri de Toulouse-Lautrec. Working in the popular and modern art form of the color lithograph, Toulouse-Lautrec reached unprecedented audiences with spellbinding visuals and eye-catching colors. His short but celebrated career immortalized the cabaret culture of Belle Époque Paris and revolutionized the art of the poster. In 1987, The San Diego Museum of Art received the spectacular gift of over one hundred paintings, drawings, and prints by Toulouse-Lautrec from the Baldwin Foundation. In celebration of this gift and the Museum’s centennial, this exhibition shares approximately fifty works from the permanent collection. Enjoy after-hours access and a guided docent tour of the exhibition. No RSVP required; use your membership card to check in at the SDMA admission desk. Not yet a member? Join today.

events.title: MAX AMINI
events.description:
Max Amini is an Iranian-American director, producer, and 
world-touring comedian. Born in Tuscon, AZ and raised on the east coast,
he graduated from UCLA’s School of Theater, Film and Television in 
2004. While still at school, Max began performing stand- up comedy at 
the LA Improv. His exceptional abilities in improvised and observational
comedy made him a standout performer and he quickly built a reputation 
as one of the fastest growing comedians in the Los Angeles comedy 
circuit. Impressed with Max’s popular performances and steady career 
growth, the world-famous Laugh Factory in Hollywood made him a resident 
comic in 2007.

Never setting aside his passion for film and 
television, Max continued to pursue directing and producing as his 
global comedy career progressed. In 2010, Max established his production
company and began creatively producing and directing television series,
comedy specials and films. More recently, Max directed a Netflix 
original stand-up special, Enissa Amani: Erhenwort, the film James The Second ,
and has sold to networks like Discovery, Voice of America and more. 
Amini has appeared on shows such as NBC’s Heroes, Netflix’s Real Rob 
with Rob Schneider, and Comedy Central’s Mind of Mencia, has garnered 
multiple hour-long comedy specials and continues to tour and perform 
around the world.

Ages 21+

Doors for open for seating approximately 30 minutes before late showtimes. Please note, doors are subject to prior show endtime and may change without notice, beyond our control.
No refunds or exchanges.

events.title: Pauper
events.description: In this Magic format, all cards must have been printed at common rarity in a Magic set or product. Common promo cards are only legal if the card meets that qualification. If a common version of a particular card was ever released in Magic, any version of that card is legal in this format. Other than that, the usual rules for constructed decks apply: Minimum of 60 cards in the main deck (There is no maximum size for main decks) Up to 15 cards in your sideboard, if used No more than 4 of any individual card in the main deck and sideboard combined (with the exception of basic lands)

events.title: FABRIZIO COPANO
events.description:
Latin America’s top comic Fabrizio Copano brings his unique brand of global stand-up comedy to the stage.

The
first South American comic to film a Netflix special, Copano is a 
global touring act selling out across the U.S., Europe, and Latin 
America. Known for his hit TV shows (El Club de la Comedia, El Late de 
Copano) and as co-writer/star of the Chilean box office smash Barrio 
Universitario, his sharp, cross-cultural material has earned him spots 
on The Late Late Show with James Corden, The Talk, and Just for Laughs.

In
2024, Amazon Prime released his Spanish special Live From Caupolicán to
rave reviews. His debut English-language special From the Future is out
now via 800 Pound Gorilla — and was just selected by Billboard as one 
of the best comedy specials of the month.

Sunbum is a dedicated champion of the San Diego community and is providing product to keep us all protected while we connect outside of the lab. ​About the Host:​ ​Nucleate is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to empowering the next generation of biotech leaders. events.title: Discover what type of founder you are
events.description: Today founders are expected to master everything. We start from a different place. This is a small-group workshop on Founder Fit — understanding your entrepreneurial nature so you can build from strength, not exhaustion. What we’ll cover: → Where you’re naturally strong — and how to build on it → Where you systematically struggle — and what to do about it → Which tasks give you energy and which drain it → Who you need around you to cover your blind spots We’ll work through real founder situations: co-founder conflicts, delegation failures, different thinking styles. And we’ll show how Neuroiam — an AI platform that maps human nature to practical decisions — helps founders understand themselves, their team, and their best growth path. Stop fixing yourself. Start building around yourself. Hosted by Perseverance × Neuroiam Speakers: Antony Art & Lena SM

Come enjoy every month this nice lay-back Rendez-Vous with the French BioBeach community to meet new biotech professionals, network with the usuals, and just unwind with a beer or cocktail before the weekend starts….. events.title: CEDH
events.description: A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition.

events.title: Modern
events.description: Modern is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards Cards from Core Set Eighth Edition and Mirrodin through today No maximum deck size, as long as you can shuffle your deck unassisted Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and Wastes), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age block Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forest are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block.

events.title: MATT RICHARDS
events.description:
MATT RICHARDS is a New York based actor, host, comedian, and writer. You can catch Matt in the Comedy Central Studios feature film  Run For The Money opposite Beck Bennett and Joel McHale on Paramount+, and as the host of A&E’s popular food travel series  Best in Chow . His TV credits also include Flatbush Misdemeanors (Showtime),  That Damn Michael Che (HBOMax), Run The World (Starz), 2 Broke Girls  (CBS), School of Rock (Nickelodeon), and Bizaardvark (Disney). Additionally, Matt has appeared as himself on  The Funny Dance Show (E!), World Star TV (MTV), and The Steve Harvey Show (NBC). Matt was selected as a NEW FACE Character 2018 for the prestigious Just For Laughs Festival in Montreal and was a standout in the 2017 CBS diversity Showcase. He has written for the WGA award nominated series That Damn Michael Che , and has been a host producer for Snoop Dogg on various programming for VH1, TBS, Peacock and Animal Planet. See Matt live at the Comedy Cellar in New York or headlining a comedy club near you!

events.title: San Diego’s Underwater Forests with Oriana Poindexter
events.description: Discover the fascinating world of seaweed forests on a visual adventure with artist and marine scientist, Oriana Poindexter. Seaweed forests are some of the most incredible habitats on Earth, and yet these powerful sanctuaries are often overlooked and understudied. That’s where Poindexter comes in. Blending experimental photography techniques with her background in marine sciences, Oriana Poindexter documents how kelp forest ecosystems shift. Embark on a visual adventure with Poindexter during her talk—through a collection of specimens, photographs, and striking art displays, she’ll compel you to reconsider how you think about kelp. Poindexter’s work has been published by Smithsonian Magazine, The New York Times, and The Wall Street Journal, and exhibited in museums and aquariums throughout California. __________ Nat Talks, made possible with support from presenting sponsor The Downing Family Foundation and media partner KPBS, feature museum staff and outside experts speaking on an array of topics, including the latest in scientific research, history, conservation, and the natural world.

events.description: Why go it alone? It only takes one person to change our lives personally or professionally. Who will you meet today at “EE“? See additional info and direct links below… Exceptional Entrepreneurs is one of the USA’s largest business networking organizations – and one of the best values in business networking. At EE: There is no membership required. There is no attendance requirement. There is no business profession or category exclusivity. ALL business professions are welcome to attend! Meeting Agenda: First 30 minutes: check-in & breakout rooms open. Remaining 60 minutes: structured agenda with everyone in main room, including introductions by participants + more! While meetings end on time (for those who need to go), we also open breakout rooms again for those wanting to meet with other attendees afterwards (at least 30 minutes, usually longer.. fyi). Interested in being the featured speaker for this event? Information and registration is at: http://EEspeaker.com Info to join this EE business networking meeting: ####################################### http://nroi.us/eeonline (the shortlink above will remain updated with the current Zoom link) Passcode: 7772021 ####################################### Limited to the first 100 participants. We also cross-livestream through the EE FB Group. No participant limit on FB, however it’s view-only mode. Please feel free to leave your contact info in the comments of the livestream when listening along: https://www.facebook.com/groups/networkingeesd The meetings (including chat) will be recorded to the cloud – and made available temporarily afterwards. Information on recordings will be provided to attendees on Zoom and Facebook. Exceptional Entrepreneurs was founded November, 2008 in San Diego, California USA by David & Gina Demangos. Since then, “EE” has become one of the USA’s largest business networking organizations, due to the Demangos’ vision and leadership to provide “exceptional” opportunities for connecting local professionals. David Demangos is an eXp real estate agent in San Diego. For more information on David, please see: Awesome San Diego Real Estate: http://www.awesomesandiegorealestate.com “Effective networking is a learned skill. Exceptional Entrepreneurs are highly trusted professionals who share ideas and know that in order to receive you must first be willing to give.” – David Demangos Thank you for your continuing support of “EE”! See you at the next event.

events.title: Nat at Night
events.description: On Fridays, the San Diego Natural History Museum is open until 11 PM just for you. Enjoy access to all open exhibitions and our rooftop deck for good views and great vibes. The rooftop is open to all visitors and serves beer, wine, non-alcoholic drinks, and food from Wolf in the Woods. Take a selfie with a megalodon shark, see scientists at work in our Paleo Center, admire glittering gems, get a close up look at spiders and snakes, and so much more. Best of all, get a look at our brand-new exhibition, Washed Ashore: Art to Save the Sea and grab a drink in the dive bar while chatting with a scientist. On select dates, channel your inner artist with Sketchparty, get competitive during trivia, or unwind while listening to the rooftop DJ. Hungry for more? The Craft Taco, located inside the Atrium, is a vibrant taco restaurant offering a delectable fusion of flavors and artisanal creations. They offer made-to-order tacos featuring signature blue corn tortillas, delectable burritos and bowls, and a variety of drinks and coffees. Admission is half-price after 5 PM, and parking is free after 6 PM. You can buy your tickets online in advance or buy them at the door the night of. As always, members of The Nat and Explorer pass holders enjoy free general admission. Fill your summer with wonder at The Nat. With weekly Nat at Night, brunches, weekend family programming, and so much more, membership just makes sense. Not to mention, you’re directly supporting nature conservation in our region. Join us today.

events.title: Business Bonfire Potluck
events.description: Sunset, a fire pit, and the kind of San Diego night you’ll be glad you didn’t skip. Builders & Backers and Jennifer Slaughter’s Women’s Walk community are getting together for a beach bonfire potluck — and if past events are any signal, this one fills up fast. SDcatering.co is handling charcuterie. Everyone else brings something from the shared signup so we don’t end up with twelve bags of chips and no plates. Claim what you’re bringing here: BuildAndBack.com/bonfire A few of you have asked how to get more involved with these events — this is a great one for it. Beach bonfires take a village. We need firewood, and a few people who can come early to help secure a fire pit and get setup going. If that’s you, mark it on the signup so we can coordinate. Bring what you can, come when you can, stay as long as you’d like. Need copy that converts or events that build real community? Reach Jennifer Slaughter here, or at cntntcaptain@gmail.com. Founder and artist Mikie Krisztal will be creating live, interactive artwork as part of the evening’s experience. Learn more at www.theschoolofimperfection.com

events.title: DERAY DAVIS
events.description:
Born and raised on the south side of Chicago, comedian-turned-actor DeRay Davis can mostecently be "scene" and heard in the blockbuster mega hit 21 Jump Street, Jumping the Broom and his wildly successful Showtime 1 hour Comedy Special, DeRay Davis: Power Play. He lends his voice and writing talent to countless characters on Kanye Wests Albums the BoonDocks and currently on Adult Swims Black Dynamite!

DeRay, developed and honed a hip, sardonic, streetwise mentality at an early age and parlayed it smoothly and efficiently into the comedy-club circuit. Davis achieved his career breakthrough at the Laffapalooza Festival in Atlanta, GA, then scored a triple whammy by winning the Comedy Central Laugh Riots Competition and landing covetable spots in the Montreal Just for Laughs Festival and the Cedric the Entertainer Festival. Throughout, the comic wove vulgar and droll, yet also telling and deeply personal, routines around the subjects of race, poverty, and his challenging experiences growing up in the Windy City projects with a dysfunctional African-American family. 

The transition from behind-the-mic spots to movie roles represented a relatively short jump, for most of Davis' early film assignments emphasized the same subject matter as his routines; for example, he played the "Hustle Guy" in Barbershop (2002) and its sequel, Barbershop 2: Back in Business (2004), rapper Mario "Fa Real" Greene in the Martin Short comedy Jiminy Glick in La La Wood (2004), and a Jamaican stoner in Johnson Family Vacation (2004). Davis expanded his genre focus with roles in Rupert Wainwright's supernatural horror outing The Fog (2005), and Todd Phillips' comedy remake School for Scoundrels (2006). Subsequent feature assignments include License to Wed (2007), Semi-Pro (2008, as basketball player Bee Bee Ellis), and Imagine That (2009). Life As We Know it and Old Dogs. Davis also appeared on television series including Entourage and Reno 911!, and televised comedy revues such as Comedy Central's Premium Blend. Deray can be seen on VH1’s new show “Hip Hop Squares” the hip-hop adaptation of the classic “Hollywood Squares” game show, which will be premiering on VH1 on March 13th.

No refunds or exchanges.
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest. 
Ages 21+

events.title: Modern
events.description: Modern is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards Cards from Core Set Eighth Edition and Mirrodin through today No maximum deck size, as long as you can shuffle your deck unassisted Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and Wastes), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age block Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forest are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block.

events.title: DAUOOD NAIMYAR
events.description:
Dauood Naimyar taped a Comedy Central stand-up set last February and was also a New Face at Just For Laughs 2022. Dauood’s comedic perspective is shaped by the duality of his constant need to fit in, and his relentless desire to be different. His search for being relatable, yet controversial has brought audiences joy for nine years- and will probably continue until he finally figures out who he is. We hope he doesn’t, ‘cause bills need to be paid. Dauood also has over 150K followers on TikTok and keeps building a large audience.

events.title: Tai Chi Ch’uan Lee Style
events.description: A class session consists of gentle limbering exercises that aid in coordination, balance, and the opening of various channels within the body to allow your Chi to flow freely. In addition, we incorporate respiration therapy such as our style’s form of Qigong, K’ai Men, otherwise known as Taoist Yoga and various specialized breathing exercises referred to as ‘Dao Yin’. Moreover, we include partner exercises that reinforce the underlying principles of being tenderly gentle, soft, and giving way to all force, provided that our style requires absolutely no physical strength. Furthermore, Taoist physical exercises include parts of the art of Tai Chi Ch’uan: The form of Tai Chi Ch’uan and Tai Chi Dance. The physical movements of Tai Chi Ch’uan are gentle, natural and an exceptionally light therapeutic exercise.

events.title: Online: Saturday Study Group
events.description: We are continuing our Study group online for the time being. We are now meeting online in the San Diego Python Discord server! Here is the link to join: https://discord.gg/8GpWuVvdbQ There is a “meetup” voice/video channel that we will be using! We may start meeting in person soon! This will be discussed at the meetup. We are considering having some online, some in-person. Decisions, decisions! Every Saturday we meet up with the larger Python community (including San Diego PyLadies (https://www.meetup.com/sd-pyladies), Open BioInformatics San Diego (https://www.meetup.com/Open-Bioinformatics-SanDiego/) and San Diego Artificial Intelligence (https://www.meetup.com/SanDiegoAI/)) to chat, learn, and hang out. Our study group is for everyone on their Python journey, whether you’re just starting out, an experienced pro, or anywhere in between. There’s no formal instruction, just a bunch of folks who like to code. Sometimes we talk about a specific subject, sometimes answer questions, whatever! Python Community Code of Conduct (http://www.pythonsd.org/coc): Python is much more than a software language. Python is a vibrant community made up of members from around the globe with a diverse set of skills, personalities, and experiences. It is through these differences that our community experiences great successes and continued growth. Overall, we’re good to each other. We contribute to this community not because we have to, but because we want to.

events.title: 3D Printing Show n’ Tell – Online!
events.description: In between the regular monthly meetups we can get together online and share our webcam or desktop or whatever. We’re using Discord which allows for desktop screen sharing and a webcam all at the same time, up to 25 people! Join our Discord with the link below. http://discord.sandiego3dprinting.com Webcam and screen sharing are totally optional, but I strongly encourage a mic and headphones. Discord does a decent job at killing feedback, but it’s not perfect without them. Mobile phones will work too, but the experience is the best with the Desktop app. See ya in a week or 2! =D Brictone

events.title: REMOTE: Pyladies/San Diego PUG Co-Study Group
events.description: We are continuing our Study group online for the time being. We are now meeting online in the San Diego Python Discord server! The SD Python User Group Meetup is https://www.meetup.com/pythonsd/ – there is a Saturday Study group hosted by the SD Python Meetup group. Every Saturday we meet up with the larger Python community to chat, learn, and hang out. Our study group is for everyone on their Python journey, whether you’re just starting out, you’re an experienced pro, or anywhere in between. There’s no formal instruction, just a bunch of folks who like to code. We have a great time–come join us! Free feel to show up at any time and stay as long as you want. Hope to see you there! events.title: Hack and Race-Prep Autonomous Cars
events.description: San Diego Robotics DIYRobocars Race-Prep Day, Hosted by UCSD. Please keep in mind that at the Saturdays meeting we expect you to have your robocar built and software installed. I hope you will understand that for us time at the track is limited to ever other week for 2 hours. We would like to use it for development as much as feasible. Please complete your robot build before coming to the Saturdays meetup. See the 2nd Wednesday of month Beginner/Builder Meetup and our slack channel. Then bring your bot and computer to the track. Please sign-up and come for our monthly Beginners and Builders Meetup before coming to the Saturdays Meetup. Agenda: 10 :00 am – 1200 pm. Training and racing We have built a double lane track with a yellow dashed center-line. Expect a track roughly 30×15 feet, with a concrete floor and duct tape lines. Don’t worry if you are still working things out with the software. We will be on hand to help all get going. This is a great chance to gather training data for you robot. Still figuring out hardware and/or configuring the software on the bot and laptop? Go to one of the 2nd Wed of month Beginner/Builder Meetups. There will be tables, power, and wifi. Please bring your laptops and bot and be ready to hack. A special thanks to UCSD for hosting us. Thank you. SD DIYRobocars. *** Checkout http://www.donkeycar.com for the build information that many of us used to get started. Checkout http://docs.donkeycar.com for parts lists, hardware and software setup instructions. Self-steering car from SD group: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nxhsv5CViBg more sample driving from San Diego meetup: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WecNKymdg3k some sample driving from Oakland DIYRobocars meetup: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sQn5PA6nl3I

events.description: SDCAMA art shows display the living art of today; you can hold it in your hands and take it into your life. Many of our artist members are full time professionals that have been plying their trade for decades, have won competitions throughout the U.S., and are world renowned. Other members only have time to create when they have a spare moment in their busy lives. We all have one thing in common; our artists are visionaries, driven to awaken within a world seldom experienced, through the expertise of their gentile hands.

events.title: San Diego Potters’ Guild Spring Patio Show and Sale
events.description: The San Diego Potters’ Guild is a working ceramics studio and gallery at Studio 29 in Spanish Village Arts Center in the heart of Balboa Park. Find us just off Park Boulevard on Village Place, north of the Natural History Museum and south of the Zoo. Our gallery/studio is open daily from 11 am to 4 pm. Themed exhibits rotate in our central gallery throughout the year, and during our two annual patio shows on the second full weekends in June and November, we fill the Spanish Village courtyard with our latest work.

Family Art Adventures are dynamic free guided tours specially designed for families with school-aged children ages 5 to 12. events.title: Cat Gunn: if only by the light of a new moon
events.description: Cat Gunn’s first solo museum exhibition, if only by the light of a new moon, explores the complex terrain of inherited memory and the seemingly impossible nostalgia for a home that one has never known through materials including fabric, ceramics, and cyanotypes. This experience, familiar to immigrants, first-generation children, and anyone displaced by colonialism, war, or occupation, forms the emotional core of Gunn’s deeply personal investigation into family history. Gunn is ICA San Diego’s 2025 Artist-in-Residence.

events.title: Pokémon
events.description: The Pokémon Trading Card Game is a tabletop game that involves collecting, trading and playing with Pokémon themed playing cards. It has its own set of rules but uses many motifs and ideas derived from the Pokémon video games. There are Pokémon cards for every species & type of Pokémon as well as Trainer cards featuring characters, items and other themes of the franchise (each with a different use) and Energy cards to power various actions.

events.title: CEDH – Bronze Event
events.description: TopDeck Championship Series – Bronze Event A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition. *Event May be downgraded to based on attendance*

events.title: Codathon
events.description: Always wanted a longer stretch of time to focus on some project/ studies ? Take this time out and join in.

events.title: LADY BUSHRA: PROFESSIONAL VICTIM
events.description:
Lady Bushra brings her new live show Professional Victim to Mic Drop Comedy San Diego for one day only! A sharp, fast-moving hour of stand-up from one of the UK’s most distinctive
emerging comedy voices.

Originally from Bradford, England and now based
in the U.S., Lady Bushra is the drag persona of comedian Amir Dean, 
blending British observational comedy with political satire, immigrant 
storytelling and brutally honest cultural commentary.
As seen on BBC, Channel 4, Revry TV and shortlisted for the BBC New 
Comedy Award, Bushra has quickly built an international audience online 
and on stage through a style that feels closer to modern stand-up than 
traditional drag performance.

The material being developed in this 
performance forms part of Bushra’s upcoming comedy special; a theatrical
stand-up project exploring identity, migration, religion, family and 
modern culture through the perspective of a British South
Asian comic trying to make sense of America.

Expect a tightly written hour covering 
everything from growing up in a conservative Muslim household in 
Northern England to navigating queer life in New York and Los Angeles, 
all delivered with dry British timing, audience interaction
and an instinct for finding the absurd in both progressive and 
conservative culture alike.

No lip-syncs. No death drops. Just stand-up 
comedy from a performer rapidly becoming one of the most original voices
working between the UK and U.S. comedy scenes.

Ages 21+

events.title: Summer Nights: TTRPG Edition
events.description: This summer, Comic-Con Museum rolls for initiative! Kick off summer with our local tabletop role-playing game community at Summer Nights 2026: TTRPG Edition. Join us for an ongoing campaign happening across three evenings of adventure, laughter, and community. Only able to make one or two of the evenings? New to tabletop role-playing games? No problem! This campaign will feature on-ramps for new and experienced players, whether you’re there for one night or all three! Food and beverages will be available for purchase from local vendors. All ages welcome; recommended for players aged 10 and up. Museum members enjoy complimentary admission. Not a member yet? Become a member starting at $75 and enjoy Summer Nights plus unlimited access to exhibits and other programming all year long. Single evening tickets are $15.00/person (includes one food item and soft drink). Series tickets are $40.00/person (includes one food item and soft drink per event). Roll with us!

events.title: C You Saturday! – June 2026
events.description: Please join us from 4 – 7pm on Saturday, June 13 for the opening reception of Cat Gunn: if only by the light of a new moon at ICA San Diego / Central. This free art event in San Diego is part of ICA’s commitment to reduce barriers to contemporary art in our community. 4:00 – 5:00 pm | Opening Reception for Cat Gunn: if only by the light of a new moon Cat Gunn was the 2025 ICA Artist in Residence and the work on view in this exhibition was created during the year Gunn spent in at the ICA artist studio at Studio 6608. Using installation, ceramics,textiles and cyanotypes. if only by the light of a new moon, Gunn’s first solo museum exhibition, explores the complex terrain of inherited memory and the seemingly impossible nostalgia for a home that one has never known 5 PM | Poetry Reading by Alexis N. Garcia Visitors are invited to a poetry reading at 5pm by writer, printmaker, and educator Alexis N. Garcia. You can find their poems, archival scans, & linocut experiments in The Hopkins Review, Poets.org’s “Poem-A-Day” series, The Slowdown, beestung, Peach Mag, among others. Garcia’s first book TIME IS A SILLY BOY, is forthcoming in Spring 2027. 5:30 PM | Exhibition Walkthrough with Cat Gunn and Jordan Karney Chaim Please join ICA curator, Jordan Karney Chaim, PhD, and Cat Gunn for a walkthrough of the exhibition to discuss the work in the exhibition and their residency. Come celebrate with us! Come celebrate with us! C you there! ICA San Diego / Central 1439 El Prado, San Diego, CA 92101 June 13 2026 4:00 – 7:00 PM RSVP to attend C You Saturday! ICA members receive free drink tickets. Get notified of more free art events in San Diego: Join our email list! __________________________ If you are able, please consider donating to ICA to keep our exhibtions free for everyone.

source.name: Institute of Contemporary Art San Diego (ICA)

events.title: Modern
events.description: Modern is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards Cards from Core Set Eighth Edition and Mirrodin through today No maximum deck size, as long as you can shuffle your deck unassisted Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and Wastes), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age block Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forest are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block.

events.title: RITZ – Rendezvous In The Zoo
events.description: Join us for Rendezvous In The Zoo (R·I·T·Z) as we continue to grow our renowned learning programs that inspire explorers to uncover the natural world around them. Each year, more than 30,000 students discover the San Diego Zoo and San Diego Zoo Safari Park and engage with our wide-ranging education programming. We also train educators from all 50 states and around the world, reaching an estimated 2 million students to date. These opportunities plant the seeds of wonder for learners of all ages—and with your support, these seeds will blossom into an enduring love for wildlife conservation. Experience Be a high achiever as you prepare for an evening of fun to support San Diego Zoo Wildlife Alliance’s education programs. Enjoy a brilliant reception with cuisine stations, gold-star entertainment, and clever cocktails. Find yourself face-to-face with the Zoo’s Rady Ambassadors wildlife. Bid on A+ auction items. Earn extra credit as you dance to live music. Discover incredible opportunities to support our renowned learning programs. Be yearbook-ready in our photo booth. Thanks for making the smart choice and joining us at R·I·T·Z!

events.title: DERAY DAVIS
events.description:
Born and raised on the south side of Chicago, comedian-turned-actor DeRay Davis can mostecently be "scene" and heard in the blockbuster mega hit 21 Jump Street, Jumping the Broom and his wildly successful Showtime 1 hour Comedy Special, DeRay Davis: Power Play. He lends his voice and writing talent to countless characters on Kanye Wests Albums the BoonDocks and currently on Adult Swims Black Dynamite!

DeRay, developed and honed a hip, sardonic, streetwise mentality at an early age and parlayed it smoothly and efficiently into the comedy-club circuit. Davis achieved his career breakthrough at the Laffapalooza Festival in Atlanta, GA, then scored a triple whammy by winning the Comedy Central Laugh Riots Competition and landing covetable spots in the Montreal Just for Laughs Festival and the Cedric the Entertainer Festival. Throughout, the comic wove vulgar and droll, yet also telling and deeply personal, routines around the subjects of race, poverty, and his challenging experiences growing up in the Windy City projects with a dysfunctional African-American family. 

The transition from behind-the-mic spots to movie roles represented a relatively short jump, for most of Davis' early film assignments emphasized the same subject matter as his routines; for example, he played the "Hustle Guy" in Barbershop (2002) and its sequel, Barbershop 2: Back in Business (2004), rapper Mario "Fa Real" Greene in the Martin Short comedy Jiminy Glick in La La Wood (2004), and a Jamaican stoner in Johnson Family Vacation (2004). Davis expanded his genre focus with roles in Rupert Wainwright's supernatural horror outing The Fog (2005), and Todd Phillips' comedy remake School for Scoundrels (2006). Subsequent feature assignments include License to Wed (2007), Semi-Pro (2008, as basketball player Bee Bee Ellis), and Imagine That (2009). Life As We Know it and Old Dogs. Davis also appeared on television series including Entourage and Reno 911!, and televised comedy revues such as Comedy Central's Premium Blend. Deray can be seen on VH1’s new show “Hip Hop Squares” the hip-hop adaptation of the classic “Hollywood Squares” game show, which will be premiering on VH1 on March 13th.

No refunds or exchanges.
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest. 
Ages 21+

events.title: DAUOOD NAIMYAR
events.description:
Dauood Naimyar taped a Comedy Central stand-up set last February and was also a New Face at Just For Laughs 2022. Dauood’s comedic perspective is shaped by the duality of his constant need to fit in, and his relentless desire to be different. His search for being relatable, yet controversial has brought audiences joy for nine years- and will probably continue until he finally figures out who he is. We hope he doesn’t, ‘cause bills need to be paid. Dauood also has over 150K followers on TikTok and keeps building a large audience.

Ages 21+

events.title: Measure for Measure
events.description: Justice and desire collide in Shakespeare’s timeless tale of power and hypocrisy. When Vienna’s Duke suddenly disappears, his morally rigid deputy Angelo takes control—determined to rid the city of vice. But when he condemns a young man to death and then propositions the man’s sister, his actions reveal the cracks in his facade of authority. Filled with rich characters, surprising twists, and a comic group of seedy grifters on the make, Measure for Measure explores who holds power, who gets punished, and what it truly means to be virtuous. Single tickets go on sale Friday, May 1, 2026 at noon. For early access, visit our subscription page for more information on the 2026 season! Measure for Measure is supported by Lead Production Sponsors Ann Davies and Darlene Marcos Shiley, in memory of Donald Shiley.

events.title: A Space for All of Us
events.description: Sensory-Friendly Quiet Mornings at the Museum The Museum’s sensory-friendly quiet hours are held every Sunday from 10 a.m. to 11 a.m. This time is dedicated to creating a calmer, quieter museum experience. We remain open to the public during this time, and all are welcome to visit the Museum. During this time, specific exhibit features in the main Museum building will be turned off to minimize audio and visual stimulation. Noise-blocking headphones are free for check-out at the main Admissions desk and the entrance to the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibition, Exhibit features in the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibit will remain on, and all exhibits will be fully turned on at 11 a.m. Admission purchased during sensory-friendly quiet hours is the same as standard admission and applies to the entire day. Please contact publicprograms@museumofus.org with any questions.

events.title: Hello Japan! Language & Culture
events.description: A perfect starting point for new learners who want both language skills and cultural understanding. Students will learn simple grammar, paired with cultural lessons about food, festivals, manners, and modern life in Japan. By the end of the course, students will feel confident using basic Japanese and more connected to the heart of Japanese culture. Theme of the month: April: Start of the School / Company year in Japan – Japanese New Year greetings, bowing etiquette (お辞儀), meaning of bow: respect and apology May: Kodomono hi (Children’s Day) – Family vocabulary, Basic sentence patterns June: Traditional Japanese comedic storytelling – Numbers Location: Education Center Time: 10:00am – 11:00am JFGM Member: $25 | Non-member $40 *Admission is included in the ticket. This class is non-refundable 7 days before the event.

events.title: Art Mart
events.description: SDCAMA art shows display the living art of today; you can hold it in your hands and take it into your life. Many of our artist members are full time professionals that have been plying their trade for decades, have won competitions throughout the U.S., and are world renowned. Other members only have time to create when they have a spare moment in their busy lives. We all have one thing in common; our artists are visionaries, driven to awaken within a world seldom experienced, through the expertise of their gentile hands.

events.title: San Diego Potters’ Guild Spring Patio Show and Sale
events.description: The San Diego Potters’ Guild is a working ceramics studio and gallery at Studio 29 in Spanish Village Arts Center in the heart of Balboa Park. Find us just off Park Boulevard on Village Place, north of the Natural History Museum and south of the Zoo. Our gallery/studio is open daily from 11 am to 4 pm. Themed exhibits rotate in our central gallery throughout the year, and during our two annual patio shows on the second full weekends in June and November, we fill the Spanish Village courtyard with our latest work.

events.title: Brunch at The Nat
events.description: Turn your museum visit into Sunday Funday with the addition of brunch at The Nat. Plan a Sunday visit that includes the full Nat experience, from bones to barnacles to brunch. After you’ve explored the galleries, head up to the roof to grab a bite and drink while soaking up the sunshine on our patio. When it comes to brunch, we mean business. That’s why we’re partnering with Johnny Rivera, local restaurateur known for starting the “Brunch Revolution” with Hash House a Go Go, Great Maple, and the viral BLT Bloody Mary. Brunch will be available 10 AM–2:30 PM on each date. Seating is open, casual, and first-come, first-served. Food and beverages may be purchased a la carte, similar to Nat at Night. The rooftop is popular, so there may be a wait at times. (Interested in reserved seating? Click here to view VIP experience options). Those looking for a different flavor of breakfast are in luck: head down to the Atrium for tacos, burritos, and bowls from The Craft Taco at The Nat. They also have the best coffee and matcha in Balboa Park, but we’re a little biased. Access to the rooftop brunch requires Museum admission. All purchases support our mission to conserve nature through science and discovery.

events.title: House of Philippines Lawn Program
events.description: The yearly lawn program is held on June 12th each year, or the date that is closest to that day. It is a National Holiday — the Independence Day. The Philippine Islands were liberated from Spain in 1898 on that day. The entertainment consists of dancing by a Philippine dance group, singing by choral groups and performances by community celebrities.

events.title: Let’s Learn Japanese Letters
events.description: A perfect starting point for new learners who want to learn Japanese letters. Students will learn three types of alphabets used in Japan: Hiragana, Katakana, and Kanji. Lesson Overview: Hiragana, Katakana, and Kanji The goal of this lesson is to help students learn to read Japanese characters through real-life examples such as tourist destinations, station names, and food. Students will also learn proper stroke order and pronunciation. As they practice reading, they will explore the meanings and origins of these names, along with cultural and historical background related to famous places in Japan. Location: Education Center Time: 11:15am – 12:15pm JFGM Member: $20 | Non-member $35 *Admission is included in the ticket. This class is non-refundable 7 days before the event.
