events.title: Nominations Due for the San Diego Business Impact Awards
events.description: We want to meet the fastest-growing San Diego companies making waves! 📣 Nominate your company for recognition in our annual San Diego Business Impact Awards with @San Diego Regional Economic Development Corporation, where we’re honoring: Early-stage innovators: Companies with ~$5M in revenue and impressive year-over-year growth Rising stars: Companies with $20M+ in revenue demonstrating staying power within the marketplace Established heavyweights: Industry leaders with $100M+ in revenue, committed to growing in San Diego Take just 15 minutes to complete your nomination by Monday, June 15—and join the celebration on July 23 ⬇️ Thank you to @Marsh McLennan for sponsoring this event, as well as @San Diego Magazine, @Scripps Research, and @Snake Oil Cocktail Company for supporting.

events.title: 2026 Congressional Luncheon
events.description: Join fellow business, community, and policy leaders for networking, and then hear directly from San Diego County’s Congressional delegation. Our nation’s leaders are tackling issues that uniquely impact us on the local, state, and national levels – border infrastructure, economic outlook, political landscape, housing & homelessness, and more. During the discussion, attendees will have the opportunity to submit questions of their own to be answered by the Congressmembers. This year’s discussion will be moderated by Joey Safchik, Emmy-nominated Political Reporter for NBC San Diego and host of “Politically Speaking.” Joey’s experience covering local, state, and national politics—including elections, immigration, homelessness, and public policy—will bring an informed and engaging perspective to the conversation. For a recap of this event, we invite you to watch the highlight video from a previous luncheon. Event Details Date: Monday, June 15, 2026 Time: 11:00 a.m. Check-in & Networking | 11:45 a.m. – 1:30 p.m. Luncheon & Program Location: Hyatt Regency La Jolla at Aventine, 3777 La Jolla Village Dr, San Diego, CA 92122 Parking: Hotel Self Parking: $15 Valet: $25/vehicle Event Contact: Monica Negrete, mnegrete@sdchamber.org, 619-544-1343 Sponsorship & Ticket Costs: Sponsorship packages are available. Please click here or contact Sherman Stocker at sstocker@sdchamber.org or 619-544-1354 for details. Ticket Rates: Chamber Member Ticket (Early Bird): $175 Chamber Member Ticket (After May 31): $225 Table of Ten (Member): $1,900 Guest & Sponsor Options: Non-member Ticket: $350 Table of Ten (Non-member): $3000 Corporate Sponsor: $7,500 Media interested in covering the Luncheon, please get in touch with Alison Phillips at APhillips@SDChamber.org. events.title: Little Italy San Diego Tech Coffee
events.description: We’re bringing back Tech Coffee to the greater downtown area after a few year hiatus. What better spot for Tech Coffee than Little Italy, the coffee capital of San Diego! Join us at Portal Coffee overlooking the beautiful Waterfront Park and San Diego Harbor. There’s lots of available street parking along Kettner between Fir and Beech St just 1-3 blocks from Portal Coffee. See the Parking Map attached image. We’re also one block from the MTA Trolley and 2 blocks from Amtrak and the Coaster. Come by for some networking, sharing, brainstorming, and mentoring. And we love it when people want to share what they are working on! There is no agenda, which means you can’t be late – come when you can, leave when you need to. Very informal. New to the San Diego startup community? Or just want to be better connected? Check out the SD Tech Scene calendar – https://sdtechscene.org

events.title: Casual Commander – (12pm » 6pm)
events.description: Gather your friends, select your legendary commander, and unleash powerful spells and creatures in epic multiplayer battles. Experience the joy of building unique and flavorful decks, creating unforgettable gaming moments, and forging lasting friendships. Whether you’re a seasoned player or new to the format, Casual Commander offers a welcoming environment where fun and creativity take center stage.

events.title: SD CTO Talks Presents: The Real Cost of AI-Driven Development
events.description: AI is moving fast. But is faster always better? Engineering teams are embedding AI across the entire development lifecycle. What happens when velocity outpaces understanding? This month’s CTO Talks brings together senior engineering leaders to explore the real tradeoffs of AI-driven development. One shares lessons from accelerating with AI at enterprise scale. Another brings perspective on engineering rigor, code ownership, and long-term reliability. Tess Chu, Director of Engineering at ServiceNow, brings 20 years leading large-scale technology initiatives across consumer and enterprise sectors. Her expertise spans distributed systems, infrastructure, scalability, and architecture. She’s built platforms that hold up under pressure and knows exactly what happens when they don’t. Whether you lead engineering teams or build products, this conversation is for you. Additional speakers will be announced shortly!

location.address: 10265 Science Center Drive, San Diego, CA

events.title: Rockets Adventures
events.description: When: Every Monday at 6:00pm-9:00pm Entry Fee (Weekly): $5 Character Creation Fee: $5 How do you join? -Anyone can join! In order to join a player must pay a $5 Character creation fee or use a pre-made character. Players will be able to attend every week or whenever they feel like it. What is a Character Creation fee? -All characters for DnD at Rockets will have to be made through our DND Beyond account. Players will have access to bonus expansions this way. Characters will be kept on the TC’s Rockets account and be made in-store as well. How does it work? -There will be a set amount of Dungeon Masters provided by Rockets. Each DM will adventure with 5 players. All stories will be provided by Rockets along with in game accessories. What else is there? -Every week you’ll receive raffle tickets with your entry fee. Raffles will vary every week!

events.title: Mad House Open Mic! Free. Open to Anyone. Come watch stars be born and dreams die 5 min at a time :)
events.description: OPEN MIC INFO… Is it free? Yes. It’s free and there are no food or drink minimums ever for open mic shows at Mad House Comedy Club. Is it free for a reason? Yes. It’s an open mic, so it’s literally “open” to anyone in the world that might want to go on stage including people who are quite insane. Is it a fun show? It depends on your pain tolerance. There are lots of comedians who are very funny practicing new material. There are new comedians who are terrible but they’re new, so they’re supposed to be awful and it’s kind of cute to watch them slowly die onstage. There are people who will make you cringe and decide it’s a great time to use the restroom and then there are comics who are so incredibly bad that it’s actually hilarious. The open mic is my favorite show. If you’re the type of person who doesn’t want to know how the sausage is made, then it’s probably not for you. If you’re the type of person who’d like to know the shocking truth of how stand up comedy really starts… this is your new home. Bonus… you get to hang out in an amazing bar with funny people. We have an open mic 7 nights a week.

events.title: Breakfast with some San Diego Digital Designers
events.description: Hello, digital designers! Let’s get together again and welcome new (and familiar) faces in and around the SD digital design scene. There are plenty of fancy design events, this is meant to be a more casual, personal, and intimate meeting of designers. Introvert- and extrovert-friendly. Conversation, not networking. Priority will be given to SDDD members (joining is easy — assuming you are in fact a digital designer in San Diego — and free at sddd.org). Again, this is totally casual. No agenda, no presentation. Everyone pays for their own meal, grabs a seat at the table, and brings their friendly faces. Let’s chat about trends, workplace issues, design tools and approaches, job opportunities, and digital culture. Hit up the chat here in Meetup — or in #events in Slack — with any questions at all. See you there!

There’s no pitching, only networking, sharing, brainstorming, mentoring, etc. And we love it when people want to share what they are working on! There is no agenda, which means you can’t be late – come when you can, leave when you need to. Very informal. New to the San Diego startup community? Or just want to be better connected? events.title: Tuesday Team Up
events.description: The Group is growing and we are excited to announce the launch of Tuesday Team Up at Maya Moon Collective at 3349 Adams Ave, San Diego, CA 92116 at 9:00 am. This new chapter is more than just a gathering. It is a chance to expand our movement of empowerment, inspiration, and support into the heart of San Diego. Whether you cannot always make it to Feel Good Fridays in Encinitas or you are ready to double down on connection by joining both, Tuesday Team Up was created for you. It is a place where authentic conversations spark new opportunities, where encouragement turns into action, and where people come together to lift each other higher both personally and professionally. Our vision is to create spaces throughout San Diego where everyone feels they belong, where ideas become reality, and where community becomes the foundation for growth. Tuesday Team Up is another step toward that vision and we would love for you to be part of it.

events.title: The Group (North Park) – San Diego’s Best Non-Networking Networking Event
events.description: June An is the visionary founder of The Group, a community born from the triumphs and trials of his own entrepreneurial journey. As a seasoned entrepreneur, June understands the personal and professional challenges that can often feel overwhelming. His journey has taken him through moments of helplessness and hopelessness, which fueled his passion to create a space where others don’t have to navigate these challenges alone. Inspired by his experiences, June founded The Group with a clear mission: to empower individuals to overcome obstacles and pursue their dreams with renewed hope and determination. The Group is more than just a community; it’s a place where dreams are nurtured, resilience is strengthened, and people come together to achieve their full potential—both personally and professionally. Let The Group inspire you to live your dreams with purpose and confidence. https://www.thegroupcoastal.com/

events.title: San Diego AgTech Gathering – June 2026
events.description: Welcome to the monthly San Diego AgTech Gathering, a monthly community meetup. Start your morning with purpose and connection at the San Diego AgTech Gathering — a monthly meetup bringing together San Diego’s agriculture, food, and agtech communities at the future home of the Escondido AgX Hub on Quince Street. Whether you’re a grower, technologist, entrepreneur, researcher, or industry champion, this is your space to gather, sip, and spark ideas. Too often, technology and agriculture operate in parallel instead of together. This gathering will explore what it looks like when founders and farmers actually work side by side — sharing a featured conversation alongside open networking time. Our theme and guest speaker will be announced soon. Coffee provided. Good conversations guaranteed. This meet-up is part of the broader vision behind Escondido AgX, a collaborative ecosystem aimed at fostering innovation in agriculture, food production, and sustainability through community, programming, and place. Check out highlights from the last AgX Gathering to get a taste of the energy. Please reach out directly by email for a ticket fee waiver.

events.title: CEDH
events.description: A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition.

events.title: Legacy
events.description: Legacy is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards No maximum deck size Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used Allows cards from all legal sets unless otherwise banned for power level reasons. With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, and Forest), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age blockâ€”Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forestâ€”are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block. Legacy decks may consist of cards from allÂ MagicÂ card sets, plus the following cards: Sewers of Estark, Windseeker Centaur, and Nalathni Dragon (Mana Crypt would also be included were it not currently bannedâ€”see below). Cards from expansions and special sets (likeÂ From the Vault,Â Magic: The Gatheringâ€”Commander,Â Duel Decks,Â Conspiracy, etc.) are legal in the Legacy format on the date of release of the expansion or special set.

events.title: Dueling Pianos San Diego Tuesday Night | Live Music at The Shout! House
events.description: TUESDAY NIGHT – Doors 7pm | Pianos 8pm | 21+ Looking for the best dueling pianos show in San Diego on Tuesday night? Every Tuesday, The Shout! House in the Gaslamp Quarter delivers one of the most interactive live music experiences in downtown San Diego. Our high-energy dueling piano show features talented entertainers performing on grand pianos while the crowd sings along, requests songs, and becomes part of the show. Whether you’re planning a night out in the Gaslamp Quarter, celebrating a birthday, or simply looking for live music in San Diego on a Tuesday night, The Shout! House delivers a one-of-a-kind nightlife experience. PIANO PLAYERS: TBD TABLE RESERVATIONS Reservations available from 7:00pm – 7:45pm. (No reservations accepted after 7:45pm.) There is no reservation fee for guaranteed seating. Optional VIP upgrades are available for an additional fee. Guests included in reservations are not charged cover upon arrival. RESERVATIONS VIP PACKAGES WALK-INS FREE cover all night every Tuesday. FREE cover every night with college or military ID* *State-issued ID also required. PARKING $12 Flat-Rate Parking for 3 hours at Horton Plaza Parking Garage w/ validation. Regular rates apply after 3 hours. Ask your server for validation sticker. The Best Dueling Piano Show in San Diego When it comes to live music and nightlife in San Diego, The Shout! House is one of the Gaslamp Quarter’s most exciting destinations. Our famous dueling piano show features two talented performers playing face-to-face grand pianos in an electrifying, request-driven performance that keeps the energy high all night long. Expect sing-alongs, comedy, audience participation, staff dancing on stage, and music ranging from classic rock anthems to today’s biggest hits. No two shows are ever the same — the audience helps create the experience. Private Events & Group Parties Planning a birthday party, corporate event, bachelor or bachelorette celebration, or group night out in San Diego? The Shout! House is one of the most unique private event venues in the Gaslamp Quarter. Enjoy live entertainment, great food, craft cocktails, and a high-energy atmosphere your guests will never forget. Reserve your table today and Laugh, Sing & Shout the night away.

events.title: Out of Plain Sight: An L.A. Times Film
events.description: Join The Nat, the San Diego Regional Climate Collaborative, and Scripps Institution of Oceanography for a special screening and Q&A discussion of the award-winning Los Angeles Times documentary Out of Plain Sight. Not far from Catalina Island, scientists discovered a corroded barrel on the seafloor that gave them chills. Partnering with Pulitzer Prize finalist Rosanna Xia, they piece together a shocking revelation: in the years after World War II, as many as half a million barrels of toxic waste were quietly dumped into the ocean—right off our coast.

location.venue: San Diego Natural History Museum (The Nat)
location.address: 1788 El Prado, Balboa Park, San Diego, CA

events.title: JEFF ALLEN: MY FIRST FINAL FAREWELL TOUR
events.description:
Jeff Allen  may
(or may not) want to admit it, but he’s getting old. At 69, Jeff is 
staring down his 70th year and decided it might be time to think about 
slowing down. The result? My First Final Farewell Tour kicks off March 
1st, 2026. Yes, you read that right—“First” and “Final” in the same 
sentence—because Jeff’s not totally sure he’s done. (After all, Cher had
20 farewell tours, and Elton John is still out there.)

What he is sure of: he’s bringing a mix of  fan favorites and fresh new material  to the stage. From the classic  Happy Wife Happy Life  routine
you’ve been quoting for years, to fresh takes on marriage, family, and 
everyday life, Jeff is pulling it all together for a night that’s 
hilarious, unforgettable, and uplifting.

Jeff
Allen is one of America’s most beloved comedians, with nearly 1 billion
online views and a record-setting seven Dry Bar Comedy specials. He’s 
been featured on  America’s Got Talent, The Today Show, Comedy Central, Showtime,  and starred in the films  Apostles of Comedy  and  My 7 Grandmas.  Millions have seen him across Facebook, Instagram, TikTok, and YouTube—but nothing compares to seeing Jeff live.

No refunds or exchanges.
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest. 
Ages 21+

location.address: 8878 Clairemont Mesa Blvd , San Diego, CA

events.title: VibeCraft Power Hour
events.description: VibeCraft is a recurring, collaborative working session where ideas turn into experiments and experiments turn into real builds. Each session is shaped by the group. We review what is emerging, explore new directions, and decide together what is worth building next. This is not a presentation or a passive meetup. It is an active, hands-on environment where members contribute ideas, react to each other’s work, and influence the direction of future sessions. Expect open discussion, live demos, shared whiteboards, and practical exploration. Sessions may include live walkthroughs of tools, automations, and AI agents, discussions around product and company design, rapid demos from members, and breakdowns of different approaches to building with modern AI systems. Topics evolve over time, but the focus remains the same: learning by doing, building in public, and exploring a high-tech, high-touch future grounded in curiosity, collaboration, and thoughtful design. Come ready to participate, share what you are building, and help shape what we explore next as a VibeCraft community.

Stop by for networking, sharing, brainstorming, mentoring, etc. And we love it when people want to share what they are working on! There is no agenda, which means you can’t be late – come when you can, leave when you need to. Very informal. New to the San Diego startup community? Or just want to be better connected? Check out the Startup San Diego “Startup Ecosystem Resource List” at https://startupsd.org/resources/.

events.title: 1 Million Cups
events.description: 1 Million Cups is a weekly event for entrepreneurs, by entrepreneurs — built on the belief that conversations over coffee can accelerate innovation. Each week, two founders take the stage to pitch their business for six minutes, followed by 20 minutes of honest, constructive feedback from a room full of fellow entrepreneurs, business professionals, students, and curious minds. This isn’t a pitch competition — it’s a collaborative environment where ideas are refined, challenges are unpacked, and next steps become clearer. Attendees are encouraged to ask thoughtful questions, offer insights, and share resources that help presenters grow. Whether you’re launching your first startup, scaling your next venture, or just looking to get plugged into your local entrepreneurial ecosystem, 1 Million Cups is where you’ll find the energy, perspective, and community you need to move forward. Come for the coffee. Stay for the conversation. Leave with a better business.

events.title: “Make A.I. Work for You” Workshop
events.description: Join us for a free “Make AI Work for You” workshop led by a Grow with Google Coach Heard the buzz about AI, but wondering what it actually means for your business? In partnership with Grow with Google, we’re bringing you a free webinar that cuts through the noise. By exploring practical applications and real-life examples from business owners like you, you’ll leave with a personalized plan to put AI to work for your business right away. You won’t just learn about Google AI tools – you’ll get hands-on practice. More importantly, you’ll discover when and how to use them strategically to tackle common business challenges. Through hands-on exercises and practical examples, you’ll practice how to: Write Effective Prompts: Get clear, targeted results from AI by defining exactly what you need. Generate Innovative Ideas: Brainstorm solutions and overcome business hurdles when you feel stuck. Streamline Repetitive Tasks: Use AI tools to help you save time and valuable resources. This workshop is specifically designed for small business owners and their teams. No prior AI knowledge or technical expertise is required – just bring your business challenges! SPEAKERS EVENT DETAILS Date: June 17, 2026 Time: 9:00 a.m. – 10:00 a.m. Location: Zoom Event contact: Jimena Villaseñor (jvillasenor@sdchamber.org) REGISTRATION This event is open to the public as well as Chamber members. RSVP is required below to access the Zoom link. RSVP NOW!

events.title: Startup Quarters Curated Conversations
events.description: Startup Quarters is a weekly gathering held every Wednesday, discussing in breakout groups, Curated Conversations where founders and builders share ideas and work through challenges in real time. Designed to create momentum through consistent connection, it brings together multiple startup-focused interactions in one place, fostering ongoing mentorship, collaboration, and meaningful conversations that build week over week.

events.title: Casual Commander – (12pm » 6pm)
events.description: Gather your friends, select your legendary commander, and unleash powerful spells and creatures in epic multiplayer battles. Experience the joy of building unique and flavorful decks, creating unforgettable gaming moments, and forging lasting friendships. Whether you’re a seasoned player or new to the format, Casual Commander offers a welcoming environment where fun and creativity take center stage.

events.title: Let’s Cowork in PB!
events.description: Tired of working from your actual home, and want a change of scenery? New to San Diego and want to meet new people? Come join us at our weekly coworking session. It’s a pretty casual affair with new people every week, some regulars, and some just passing through. We take over a few tables at Second Nature in Pacific Beach and do a bit of work and a bit of getting to know each other. Come and go as you please, but do remember to bring your laptop (charged as plugs are few). It’s free to join, but ideally grab some coffee or lunch & tip the servers. A note about RSVPs: This group aims to help people meet in a small group. The max amount of people for this event is 12 people. We close the RSVP list on Mondays if there is a waitlist, and if you can’t make it we do kindly ask that you change your status as soon as you know to give someone else a chance to attend. Those on the waitlist will receive a note on the day of the meetup letting them know if there is space or not. If you sign up, but do not attend, you will be marked as a No Show. People with 5 No Shows will be removed from the group.

events.title: Warhammer
events.description: Warhammer is a tabletop miniature wargame with a medieval fantasy theme where players use miniature models to represent a battlefield filled with buildings, trees, hills, and other terrain features.

events.title: Pokémon
events.description: The Pokémon Trading Card Game is a tabletop game that involves collecting, trading and playing with Pokémon themed playing cards. It has its own set of rules but uses many motifs and ideas derived from the Pokémon video games. There are Pokémon cards for every species & type of Pokémon as well as Trainer cards featuring characters, items and other themes of the franchise (each with a different use) and Energy cards to power various actions.

events.title: Pauper
events.description: In this Magic format, all cards must have been printed at common rarity in a Magic set or product. Common promo cards are only legal if the card meets that qualification. If a common version of a particular card was ever released in Magic, any version of that card is legal in this format. Other than that, the usual rules for constructed decks apply: Minimum of 60 cards in the main deck (There is no maximum size for main decks) Up to 15 cards in your sideboard, if used No more than 4 of any individual card in the main deck and sideboard combined (with the exception of basic lands)

events.title: Standard Showdown
events.description: The Standard format uses the most recently released Magic sets. Standard is a dynamic format where you build decks and play using cards in your collection from recently released Magic sets. Evolving gameplay and fresh strategies make it one of the most fun and popular ways to play Magic.

events.title: San Diego Arduino Enthusiasts
events.description: Please join us at our monthly meeting. 7pm on the third Wednesday. The Escape Room Steal & Escape graciously allow us to use their space. Please enter through the door under the “A | ROYALE WEAR” sign. Lat/Lon 32°39’35.6″N 117°06’09.1″W Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/zGrMUfKtBgo1rtnc8 Tonight — We will continue with the development of the low-power PCB arduino project: demonstrate programming the WeAct STM32G431CB boards and as time allows: 2) drive the OLED display, 3) Explore low power modes of the STM32G431CB Bring your WeAct dev kit! What we’re about The purpose of this groups is to share ideas, experiences, projects, and code for Arduino-based electronics.

events.title: SAMUEL ORSON
events.description:
Samuel
Orson is a Los Angeles based stand-up comedian known
for his surreal yet high energy style. He is most known for his viral 
“POV: You Are an Open Mic Stand-Up Comedian” series, where he shows the 
awkward, chaotic reality of grinding through open mics as a comic. The 
series gives viewers an inside look at bombing
on stage, late night sets, empty rooms, and the strange world of 
stand-up comedy, and has generated more than 100 million views across 
social media.

His hour long show is full of well crafted bits, and absurd punchlines
creating an act where you can appreciate the writing as well how it’s performed. 

Samuel has performs at The Comedy Store and appeared on Don’t Tell
Comedy showcases while building a growing audience online through stand-up clips and short-form videos. 

Ages 21+

events.title: The Builders and the Butchers, Little Foot, Yes Ma'am
$125K International Pitch Competition
events.description: Aquillius Ventures partnered with Big Tree Startup Academy and BioInc to organize the pitch competition you don’t want to miss! This premier life sciences event brings together innovative startups, seasoned investors, and industry leaders for a day of pitching, networking, and collaboration — held in a hybrid format across Aquillius in San Diego, CA and Mansfield Bio-Incubator in Mansfield, MA. Competing startups will present their breakthrough solutions in human health and environmental sustainability for a chance to win $125,000 in prizes, including cash awards, lab space, and professional services. Attendees can expect two rounds of startup presentations, a unique Reverse Pitch segment where investors pitch to founders, facility tours, mentorship, and ample networking. Why attend Learn about innovations that will revolutionize health Explore investment opportunities Network with industry veterans and the new generation shaping the future of life sciences Showcase your products and services to a targeted audience of professionals and stakeholders Visibility within the Big Tree ecosystem and investor community Why compete $125K in Prizes: Cash awards, lab space, and professional services Showcase Your Startup: Present your innovative solutions to leading investors and prospective partners Mentorship: Receive valuable feedback from judges and mentors to refine your pitch Networking: Access our extensive life science ecosystem’s network This registration is for the GENERAL ATTENDEES ONLY If you’re an investor, please register here: https://www.bigtreeinnovationfund.com/investor-registration

events.title: Coffee, Networking & Success: Entrepreneurs and Investors Unite
events.description: “Coffee, Networking, and the Path to Success.” This gathering is the perfect arena for business owners eager to widen their network, real estate aficionados keen on uncovering the newest investment tactics, and budding entrepreneurs on the quest for success. It’s an inviting platform to share your journey, soak up wisdom from sector specialists, and forge meaningful ties to boost your professional trajectory. Our sessions are centered around engaging discussions on crafting a lucrative enterprise, mastering growth strategies, and evolving into an accomplished professional. Tackle the barriers to success, including overcoming doubt and harnessing your sales and business development talents for mutual enhancement. This meetup is not just an event; it’s a stepping stone to broadening your horizons, making impactful connections, and setting forth on a triumphant path in your personal and career pursuits. Join us at the serene cove wood in Mission Bay, where like-minded spirits unite. Embrace this chance to enrich your knowledge and network and navigate your way to success. See you there!

events.title: Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain Discussion
events.description: Join us for an engaging discussion on the latest advancements in Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain technology. Our event will cover topics such as Machine Learning, Ethereum, Smart Contracts, and how these innovations are shaping the future of Decentralized Systems & Applications. Whether you are a tech enthusiast, developer, or industry professional, this event will offer valuable insights into the intersection of AI and Blockchain. In this meetup, we will dive deep into the potential of leveraging AI within Blockchain networks, the impact of Cryptocurrency on decentralized systems, and the evolution of Bitcoin in the digital landscape. From understanding the basics of Blockchain to exploring real-world applications of Smart Contracts, this event is designed to cater to individuals at all levels of expertise. Don’t miss this opportunity to network with like-minded individuals and gain valuable knowledge about the dynamic world of Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain.

Your role is the commander of a unique army filled with troopers, powerful vehicles, and iconic characters like Darth Vader or Luke Skywalker. While innovative mechanics simulate the chaos of battle, the unpainted, easily assembled miniatures give you a canvas to create the Star Wars army you’ve always dreamed of leading into battle.

events.title: CEDH
events.description: A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition.

events.title: Modern
events.description: Modern is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards Cards from Core Set Eighth Edition and Mirrodin through today No maximum deck size, as long as you can shuffle your deck unassisted Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and Wastes), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age block Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forest are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block.

events.title: LED VARELA - POPULAR STAND UP COMEDY
events.description:
SHOW DESCRIPTION 

Led
Varela is a Venezuelan-Uruguayan comedian, writer, and actor who has 
built an impressive career across stand-up, television, and digital 
platforms. Residing in Miami, he is one of the most recognized 
Spanish-speaking comedians, with a growing international audience.

Currently,
Led is touring, performing to sold-out crowds in major cities across 
Latin America, the U.S., and Europe. His previous stand-up specials, 
“Incorrecto”, “Felicidad”, “Orgullo Nacional” and “Locura”, have 
accumulated millions of views on YouTube, solidifying his status as a 
must-watch voice in comedy. 

Beyond
stand-up, Led has had multiple television appearances, including 
hosting “La Sopa Venezuela” on E! Entertainment Television and working 
as a writer and cast member on the popular late-night show “Chataing TV”
in Venezuela. 

Led
is also the host of the popular “Bla Bla Bla Podcast”, where he 
explores comedy, culture, and personal anecdotes, connecting with a 
dedicated fan base across platforms. His sharp, observational humor has 
earned him a place among the top Spanish-language comedians today.

DESCRIPCIÓN EN ESPAÑOL 

Led
Varela es un comediante, escritor y actor venezolano-uruguayo que ha 
construido una carrera destacada en el stand-up, la televisión y las 
plataformas digitales.

Residiendo
en Miami, es uno de los comediantes de habla hispana más reconocidos, 
con una audiencia internacional en crecimiento. 

Actualmente,
Led se encuentra de gira, presentándose ante salas llenas en 
importantes ciudades de América Latina, Estados Unidos y Europa. Sus 
especiales de stand-up, “Incorrecto”, “Felicidad”, “Orgullo Nacional” y 
“Locura”, han acumulado millones de vistas en YouTube, consolidándolo 
como una de las voces más relevantes de la comedia en español.

Más
allá del stand-up, Led ha tenido múltiples apariciones en televisión, 
incluyendo su rol como host del programa “La Sopa Venezuela” en E! 
Entertainment Television y su trabajo como guionista y miembro del 
elenco en el popular late-night show “Chataing TV” en Venezuela. 

Además,
es el creador y conductor de “Bla Bla Bla Podcast”, donde explora la 
comedia, la cultura y anécdotas personales, conectando con una audiencia
fiel en diversas plataformas. Su estilo de humor, agudo y 
observacional, lo ha convertido en uno de los comediantes más destacados
en español en la actualidad.

No refunds or exchanges.
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest. 
 Ages 21+

events.title: GROUP CHAT GIRLIES
events.description:
Get ready for a special girls’ night out presented by Group Chat Girlies.

Skylar Schock and Carol Tyner host a one-of-a-kind evening made just for the girlies. 

Enjoy
stand up comedy, interactive segments, and unpacking what's in the 
group chat. We plan to “spill the tea”, share recommendations, discuss 
pop culture and more. Think your group chat — but in person, on stage, 
with plenty of audible gasps and laughs. 

Girlie fight club is here.

Skylar
Schock and Carol Tyner are known for their sharp, relatable takes on 
dating, friendship, and all things girlhood. This show blends comedy and
conversations in a unique format.

Skylar is a bi-coastal stand-up
comic, actor, writer, and producer. She has performed across the 
country at venues including The Comedy Store (La Jolla), Jimmy Kimmel’s 
Comedy Club (Las Vegas), and the Laugh After Dark stage. She's opened 
for headliners including Cristina Mariani, Michael Yo, John Caparulo, 
and Ivy Wolk. Skylar was a Best of Fest selection at the Comedy Chateau 
Comedy Festival (LA), an official performer at the Queen City Comedy 
Experience (CLT) and advanced in the WSOC (ATL and Ohio). She is the 
creator of Group Chat Girlies, a unique format live show, and Laugh 
Until You Cry, a stand-up show spotlighting mental health. Film credits 
include For the Love of Lily and the Austin Film Fest Award-winning 
short Betsy F*ing Ross.

Carol Tyner is a stand-up comedian, 
improviser, and producer. Carol has performed at comedy clubs including 
The Idiot Box Comedy Club, The Comedy Club at Duckworth’s, The Comedy 
Zone (Charlotte & Greenville). She has opened for headliners 
including Tim Meadows, Erica Rhodes, Cliff Cash, Cam Bertrand, and 
Conner King. Carol has been a featured performer in festivals including:
The North Carolina Comedy Festival, The Queen City Comedy 
Experience,, The NewSouth Comedy Festival, and The Asheville Comedy 
Festival. She is a founding cast member of the award-winning Shameless 
Improv Society. Carol also co-produces Group Chat Girlies, a live 
comedy show that mixes stand-up with interactive audience segments 
driving women-led conversations. In addition, she co-creates The Lynns, a
character-driven sketch duo, and We Rate Your Run, a 
(wo)man-on-the-street social series known for its quick, off-the-cuff 
humor.

Bring your group chat or find a new one! 

Ages 21+

events.title: [ONLINE] EE Business Networking @Lunch (free!)
events.description: Why go it alone? It only takes one person to change our lives personally or professionally. Who will you meet today at “EE“? See additional info and direct links below… Exceptional Entrepreneurs is one of the USA’s largest business networking organizations – and one of the best values in business networking. At EE: There is no membership required. There is no attendance requirement. There is no business profession or category exclusivity. ALL business professions are welcome to attend! Meeting Agenda: First 30 minutes: check-in & breakout rooms open. Remaining 60 minutes: structured agenda with everyone in main room, including introductions by participants + more! While meetings end on time (for those who need to go), we also open breakout rooms again for those wanting to meet with other attendees afterwards (at least 30 minutes, usually longer.. fyi). Interested in being the featured speaker for this event? Information and registration is at: http://EEspeaker.com Info to join this EE business networking meeting: ####################################### http://nroi.us/eeonline (the shortlink above will remain updated with the current Zoom link) Passcode: 7772021 ####################################### Limited to the first 100 participants. We also cross-livestream through the EE FB Group. No participant limit on FB, however it’s view-only mode. Please feel free to leave your contact info in the comments of the livestream when listening along: https://www.facebook.com/groups/networkingeesd The meetings (including chat) will be recorded to the cloud – and made available temporarily afterwards. Information on recordings will be provided to attendees on Zoom and Facebook. Exceptional Entrepreneurs was founded November, 2008 in San Diego, California USA by David & Gina Demangos. Since then, “EE” has become one of the USA’s largest business networking organizations, due to the Demangos’ vision and leadership to provide “exceptional” opportunities for connecting local professionals. David Demangos is an eXp real estate agent in San Diego. For more information on David, please see: Awesome San Diego Real Estate: http://www.awesomesandiegorealestate.com “Effective networking is a learned skill. Exceptional Entrepreneurs are highly trusted professionals who share ideas and know that in order to receive you must first be willing to give.” – David Demangos Thank you for your continuing support of “EE”! See you at the next event.

events.description: Gather your friends, select your legendary commander, and unleash powerful spells and creatures in epic multiplayer battles. Experience the joy of building unique and flavorful decks, creating unforgettable gaming moments, and forging lasting friendships. Whether you’re a seasoned player or new to the format, Casual Commander offers a welcoming environment where fun and creativity take center stage.

events.title: Nat at Night
events.description: On Fridays, the San Diego Natural History Museum is open until 11 PM just for you. Enjoy access to all open exhibitions and our rooftop deck for good views and great vibes. The rooftop is open to all visitors and serves beer, wine, non-alcoholic drinks, and food from Wolf in the Woods. Take a selfie with a megalodon shark, see scientists at work in our Paleo Center, admire glittering gems, get a close up look at spiders and snakes, and so much more. Best of all, get a look at our brand-new exhibition, Washed Ashore: Art to Save the Sea and grab a drink in the dive bar while chatting with a scientist. On select dates, channel your inner artist with Sketchparty, get competitive during trivia, or unwind while listening to the rooftop DJ. Hungry for more? The Craft Taco, located inside the Atrium, is a vibrant taco restaurant offering a delectable fusion of flavors and artisanal creations. They offer made-to-order tacos featuring signature blue corn tortillas, delectable burritos and bowls, and a variety of drinks and coffees. Admission is half-price after 5 PM, and parking is free after 6 PM. You can buy your tickets online in advance or buy them at the door the night of. As always, members of The Nat and Explorer pass holders enjoy free general admission. Fill your summer with wonder at The Nat. With weekly Nat at Night, brunches, weekend family programming, and so much more, membership just makes sense. Not to mention, you’re directly supporting nature conservation in our region. Join us today.

events.title: Modern
events.description: Modern is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards Cards from Core Set Eighth Edition and Mirrodin through today No maximum deck size, as long as you can shuffle your deck unassisted Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and Wastes), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age block Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forest are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block.

events.title: Lernazang Ensemble: Revitalizing Armenian Heritage Through Music and Dance
events.description: Experience the living pulse of Armenian folk culture through a captivating performance by Lernazang Ensemble, a collective of musicians, dancers, and ethnomusicologists dedicated to preserving and reimagining Armenian music and dance. Featuring the evocative sounds of duduk, oud, dhol, and voice, alongside Armenian dance, the ensemble brings to life the music and movement of Western Armenia (Anatolia) and the Southern Caucasus. Their approach aims to revitalize Armenian folk heritage by blending deep cultural research with striking artistic expression. Members of the audience are invited to engage with the artists in a Q&A following the performance to learn more about the traditions, history, and contemporary significance of Armenian music and dance. About the Artists Lernazang is a nonprofit organization, cultural collective, and performance ensemble based in Los Angeles. Their mission is to decolonize Armenian dance and music aesthetics while strengthening Armenian cultural heritage in the diaspora. Mingei Members get a 10% discount using code MINGEI. Complimentary valet parking is available for this event from 7:00 PM to 9:30 PM. Please check in with an attendant near Mingei entrance.

events.title: Fleet Accessibility Mornings
events.description: Early access to galleries at 9 a.m. On the third Saturday of every month, we open our doors one hour before the museum opens to the public and screen a documentary with house lights on and the soundtrack volume low. And if you want to stay and enjoy the museum all day? You can! Accessibility Morning features a special film screening of a documentary at 10 a.m. with house lights on and the soundtrack volume low. The Fleet Science Center strives to be an inclusive place where people of all needs and abilities are welcome and respectfully accommodated every day. Science is for everyone. Admission is free for one visitor and an accompanying guest. Regular admission rates apply for additional visitors. Noise-canceling headsets are available for use during your visit to the Fleet Science Center. Please inquire at the ticket counter or at the Heikoff Giant Dome Theater. For more information regarding accessibility at the Fleet Science Center, please visit our Accessibility page.

events.title: Tai Chi Ch’uan Lee Style
events.description: A class session consists of gentle limbering exercises that aid in coordination, balance, and the opening of various channels within the body to allow your Chi to flow freely. In addition, we incorporate respiration therapy such as our style’s form of Qigong, K’ai Men, otherwise known as Taoist Yoga and various specialized breathing exercises referred to as ‘Dao Yin’. Moreover, we include partner exercises that reinforce the underlying principles of being tenderly gentle, soft, and giving way to all force, provided that our style requires absolutely no physical strength. Furthermore, Taoist physical exercises include parts of the art of Tai Chi Ch’uan: The form of Tai Chi Ch’uan and Tai Chi Dance. The physical movements of Tai Chi Ch’uan are gentle, natural and an exceptionally light therapeutic exercise.

events.title: Online: Saturday Study Group
events.description: We are continuing our Study group online for the time being. We are now meeting online in the San Diego Python Discord server! Here is the link to join: https://discord.gg/8GpWuVvdbQ There is a “meetup” voice/video channel that we will be using! We may start meeting in person soon! This will be discussed at the meetup. We are considering having some online, some in-person. Decisions, decisions! Every Saturday we meet up with the larger Python community (including San Diego PyLadies (https://www.meetup.com/sd-pyladies), Open BioInformatics San Diego (https://www.meetup.com/Open-Bioinformatics-SanDiego/) and San Diego Artificial Intelligence (https://www.meetup.com/SanDiegoAI/)) to chat, learn, and hang out. Our study group is for everyone on their Python journey, whether you’re just starting out, an experienced pro, or anywhere in between. There’s no formal instruction, just a bunch of folks who like to code. Sometimes we talk about a specific subject, sometimes answer questions, whatever! Python Community Code of Conduct (http://www.pythonsd.org/coc): Python is much more than a software language. Python is a vibrant community made up of members from around the globe with a diverse set of skills, personalities, and experiences. It is through these differences that our community experiences great successes and continued growth. Overall, we’re good to each other. We contribute to this community not because we have to, but because we want to.

events.title: Diving deeper into software development discussions
events.description: • What we’ll do Come join in discussions about Vim, Emacs, Linux, Elixir, Erlang, OTP, Lisp, Haskell, Ruby, Javascript, Functional Programming, Object Oriented Programming, Blockchain, HashGraph, Distributed Systems, Decentralized Systems, and whatever peaks your interest. • What to bring A laptop, good questions, exciting ideas, an open mind and a positive attitude. • Where to meet Meet by the corn hole game in the back and look for others with laptops. Ask for others that are there for the software discussion group. • Alternate location Some nights the Rabbit Hole has live music. I was not able to pin down an exact schedule, just sometimes we would show up and there would be a band but most times there was not. As an alternate location on live music nights the discussion group could be moved just down the street to Lestats coffee shop. They have great coffee and a nice atmosphere for hanging out and discussing software. • Important to know This is a self organizing open discussion group that is meant to be organic, with no main speaker or leader. It is just a time and a place for like minded people to come together to talk about software. • Background When I was staying in SD (Jan-Aug 2018) I was teaching software development at UCSD and the Meetup gave my cohort a space to dive further into topics that the curriculum did not allow time for. When the class was over and I went back to traveling full time, I thought it would be an interesting experiment to leave the Meetup going and see if people would still gather to discuss software without someone to lead it. I liked the idea of a group that did not require some one to always lead it. A lot off Meetups I have been to before were always one or two people doing a lot of work to organize and put on talks but everyone else just shows up to consume and doesn’t really participate that much. Kinda like going to a movie, in a way. The idea for this group is that it is what people make it. You get out of it what you put into it. It’s great that people are showing up still and I hope you have some great discussions. When I get back to San Diego (planning on a handful of times a year) and there is a meeting I will stop by and hang out and invite my cohort of previous students.

events.title: GREG WARREN: IMPULSE BUY TOUR
events.description:
Greg Warren is a beloved stand-up comedian known for his sharp, 
relatable humor that resonates with everyday families across the 
country. Warren’s latest comedy special, The Champ, was produced by 
Nate Bargatze and premiered on Nateland’s YouTube channel. The Champ 
takes audiences through a hilarious exploration of the dangers of 
poison ivy, the rise of high school fishing teams, and his own 
(somewhat questionable) claims to champion status. 

Greg’s previous special, The Salesman — co-produced by Nate Bargatze and 800 Pound Gorilla Media — was hailed by The New York Times ,
as “ [having] done for peanut butter what Jerry Seinfeld did for 
Pop-Tarts and Jim Gaffigan did for Hot Pockets.” The hour tells the 
story of Greg’s post-college years selling Jif and Pringles for Procter
& Gamble, a formative chapter that ultimately launched his career 
in stand-up comedy. He can be seen in his Amazon Prime Special, Where The Field Corn Grows ,
Fish Sandwich on Dry Bar Comedy, Late Night with Seth Meyers, The Late
Late Show and Comedy Central Presents. Greg’s audience consists of a 
widely diverse spectrum, having performed as a finalist on BET’s Coming
to the Stage and on Country Music Television. He is a longtime 
favorite on the nationally syndicated Bob & Tom Show, where he also
contributes a recurring call-in segment known as The Warren Report. 

Greg’s material has been a daily staple on SiriusXM for over a decade 
and can now be heard regularly on the Pure Comedy channel (formerly 
Laugh USA), as well as Jeff & Larry’s Comedy Roundup. He’s guested 
on top podcasts including Nateland, Never Not Funny, Bertcast, Dumb 
People Town, The Adam Carolla Show, and many more. He was selected as a 
New Face of Comedy at JFL, has performed on Comedy Central’s Premium 
Blend and participated in NBC’s Last Comic Standing. Greg is also the 
co-host of The Consumers, a podcast on Nate Bargatze’s Nateland 
network, where he and fellow comedians Tim Convy & Sean O’Brien do a
weekly deep dive into one national brand. 

Greg hails from St. 
Louis, MO, where his father was a high school wrestling coach, and his 
mother made him play clarinet in the band. He has captivated audiences 
with anecdotes about the conflict inherent in meshing the two disparate
high school identities of varsity wrestler and band geek. Greg was 
also a West Point Cadet, where he distinguished himself by amassing an 
impressive tally of demerits and endless hours of pointless marching. 
After moving on to the University of Missouri, he studied journalism

events.title: GREG WARREN: IMPULSE BUY TOUR
events.description:
Greg Warren is a beloved stand-up comedian known for his sharp, 
relatable humor that resonates with everyday families across the 
country. Warren’s latest comedy special, The Champ, was produced by 
Nate Bargatze and premiered on Nateland’s YouTube channel. The Champ 
takes audiences through a hilarious exploration of the dangers of 
poison ivy, the rise of high school fishing teams, and his own 
(somewhat questionable) claims to champion status. 

Greg’s previous special, The Salesman — co-produced by Nate Bargatze and 800 Pound Gorilla Media — was hailed by The New York Times ,
as “ [having] done for peanut butter what Jerry Seinfeld did for 
Pop-Tarts and Jim Gaffigan did for Hot Pockets.” The hour tells the 
story of Greg’s post-college years selling Jif and Pringles for Procter
& Gamble, a formative chapter that ultimately launched his career 
in stand-up comedy. He can be seen in his Amazon Prime Special, Where The Field Corn Grows ,
Fish Sandwich on Dry Bar Comedy, Late Night with Seth Meyers, The Late
Late Show and Comedy Central Presents. Greg’s audience consists of a 
widely diverse spectrum, having performed as a finalist on BET’s Coming
to the Stage and on Country Music Television. He is a longtime 
favorite on the nationally syndicated Bob & Tom Show, where he also
contributes a recurring call-in segment known as The Warren Report. 

Greg’s material has been a daily staple on SiriusXM for over a decade 
and can now be heard regularly on the Pure Comedy channel (formerly 
Laugh USA), as well as Jeff & Larry’s Comedy Roundup. He’s guested 
on top podcasts including Nateland, Never Not Funny, Bertcast, Dumb 
People Town, The Adam Carolla Show, and many more. He was selected as a 
New Face of Comedy at JFL, has performed on Comedy Central’s Premium 
Blend and participated in NBC’s Last Comic Standing. Greg is also the 
co-host of The Consumers, a podcast on Nate Bargatze’s Nateland 
network, where he and fellow comedians Tim Convy & Sean O’Brien do a
weekly deep dive into one national brand. 

Greg hails from St. 
Louis, MO, where his father was a high school wrestling coach, and his 
mother made him play clarinet in the band. He has captivated audiences 
with anecdotes about the conflict inherent in meshing the two disparate
high school identities of varsity wrestler and band geek. Greg was 
also a West Point Cadet, where he distinguished himself by amassing an 
impressive tally of demerits and endless hours of pointless marching. 
After moving on to the University of Missouri, he studied journalism 
and became an All-American college wrestler. Warren can be seen working
out his new material on a brand new domestic tour throughout 2025 and 
2026. You can find tickets on GregWarrencomedy.com.

Ages 21+

Every Third Sunday of the month (except January and February) This artisan market’s mission is to create a space that connects local crafters and community members to promote an autonomous economy and community building. There are always arts and crafts, natural self-care products, home goods, and more. Plus, there will be fun activities for the entire family, including: Live Music and Children’s art workshops and more! Come out to connect, build community and support local art.

events.title: 4th Annual Juneteenth Harriet Tubman Bird Walk
events.description: Join us for a powerful morning of remembrance, nature, and cultural history as WorldBeat Cultural Center hosts the 4th Annual Harriet Tubman Freedom Birdwatch in honor of Juneteenth and the legacy of Black resistance and liberation. This year’s experience will take place in and around WorldBeat Center’s gardens, where participants will engage in guided birdwatching, cultural storytelling, and hands-on learning. In partnership with Celebrate Urban Birds and the Cornell Lab of Ornithology, we’ll be using the Merlin Bird ID app to identify local birds by sight and sound. We’ll also reflect on the important role of birds in history—especially how Harriet Tubman used the call of the barred owl as a secret signal during Underground Railroad missions. Event Highlights: Guided birdwatching using the Merlin Bird ID app and led by the SoCal Bird Nerds and San Diego Bird Alliance Stories of how the barred owl and other birds were used as coded signals Native habitat restoration with native planting African Peace Drum Circle Juneteenth Freedom Soul Food Plate This is more than a birdwatch—it’s a celebration of heritage, resilience, and the deep connection between nature and liberation. Free and open to the public. All ages welcome. Let’s learn, honor, and walk in the path of freedom—together.

events.title: A Space for All of Us
events.description: Sensory-Friendly Quiet Mornings at the Museum The Museum’s sensory-friendly quiet hours are held every Sunday from 10 a.m. to 11 a.m. This time is dedicated to creating a calmer, quieter museum experience. We remain open to the public during this time, and all are welcome to visit the Museum. During this time, specific exhibit features in the main Museum building will be turned off to minimize audio and visual stimulation. Noise-blocking headphones are free for check-out at the main Admissions desk and the entrance to the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibition, Exhibit features in the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibit will remain on, and all exhibits will be fully turned on at 11 a.m. Admission purchased during sensory-friendly quiet hours is the same as standard admission and applies to the entire day. Please contact publicprograms@museumofus.org with any questions.

events.title: JUSTIN RIVERA COMEDIAN MAGICIAN
events.description:
Justin Rivera is a nationally recognized, headlining, Filipino American comedian and magician, who has appeared on many, worldwide stages and national and international television shows. Rivera completed an 10-month, international tour in Canada, Australia, Japan, China, Germany, France, Spain, Belgium, Portugal, Netherlands, Singapore, South Africa, Thailand, Malaysia, Seychelles, Philippines, Taiwan, India, Vietnam, Indonesia, Myanmar, Mongolia, and Cambodia with rave reviews and standing ovations which included royalty from the Middle East.

Justin competed on NBC's "America's Got Talent" hosted by Nick Cannon and was named a top 4, judges' favorite by Howard Stern, Howie Mandel, and Sharon Osbourne. He has worked as talent for a pilot shot with Ken Jeong, live shows with Jo Koy, a Peacock Christmas special, FOX’s 30 Seconds to Fame, TruTV’s Operation Repo, Estrella TV’s Tengo Talento Mucho Talento, and appeared on "Fast Track to Fame” on the Speed Channel, “The Gong Show” on Comedy Central with Dave Attell and Adam Carolla, Comedy Central Stand Up Asia, Playboy TV, Discovery, and Univision. Rivera also finished an international comedy series for the Asia-based streaming service iFlix which has 5 million subscribers and major investors such as MGM.

Justin consulted for the Grammy-nominated band OneRepublic—customizing and creating visual magic illusions for the popular music video “Love Runs Out.” He has also performed at singer Christina Milian’s private event and done charity work for Apl.de.Ap of the Grammy award-winning The Black Eyed Peas.

As a seasoned performer of 35 years in magic and 20 years in comedy across 47 US states and 35 countries on 5 continents Justin is known as the “anti-magician” and “comedian’s magician” and has performed before esteemed public venues such as Pechanga, Morongo, Pala casinos, the Laugh Factory, the Comedy & Magic Club, and the Improv, and private venues such as the Magic Castle in Hollywood, CA where he passed the audition for their Junior Society on his first attempt at the minimum age of 13 only after starting magic at 6 years old when he received a magic set for his birthday.

Due to Rivera's established reputation and career he has been in demand from corporations such as Google and international cruises on Princess Cruise Corp. which led him to land deals that include a Las Vegas hotel & casino headlining residency show. He was part of a residency show at Jimmy Kimmel’s Comedy Club on the Vegas Strip and headlined a residency show at the House of Magic in Las Vegas. Justin is currently in negotiations for a new headlining residency show in Hawaii for his 2026 touring schedule.

Justin's contributions have been showcased within large corporate gatherings at the Consumer Electronic Show (CES) in Las Vegas, NV. He has also consulted for McDonald's using his talents to help brainstorm ideas for new marketing strategies.

No refunds or exchanges.
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest. 
 Ages 10+

events.title: Art + Design Workshop: Ceramic Candleholders
events.description: Discover the meditative art of hand-building with clay in the immersive ceramic workshop, set within the inspiring surroundings of the Museum’s James A Lester Gallery. Led by local ceramic artist Aeryn O’Halloran, participants will learn foundational techniques- including pinch and slab construction, slip-and-score joining, and elemental coiling- while crafting a Modernist-inspired pair of ceramic candleholders. For the finishing touch, select a glaze palette inspired by Thomas Birch’s An American Ship in Distress (1841), echoing the painting’s deep sea greens and muted sage tones drawn from the Timken’s celebrated maritime masterpiece. Whether you are new to ceramics or looking to refine your practice, join us in welcoming the summer season through artful making. Workshop fee includes all materials, tools, and light refreshments. Ceramic materials possess a natural unpredictability. Even when handled with the utmost care and according to best practices, pieces may occasionally crack, warp, or break during the drying or firing process. Workshop participants acknowledge and understand these natural characteristics of working with clay.

events.title: Mad House Open Mic! Free. Open to Anyone. Come watch stars be born and dreams die 5 min at a time :)
events.description: OPEN MIC INFO… Is it free? Yes. It’s free and there are no food or drink minimums ever for open mic shows at Mad House Comedy Club. Is it free for a reason? Yes. It’s an open mic, so it’s literally “open” to anyone in the world that might want to go on stage including people who are quite insane. Is it a fun show? It depends on your pain tolerance. There are lots of comedians who are very funny practicing new material. There are new comedians who are terrible but they’re new, so they’re supposed to be awful and it’s kind of cute to watch them slowly die onstage. There are people who will make you cringe and decide it’s a great time to use the restroom and then there are comics who are so incredibly bad that it’s actually hilarious. The open mic is my favorite show. If you’re the type of person who doesn’t want to know how the sausage is made, then it’s probably not for you. If you’re the type of person who’d like to know the shocking truth of how stand up comedy really starts… this is your new home. Bonus… you get to hang out in an amazing bar with funny people. We have an open mic 7 nights a week.

events.description: A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition.

events.title: Legacy
events.description: Legacy is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards No maximum deck size Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used Allows cards from all legal sets unless otherwise banned for power level reasons. With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, and Forest), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age blockâ€”Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forestâ€”are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block. Legacy decks may consist of cards from allÂ MagicÂ card sets, plus the following cards: Sewers of Estark, Windseeker Centaur, and Nalathni Dragon (Mana Crypt would also be included were it not currently bannedâ€”see below). Cards from expansions and special sets (likeÂ From the Vault,Â Magic: The Gatheringâ€”Commander,Â Duel Decks,Â Conspiracy, etc.) are legal in the Legacy format on the date of release of the expansion or special set.

events.title: Mad House Open Mic! Free. Open to Anyone. Come watch stars be born and dreams die 5 min at a time :)
events.description: OPEN MIC INFO… Is it free? Yes. It’s free and there are no food or drink minimums ever for open mic shows at Mad House Comedy Club. Is it free for a reason? Yes. It’s an open mic, so it’s literally “open” to anyone in the world that might want to go on stage including people who are quite insane. Is it a fun show? It depends on your pain tolerance. There are lots of comedians who are very funny practicing new material. There are new comedians who are terrible but they’re new, so they’re supposed to be awful and it’s kind of cute to watch them slowly die onstage. There are people who will make you cringe and decide it’s a great time to use the restroom and then there are comics who are so incredibly bad that it’s actually hilarious. The open mic is my favorite show. If you’re the type of person who doesn’t want to know how the sausage is made, then it’s probably not for you. If you’re the type of person who’d like to know the shocking truth of how stand up comedy really starts… this is your new home. Bonus… you get to hang out in an amazing bar with funny people. We have an open mic 7 nights a week.

events.title: VibeCraft Power Hour
events.description: VibeCraft is a recurring, collaborative working session where ideas turn into experiments and experiments turn into real builds. Each session is shaped by the group. We review what is emerging, explore new directions, and decide together what is worth building next. This is not a presentation or a passive meetup. It is an active, hands-on environment where members contribute ideas, react to each other’s work, and influence the direction of future sessions. Expect open discussion, live demos, shared whiteboards, and practical exploration. Sessions may include live walkthroughs of tools, automations, and AI agents, discussions around product and company design, rapid demos from members, and breakdowns of different approaches to building with modern AI systems. Topics evolve over time, but the focus remains the same: learning by doing, building in public, and exploring a high-tech, high-touch future grounded in curiosity, collaboration, and thoughtful design. Come ready to participate, share what you are building, and help shape what we explore next as a VibeCraft community.

events.title: San Diego Tech Coffee Meetup
events.description: Del Mar Highlands Sky Deck The San Diego Tech Coffee is in its 14th year of being the largest weekly gathering of entrepreneurs and tech people in San Diego. Stop by for networking, sharing, brainstorming, mentoring, etc. And we love it when people want to share what they are working on! There is no agenda, which means you can’t be late – come when you can, leave when you need to. Very informal. New to the San Diego startup community? Or just want to be better connected? Check out the Startup San Diego “Startup Ecosystem Resource List” at https://startupsd.org/resources/.

location.address: 12841 El Camino Real suite 208, San Diego, CA

events.title: 1 Million Cups
events.description: 1 Million Cups is a weekly event for entrepreneurs, by entrepreneurs — built on the belief that conversations over coffee can accelerate innovation. Each week, two founders take the stage to pitch their business for six minutes, followed by 20 minutes of honest, constructive feedback from a room full of fellow entrepreneurs, business professionals, students, and curious minds. This isn’t a pitch competition — it’s a collaborative environment where ideas are refined, challenges are unpacked, and next steps become clearer. Attendees are encouraged to ask thoughtful questions, offer insights, and share resources that help presenters grow. Whether you’re launching your first startup, scaling your next venture, or just looking to get plugged into your local entrepreneurial ecosystem, 1 Million Cups is where you’ll find the energy, perspective, and community you need to move forward. Come for the coffee. Stay for the conversation. Leave with a better business.

events.title: Startup Quarters Curated Conversations
events.description: Startup Quarters is a weekly gathering held every Wednesday, discussing in breakout groups, Curated Conversations where founders and builders share ideas and work through challenges in real time. Designed to create momentum through consistent connection, it brings together multiple startup-focused interactions in one place, fostering ongoing mentorship, collaboration, and meaningful conversations that build week over week.

events.title: Business Networking Meeting
events.description: Join us on the first and third Wednesday of each month for a dynamic professional networking experience! Our group is dedicated to building strong business connections and fostering growth. Each meeting offers an opportunity to learn more about our members, exchange insights, and create valuable networking opportunities to help our businesses thrive. To enhance our networking experience, we plan to invite guest speakers and share upcoming events that members may find valuable. Additionally, twice a year, we will host an open event to welcome new members and expand our network. For our meeting location, group guidelines, and a list of industries we’re looking to welcome, visit: Gather & Grow Networking – Ansir Cowork

events.description: Gather your friends, select your legendary commander, and unleash powerful spells and creatures in epic multiplayer battles. Experience the joy of building unique and flavorful decks, creating unforgettable gaming moments, and forging lasting friendships. Whether you’re a seasoned player or new to the format, Casual Commander offers a welcoming environment where fun and creativity take center stage.

events.title: Let’s Cowork in PB!
events.description: Tired of working from your actual home, and want a change of scenery? New to San Diego and want to meet new people? Come join us at our weekly coworking session. It’s a pretty casual affair with new people every week, some regulars, and some just passing through. We take over a few tables at Second Nature in Pacific Beach and do a bit of work and a bit of getting to know each other. Come and go as you please, but do remember to bring your laptop (charged as plugs are few). It’s free to join, but ideally grab some coffee or lunch & tip the servers. A note about RSVPs: This group aims to help people meet in a small group. The max amount of people for this event is 12 people. We close the RSVP list on Mondays if there is a waitlist, and if you can’t make it we do kindly ask that you change your status as soon as you know to give someone else a chance to attend. Those on the waitlist will receive a note on the day of the meetup letting them know if there is space or not. If you sign up, but do not attend, you will be marked as a No Show. People with 5 No Shows will be removed from the group.

events.title: Carlsbad Coworking
events.description: We are all writers. Whether you’re writing copy for your next launch, tackling those client emails, drafting your newsletter, or finally updating your resume – bring your laptop and get it done. We meet every other week, 2:30-4:30pm at Wildland in Carlsbad. Structure: – 30 min: Intros & set your intention (what are you working on today?) – 60 min: Focused work time (no distractions, everyone’s heads down) – 30 min: Share progress, get feedback, ask questions This is for anyone who writes as part of their work: founders, marketers, consultants, creatives, job searchers. If you’re tired of procrastinating alone, come procrastinate with us (and then actually get stuff done). Details: This event is FREE. Please support Wildland by grabbing a coffee, tea, or treat while you’re there. RSVP to secure your spot. Refund policy Paid tickets are non-refundable and all sales are final.

events.title: Warhammer
events.description: Warhammer is a tabletop miniature wargame with a medieval fantasy theme where players use miniature models to represent a battlefield filled with buildings, trees, hills, and other terrain features.

events.title: Pokémon
events.description: The Pokémon Trading Card Game is a tabletop game that involves collecting, trading and playing with Pokémon themed playing cards. It has its own set of rules but uses many motifs and ideas derived from the Pokémon video games. There are Pokémon cards for every species & type of Pokémon as well as Trainer cards featuring characters, items and other themes of the franchise (each with a different use) and Energy cards to power various actions.

events.title: Pauper
events.description: In this Magic format, all cards must have been printed at common rarity in a Magic set or product. Common promo cards are only legal if the card meets that qualification. If a common version of a particular card was ever released in Magic, any version of that card is legal in this format. Other than that, the usual rules for constructed decks apply: Minimum of 60 cards in the main deck (There is no maximum size for main decks) Up to 15 cards in your sideboard, if used No more than 4 of any individual card in the main deck and sideboard combined (with the exception of basic lands)

events.title: Standard Showdown
events.description: The Standard format uses the most recently released Magic sets. Standard is a dynamic format where you build decks and play using cards in your collection from recently released Magic sets. Evolving gameplay and fresh strategies make it one of the most fun and popular ways to play Magic.

ABOUT US 

With 5M+ renters paying their rent 
with Bilt every month, we now represent 1 in 4 residential buildings 
nationwide. Paying rent through Bilt allows our residents to earn points

plus attend neighborhood experiences unlocked with their points. As

comedy scene closer to home while spotlighting the best local talent. 
We're getting our members off their couches and into the spots where

OUR PASSION FOR COMEDY EXPLAINED

isn't just entertainment—it's the heartbeat of neighborhood culture.

WANT TO LEARN MORE?

events.title: Workshop: Generating Your Unique Thought Leadership w/ Carly Chomer
events.description: This is a working session to define your unique thought leadership and generate topic ideas that focus on your audience, their commonly asked questions, and the topics that drive you forward. The session will also cover content best practices and frameworks you can use on an ongoing basis to build a consistent cadence of long-and short-form content. About Carly Chomer Carly has built a career on helping mission-driven organizations find and refine their voices. For over 20 years, she’s worked across the full spectrum of marketing and communications, including strategic planning, advertising, brand communications, advocacy and donor communications, and content marketing. She’s also worked with non-profits, for-profits, foundations and governmental organizations and with subject-matter experts in finance and economics to sustainability and public health. Regardless of the organization, everyone has a story to tell and a passion for the work they do, and she’s passionate about empowering and helping give voice to those creating greater good in the world. https://www.linkedin.com/in/carlychomer/

events.title: KIKI YEUNG & ESTHER KU: SWEET & SOUR CHICKS COMEDY TOUR
events.description:
Kiki Yeung is a comedy pioneer!” - NBCLA 

Kiki Yeung is a Hong 
Kong born LA-based comedian, actress, and producer. She’s the founder of
Crazy Woke Asians, the award-winning, nationally touring comedy show, 
and creator of the docu-series Kiki Funny Mama’s Night Out. Kiki’s been 
featured in Elizabeth Bank’s Whohaha, LA Times, The Seattle Times, NBC 
LA, CBS, and more. You’ve seen her on ABC’s The Parent Test and on stage
at the Netflix Is A Joke Fest, headliner at the New York Comedy 
Festival, Knockouts Women’s Comedy Festival and Hong Kong International 
Comedy Festival. Voted “funniest comedian” twice at Kenan Presents Vote 
Laugh Repeat at Jimmy Kimmel’s Comedy Club in Las Vegas. She was 
nominated for The Comedy Reviewers “Distinguished Comedian Award” in 
2026. Her debut one-hour special “Don’t Get Sick and Die” filmed in 
Seattle, is streaming now on YouTube. www.kikiyeung.com 

Esther
Ku is a Korean-American stand-up comedian born and raised in Chicago. 
Ku made her television debut as a contestant on NBC’s Last Comic 
Standing Season Six, advancing to the semi-finals in Vegas, then 
becoming one of the top ten finalists in Hollywood. She made her radio 
debut on the Howard Stern show as a finalist on The Hottest Funniest 
Chick Contest. She was a regular cast member on MTV’s Girl Code for 
three seasons. She's made guest appearances on MTV2’s Wild’n Out with 
Nick Cannon. She hosted the 2019 AVN Awards for Showtime. Ku played a 
receptionist in The Jack and Triumph Show sitcom alongside Jack 
MacBrayer, June Squibb, and Triumph the Insult Comic Dog, aka Robert 
Smigel on Adult Swim. She’s recorded several voices for Family Guy on 
Fox. She’s made cameos on The Jim Gaffigan Show,@Midnight, Boy Band, and
The Fiddling Horse.
