events.title: Art of Reading Book Club
events.description: Saturday, August 8 10:15 a.m.–noon SDMA Boardroom Be part of the Art of Reading Book Club, where art, literature, and art history converge. Join fellow Museum members in August to discuss Keeping an Eye Open: Essays on Art by Julian Barnes. This collection of essays by Booker Prize-winning author Julian Barnes explores the lives and work of major artists of the nineteenth and twentieth centuries. Featuring works like The Raft of the Medusa and artists such as Édouard Manet and Lucian Freud. Insightful, engaging, and deeply human, this collection is perfect for anyone who’s ever stood before a painting and wanted to understand what it was saying—and why it matters. Coffee and light snacks will be served. The book club will be followed by a docent-led tour of art works relating to the book. Please save your spot by Wednesday, August 5, by clicking here. Prior to entry all participants are required to check in at the SDMA rotunda admissions desk, after which you will meet in the Museum boardroom. Save My Spot Free for members | $25 for nonmembers Click here to purchase Keeping an Eye Open: Essays on Art by Julian Barnes from The Museum Store.

events.title: Pokémon
events.description: The Pokémon Trading Card Game is a tabletop game that involves collecting, trading and playing with Pokémon themed playing cards. It has its own set of rules but uses many motifs and ideas derived from the Pokémon video games. There are Pokémon cards for every species & type of Pokémon as well as Trainer cards featuring characters, items and other themes of the franchise (each with a different use) and Energy cards to power various actions.

events.title: CEDH – Bronze Event
events.description: TopDeck Championship Series – Bronze Event A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition. *Event May be downgraded to based on attendance*

events.title: A Space for All of Us
events.description: Sensory-Friendly Quiet Mornings at the Museum The Museum’s sensory-friendly quiet hours are held every Sunday from 10 a.m. to 11 a.m. This time is dedicated to creating a calmer, quieter museum experience. We remain open to the public during this time, and all are welcome to visit the Museum. During this time, specific exhibit features in the main Museum building will be turned off to minimize audio and visual stimulation. Noise-blocking headphones are free for check-out at the main Admissions desk and the entrance to the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibition, Exhibit features in the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibit will remain on, and all exhibits will be fully turned on at 11 a.m. Admission purchased during sensory-friendly quiet hours is the same as standard admission and applies to the entire day. Please contact publicprograms@museumofus.org with any questions.

events.title: Brunch at The Nat
events.description: Turn your museum visit into Sunday Funday with the addition of brunch at The Nat. Plan a Sunday visit that includes the full Nat experience, from bones to barnacles to brunch. After you’ve explored the galleries, head up to the roof to grab a bite and drink while soaking up the sunshine on our patio. When it comes to brunch, we mean business. That’s why we’re partnering with Johnny Rivera, local restaurateur known for starting the “Brunch Revolution” with Hash House a Go Go, Great Maple, and the viral BLT Bloody Mary. Brunch will be available 10 AM–2:30 PM on each date. Seating is open, casual, and first-come, first-served. Food and beverages may be purchased a la carte, similar to Nat at Night. The rooftop is popular, so there may be a wait at times. (Interested in reserved seating? Click here to view VIP experience options). Those looking for a different flavor of breakfast are in luck: head down to the Atrium for tacos, burritos, and bowls from The Craft Taco at The Nat. They also have the best coffee and matcha in Balboa Park, but we’re a little biased. Access to the rooftop brunch requires Museum admission. All purchases support our mission to conserve nature through science and discovery.

Join us at Portal Coffee overlooking the beautiful Waterfront Park and San Diego Harbor. There’s lots of available street parking along Kettner between Fir and Beech St just 1-3 blocks from Portal Coffee. See the Parking Map attached image. We’re also one block from the MTA Trolley and 2 blocks from Amtrak and the Coaster. Come by for some networking, sharing, brainstorming, and mentoring. events.title: Rockets Adventures
events.description: When: Every Monday at 6:00pm-9:00pm Entry Fee (Weekly): $5 Character Creation Fee: $5 How do you join? -Anyone can join! In order to join a player must pay a $5 Character creation fee or use a pre-made character. Players will be able to attend every week or whenever they feel like it. What is a Character Creation fee? -All characters for DnD at Rockets will have to be made through our DND Beyond account. Players will have access to bonus expansions this way. Characters will be kept on the TC’s Rockets account and be made in-store as well. How does it work? -There will be a set amount of Dungeon Masters provided by Rockets. Each DM will adventure with 5 players. All stories will be provided by Rockets along with in game accessories. What else is there? -Every week you’ll receive raffle tickets with your entry fee. Raffles will vary every week!

There’s no pitching, only networking, sharing, brainstorming, mentoring, etc. And we love it when people want to share what they are working on! There is no agenda, which means you can’t be late – come when you can, leave when you need to. Very informal. New to the San Diego startup community? Or just want to be better connected? events.title: Tuesday Team Up
events.description: The Group is growing and we are excited to announce the launch of Tuesday Team Up at Maya Moon Collective at 3349 Adams Ave, San Diego, CA 92116 at 9:00 am. This new chapter is more than just a gathering. It is a chance to expand our movement of empowerment, inspiration, and support into the heart of San Diego. Whether you cannot always make it to Feel Good Fridays in Encinitas or you are ready to double down on connection by joining both, Tuesday Team Up was created for you. It is a place where authentic conversations spark new opportunities, where encouragement turns into action, and where people come together to lift each other higher both personally and professionally. Our vision is to create spaces throughout San Diego where everyone feels they belong, where ideas become reality, and where community becomes the foundation for growth. Tuesday Team Up is another step toward that vision and we would love for you to be part of it.

events.title: The Group (North Park) – San Diego’s Best Non-Networking Networking Event
events.description: June An is the visionary founder of The Group, a community born from the triumphs and trials of his own entrepreneurial journey. As a seasoned entrepreneur, June understands the personal and professional challenges that can often feel overwhelming. His journey has taken him through moments of helplessness and hopelessness, which fueled his passion to create a space where others don’t have to navigate these challenges alone. Inspired by his experiences, June founded The Group with a clear mission: to empower individuals to overcome obstacles and pursue their dreams with renewed hope and determination. The Group is more than just a community; it’s a place where dreams are nurtured, resilience is strengthened, and people come together to achieve their full potential—both personally and professionally. Let The Group inspire you to live your dreams with purpose and confidence. https://www.thegroupcoastal.com/

events.title: Mission Valley Networking Luncheon
events.description: Why go it alone? It only takes one person to change our lives personally or professionally. Who will you meet at our next event? Exceptional Entrepreneurs – Southern California’s largest non-exclusive category business networking referrals organization – has partnered with Business Network Connection to serve the local San Diego community of business professionals, entrepreneurs and investors with regular opportunities to connect face-to-face with other generous professionals. Nearly every one of our events has new, first-time attendees looking to make brand new connections. # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # Space is limited. For guaranteed entry, please pre-register here: https://bncsd.eventbrite.com # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # ALL business professions are welcome to attend! First-time visitors should anticipate a positive and productive experience to increase business visibility among other supportive San Diego professionals. Networking Tip: Bring plenty of business cards with you to the event. # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # Space is limited. For guaranteed entry, please pre-register here: https://bncsd.eventbrite.com # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # Meeting Fee: FREE. Restaurant purchase expected. *** Complimentary Admission Available: for up to 35 attendees on Eventbrite ONLY. First-come, first served. Attendance no-show will risk the loss of privilege for complimentary admission in the future. Please cancel registration at least 1 day ahead of event if unavailable. *** # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # Space is limited. For guaranteed entry, please pre-register here: https://bncsd.eventbrite.com # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # # Luncheon Meeting Agenda: 11:30am – 12:00pm; Open Networking 12:00pm – 1:00pm; Introductions, featured speaker, + more! We end at 1:00pm sharp. — Everyone loves The Business Network Connection (“BNC”) where our Mission is connecting attendees with the right people expand one’s endeavors and influence! We have quality speakers at each meeting to help those who attend grow both personally and professionally. Exceptional Entrepreneurs was founded in November, 2008 by David & Gina Demangos. Since then, “EE” has become one of San Diego’s largest business networking organizations, due to the Demangos’ vision and leadership to provide “exceptional” opportunities for connecting local professionals. David Demangos is a eXp Real Estate agent in San Diego. For more information on David, please see: Awesome San Diego Real Estate: http://www.awesomesandiegorealestate.com “Effective networking is a learned skill. Exceptional Entrepreneurs are highly trusted professionals who share ideas and know that in order to receive you must first be willing to give.” – David Demangos

events.title: FilmNet’s Monthly Event
events.description: Happy New Year, everyone! Let’s march into 2026 with renewed energy and focus on our shared goals, to make this the year we really come together to make our dreams come true. This month the attention will be on the art of cinematography, on that special window into what our visions ought to look like on the screen. It is said that there are four directors: the screenwriter, the actual director, the cinematographer and the editor. Each brings their own skills and vision and each puts their own unique stamp on the final product. The January panel, moderated by producer/actor Anthony De La Cruz, includes Mike Logan of Spark Studios and Kraftwerks Productions, Jeff Arballo of Arballo Entertainment, Mauricio Navarro of Influence Films, and student filmmaker Daniel Hernandez. Panelists will discuss why they chose to be cinematographers as well as equipment they use and their current projects, followed by audience Q & A. We also have a SPECIAL ANNOUNCEMENT about a future FilmNet offering, so be sure to keep your antennae up and your ears unplugged! Back by popular demand . . . SUSHI and POKE BOWLS by celebrity Chef Mer! And as always, we’ll be enjoying a red carpet and photo stations c/o Tony Amat and our beloved partner R3 Productions, drinks for sale, a studio tour (get there at 4:30 pm!), more free headshots and lots of glitzy, campy, Hollywood-style fun. And of course, more exciting networking opportunities to give you the chance to make ALL the connections you really want to make. ********************************* Filmmakers Network San Diego’s Monthly Event is back!!! At long last we’re starting up monthly networking events again to support the local film community. It’s going to be even more hands-on, interactive and productive this time. Besides networking and hosting guest speakers, we will showcase short films and promo material, work on pitches and invite industry gatekeepers to hear them, put on fun games and contests as well as many more enriching activities. Networking includes supporting local producers, creators, actors, crew and film professionals in their effort to assemble teams and develop content and marketing opportunities, along with original FilmNet content and a documentary of our group, events, participants and their projects. Feel free to bring a guest or two! Below is the schedule for our first meeting: 4:30–6:30 / Studio tour, doors open for sign-up and seating 6:30–7:15 / Intros and success stories 7:15–8:15 / Cinematography panel, , Q & A 8:15–9:00 / Special announcement, networking Actors, feel free to bring headshots. Business cards or any promotional materials a plus. $5.00 admission at the event. Karl – 858-752-9985 (text)

events.title: CEDH
events.description: A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition.

events.title: Legacy
events.description: Legacy is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards No maximum deck size Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used Allows cards from all legal sets unless otherwise banned for power level reasons. With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, and Forest), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age blockâ€”Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forestâ€”are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block. Legacy decks may consist of cards from allÂ MagicÂ card sets, plus the following cards: Sewers of Estark, Windseeker Centaur, and Nalathni Dragon (Mana Crypt would also be included were it not currently bannedâ€”see below). Cards from expansions and special sets (likeÂ From the Vault,Â Magic: The Gatheringâ€”Commander,Â Duel Decks,Â Conspiracy, etc.) are legal in the Legacy format on the date of release of the expansion or special set.

events.title: VibeCraft Power Hour
events.description: VibeCraft is a recurring, collaborative working session where ideas turn into experiments and experiments turn into real builds. Each session is shaped by the group. We review what is emerging, explore new directions, and decide together what is worth building next. This is not a presentation or a passive meetup. It is an active, hands-on environment where members contribute ideas, react to each other’s work, and influence the direction of future sessions. Expect open discussion, live demos, shared whiteboards, and practical exploration. Sessions may include live walkthroughs of tools, automations, and AI agents, discussions around product and company design, rapid demos from members, and breakdowns of different approaches to building with modern AI systems. Topics evolve over time, but the focus remains the same: learning by doing, building in public, and exploring a high-tech, high-touch future grounded in curiosity, collaboration, and thoughtful design. Come ready to participate, share what you are building, and help shape what we explore next as a VibeCraft community.

There is no agenda, which means you can’t be late – come when you can, leave when you need to. Very informal. New to the San Diego startup community? Or just want to be better connected? events.title: 1 Million Cups
events.description: 1 Million Cups is a weekly event for entrepreneurs, by entrepreneurs — built on the belief that conversations over coffee can accelerate innovation. Each week, two founders take the stage to pitch their business for six minutes, followed by 20 minutes of honest, constructive feedback from a room full of fellow entrepreneurs, business professionals, students, and curious minds. This isn’t a pitch competition — it’s a collaborative environment where ideas are refined, challenges are unpacked, and next steps become clearer. Attendees are encouraged to ask thoughtful questions, offer insights, and share resources that help presenters grow. Whether you’re launching your first startup, scaling your next venture, or just looking to get plugged into your local entrepreneurial ecosystem, 1 Million Cups is where you’ll find the energy, perspective, and community you need to move forward. Come for the coffee. Stay for the conversation. Leave with a better business.

events.title: Coworking with some San Diego Digital Designers
events.description: Sometimes you just want to be surrounded by percolating design minds. At this gathering, bring your laptop and work on whatever’s on your plate that day — now surrounded by like minds. This is your chance to see Figma on the next screen over. To chat through a perplexing interaction design. To focus, inspired, on your portfolio. As with all SDDD events, this is a casual, personal, and quiet-ish meeting of designers. Introvert- and extrovert-friendly. Coworking, collaboration, and conversation, not networking. We’ve got wifi and power (duh), I’ll brew up a fresh batch of coffee, and we’ve got some tasty teas. What else do you need? (No, srsly, what else do you need?) Priority will be given to SDDD members (joining is easy — assuming you are a digital designer in San Diego — and free at sddd.org). Hit up the chat here in Meetup — or #events in Slack — with any questions. Limited capacity, so please RSVP accurately! 🙂 See you there!

events.title: Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain Discussion
events.description: Join us for an engaging discussion on the latest advancements in Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain technology. Our event will cover topics such as Machine Learning, Ethereum, Smart Contracts, and how these innovations are shaping the future of Decentralized Systems & Applications. Whether you are a tech enthusiast, developer, or industry professional, this event will offer valuable insights into the intersection of AI and Blockchain. In this meetup, we will dive deep into the potential of leveraging AI within Blockchain networks, the impact of Cryptocurrency on decentralized systems, and the evolution of Bitcoin in the digital landscape. From understanding the basics of Blockchain to exploring real-world applications of Smart Contracts, this event is designed to cater to individuals at all levels of expertise. Don’t miss this opportunity to network with like-minded individuals and gain valuable knowledge about the dynamic world of Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain.

events.title: Star Wars Legion
events.description: Whether you fight for the Galactic Empire or the Rebel Alliance, join us for Star Wars: Legion League battles of the Galactic Civil War! Your role is the commander of a unique army filled with troopers, powerful vehicles, and iconic characters like Darth Vader or Luke Skywalker. While innovative mechanics simulate the chaos of battle, the unpainted, easily assembled miniatures give you a canvas to create the Star Wars army you’ve always dreamed of leading into battle.

events.title: French Bio Beach Monthly Networking Happy Hour
events.description: Dear San Diego BIO enthusiasts !!!!!! Imagine a sunset-kissed setting where the waves of the ocean meet the waves of innovation. Our beachside Happy Hour is your chance to cultivate connections that could spark the next big breakthrough in your biotech endeavors. Whether you are French, not French, Francophile or not, loving the French or not but very fond of the BIO community, just end your week with a happy hour networking event (No Host Bar for now, but you may be lucky and get a free drink from a local biotech entrepreneur feeling generous or a sponsor if we happen to have scored one). What Awaits You: 🔬 Shared Insights: Engage in candid conversations with fellow biotech aficionados who have tasted both triumph and tribulation. Learn from their experiences, uncover strategies, and ignite your own journey with fresh perspectives. 🌐 Networking Nook: Unwind in our cozy corner for connection-building. Exchange ideas, form collaborations, and forge alliances that transcend the lab and venture onto the sands of progress. 📣 Trials & Triumphs: Gather ’round as industry experts reveal their paths paved with both successes and setbacks. You’ll discover that behind every achievement lies a tapestry of lessons that shape remarkable accomplishments. 🚀 Collective Lift: Be prepared to be part of an empowering community that champions each other’s growth. Our gathering is more than just an event—it’s a catalyst for your biotech aspirations. Come enjoy every month this nice lay-back Rendez-Vous with the French BioBeach community to meet new biotech professionals, network with the usuals, and just unwind with a beer or cocktail before the weekend starts….. Feel free to invite your Biotech friends & acquaintances. A bientot, The French Bio Beach Team

events.title: CEDH
events.description: A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition.

events.title: Modern
events.description: Modern is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards Cards from Core Set Eighth Edition and Mirrodin through today No maximum deck size, as long as you can shuffle your deck unassisted Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and Wastes), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age block Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forest are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block.

events.description: An anime style trading card game with a western game design.

events.title: Informal Meeting (AleSmith)
events.description: We’ll be meeting again on the second Thursday at Alemith starting at 7pm, on the second floor. There’s no formal agenda, just come by and tell us what you’ve been up to in the Linux/FOSS world. Alesmith website: https://alesmith.com/ Yelp page: https://www.yelp.com/biz/alesmith-brewing-company-san-diego-3 (They usually have food trucks at the entrance, but you can also bring your own food.)

events.title: San Diego Rust Meetup
events.description: San Diego Rust is back! Bigger, better, In-person-er. Our first two meetups back in person since the before-times went great, and we’re thinking about going steady! 🦀 🦀Join us for the ✨third✨ meeting in person at Ansir! Whether you’re brand new to Rust, a seasoned veteran of the borrow checker wars, or a dark wizard of HRTBs and MIR async function lowering – everyone is welcome. We’ll help you get started, share what’s new, and get connected to the San Diego Rust community. When: Second Thursday of every month from 7pm-9pm Where: Ansir Event Center on Convoy 7670 Opportunity Road, Suite #255 San Diego, CA 92111 Parking info here (park in the back row and scan the QR code once arrived for 3 hours of free parking. You will need to remember your license plate number) Agenda Greetings and topic suggestions What’s new in Rust “What are you working on or learning about in Rust?” roundtable Talks TBA General Q&A and open discussion Join the SD Rust Discord for questions, chat, and banter between meetups: 👉 https://discord.gg/YqAT4MzN7u

events.title: ARIES SPEARS
events.description:
Ever since New York native Aries Spears was 14 years old, he has been
a force to be reckoned with in the comedy scene throughout America. His
quick wit, charisma and ferociously aggressive style of comedy have 
earned him critical acclaim, high accolades, and above all, a busy 
schedule. From being a regular on Fox’s Mad TV, starring in feature 
films, appearing on a number of national talk shows, and continually 
touring the country where he continually sells out each and every city, 
Aries’ talents are widespread as he just passed the 3 million spins 
played on Pandora and his podcast is one of the "Musts" in the US. 

This
past summer, Aries guest starred in CNN’s “See It Loud” new summer 
series on the history of Black Television which was one of their hit 
docuseries of 2023, alongside Amanda Seales,Da’Vinchi, Debbie Allen, 
Deon Cole, Desus & Mero, Gabrielle Union, Jimmie Walker, Judge 
GregMathis, Kevin Frazier, Loni Love, Lynn Whitfield, Mo’Nique, Naturi 
Naughton, Omari Hardwick,Ray J, Ruben Studdard, Sherri Shepherd, Tatyana
Ali, Tiffany Pollard, Tisha Campbell, Vivica A. Fox and many more. 
Trailer: https://tinyurl.com/AriesCNNSeeItLoud 

As
a principal cast member on Fox’s hit sketch comedy show Mad TV, Aries 
brought a fresh, hip style to the already-edgy program from the third 
through the tenth season. The producers made full use of many of 
Aries’s talents by calling on him frequently to create new hilarious 
characters and write sketches where he thrived for eight seasons. 

Some
of the many recurring characters that Aries is famous for on the show 
include: Belma Buttons, Bill Cosby, Jesse Jackson, Mike Tyson, Walter 
(Crackheads), Reggie (Erascist), Dollar Bill Montgomery, Shaquille 
O'Neal, The Klumps, Michael Jackson, Sisqo, Evander Holyfield, El 
Diablo Negro and more. Aries also boasts a number of uproarious 
impersonations on the show that include James Brown, Al Pacino, and his
childhood idol Eddie Murphy. The Aries-branded sketch “Talkin’ 
American” was Mad TV’s most popular bit, keeping fans tuned in and 
boosting Mad’s ratings impressively on Saturday night. 

Aries has
also found great success in the world of feature films. At the age of 
17, after being spotted in a comedy club, Aries landed a part in the 
movie Home of Angels which eventually led to a starring role alongside 
John Leguizamo in TriStar’s The Pest followed by the notable role as 
Cuba Gooding, Jr.’s brother in Columbia’s blockbuster hit, Jerry 
Maguire.

Aries’ fierce momentum paved the way from features to tv 
and his eight seasons on Mad TV . Aries continues to spread his comedic
wings with the development of feature film projects -- in addition to 
TV -- which he is currently involved in writing. He is presently 
developing a feature project based on an original character that he 
created on Mad TV, as well as a thriller about a young black man 
immersed in hip-hop culture. Additionally, Aries has made appearances 
on many talk shows including: Loveline, Vibe, Keenen Ivory Wayans, Soul 
Train and Late Friday. He has also lent his voice to an animated 
character on C-Bear and Jamal and had a recurring stint on Fox’s The 
Best Damn Sports Show Period, where he created some of the show’s 
funniest characters during his short time there. 

Aries maintains a
strong connection to his African roots and is involved in many 
charitable events, including a number of organizations that help abused
women. He feels blessed to have been able to achieve everything that 
he has, but he in no way intends on stopping there. Given the extreme 
growing success rate of action comedies coming out of Hollywood, it’s 
safe to say that Aries Spears will be in demand for quite some time. 

No refunds or exchanges.
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest. 
Ages 21+

The Best Dueling Piano Show in San Diego When it comes to live music and nightlife in San Diego, The Shout! House is one of the Gaslamp Quarter’s most exciting destinations. Our renowned dueling piano show features two talented performers playing face-to-face grand pianos in a high-energy, request-driven performance that keeps the crowd entertained all night long. Expect sing-alongs, comedy, audience participation, staff dancing on stage, and music ranging from classic rock anthems to today’s biggest hits. No two shows are ever the same — the audience helps create the experience. Private Events & Group Parties Planning a birthday party, corporate event, bachelor or bachelorette celebration, or group night out in San Diego? The Shout! House is one of the most unique private event venues in the Gaslamp Quarter. Enjoy live entertainment, delicious food, craft cocktails, and a high-energy atmosphere your guests will never forget. Reserve your table today and Laugh, Sing & Shout the night away.

events.title: [ONLINE] EE Business Networking @Lunch (free!)
events.description: Why go it alone? It only takes one person to change our lives personally or professionally. Who will you meet today at “EE“? See additional info and direct links below… Exceptional Entrepreneurs is one of the USA’s largest business networking organizations – and one of the best values in business networking. At EE: There is no membership required. There is no attendance requirement. There is no business profession or category exclusivity. ALL business professions are welcome to attend! Meeting Agenda: First 30 minutes: check-in & breakout rooms open. Remaining 60 minutes: structured agenda with everyone in main room, including introductions by participants + more! While meetings end on time (for those who need to go), we also open breakout rooms again for those wanting to meet with other attendees afterwards (at least 30 minutes, usually longer.. fyi). Interested in being the featured speaker for this event? Information and registration is at: http://EEspeaker.com Info to join this EE business networking meeting: ####################################### http://nroi.us/eeonline (the shortlink above will remain updated with the current Zoom link) Passcode: 7772021 ####################################### Limited to the first 100 participants. We also cross-livestream through the EE FB Group. No participant limit on FB, however it’s view-only mode. Please feel free to leave your contact info in the comments of the livestream when listening along: https://www.facebook.com/groups/networkingeesd The meetings (including chat) will be recorded to the cloud – and made available temporarily afterwards. Information on recordings will be provided to attendees on Zoom and Facebook. Exceptional Entrepreneurs was founded November, 2008 in San Diego, California USA by David & Gina Demangos. Since then, “EE” has become one of the USA’s largest business networking organizations, due to the Demangos’ vision and leadership to provide “exceptional” opportunities for connecting local professionals. David Demangos is an eXp real estate agent in San Diego. For more information on David, please see: Awesome San Diego Real Estate: http://www.awesomesandiegorealestate.com “Effective networking is a learned skill. Exceptional Entrepreneurs are highly trusted professionals who share ideas and know that in order to receive you must first be willing to give.” – David Demangos Thank you for your continuing support of “EE”! See you at the next event.

events.title: Knot Together
events.description: Inspired by the traditional fishing nets and nautical knots featured in To Catch a Fish, our newest Together workshop series, Knot Together, explores the art of macramé. The series is led by Sitoë Thiam, a fiber artist and mechanical engineer who bridges technical precision with organic expression. As the founder of Unknot, a design studio specializing in knotting and weaving, she brings a unique interdisciplinary perspective to the world of fiber arts. Stone and Driftwood Wrapping with Knotting and Weaving In August, explore the quiet dialogue between organic form and the human hand by transforming foraged stones and driftwood into tactile works of art. Through the rhythmic practice of weaving with elemental hemp, you will trace the contours of each object with layered color and traditional knots. This meditative process elevates simple natural fragments into finished pieces of functional folk art The Together series at Mingei invites makers of all skill levels to come together and create in a supportive, low-pressure environment. Past iterations—Sew Together, Weave Together, Dye Together, Stitch Together, and Clay Together—explore a variety of craft techniques while emphasizing collaboration, experimentation, and hands-on learning. Each workshop encourages participants to connect with materials, try new skills, and enjoy the process of making together.

events.title: Nat at Night
events.description: On Fridays, the San Diego Natural History Museum is open until 11 PM just for you. Enjoy access to all open exhibitions and our rooftop deck for good views and great vibes. The rooftop is open to all visitors and serves beer, wine, non-alcoholic drinks, and food from Wolf in the Woods. Take a selfie with a megalodon shark, see scientists at work in our Paleo Center, admire glittering gems, get a close up look at spiders and snakes, and so much more. Best of all, get a look at our brand-new exhibition, Washed Ashore: Art to Save the Sea and grab a drink in the dive bar while chatting with a scientist. On select dates, channel your inner artist with Sketchparty, get competitive during trivia, or unwind while listening to the rooftop DJ. Hungry for more? The Craft Taco, located inside the Atrium, is a vibrant taco restaurant offering a delectable fusion of flavors and artisanal creations. They offer made-to-order tacos featuring signature blue corn tortillas, delectable burritos and bowls, and a variety of drinks and coffees. Admission is half-price after 5 PM, and parking is free after 6 PM. You can buy your tickets online in advance or buy them at the door the night of. As always, members of The Nat and Explorer pass holders enjoy free general admission. Fill your summer with wonder at The Nat. With weekly Nat at Night, brunches, weekend family programming, and so much more, membership just makes sense. Not to mention, you’re directly supporting nature conservation in our region. Join us today.

location.venue: San Diego Natural History Museum (The Nat)

and his eight seasons on Mad TV . Aries continues to spread his comedic
wings with the development of feature film projects -- in addition to 
TV -- which he is currently involved in writing. He is presently 
developing a feature project based on an original character that he 
created on Mad TV, as well as a thriller about a young black man 
immersed in hip-hop culture. Additionally, Aries has made appearances 
on many talk shows including: Loveline, Vibe, Keenen Ivory Wayans, Soul 
Train and Late Friday. He has also lent his voice to an animated 
character on C-Bear and Jamal and had a recurring stint on Fox’s The 
Best Damn Sports Show Period, where he created some of the show’s 
funniest characters during his short time there. 

Aries maintains a
strong connection to his African roots and is involved in many 
charitable events, including a number of organizations that help abused
women. He feels blessed to have been able to achieve everything that 
he has, but he in no way intends on stopping there. Given the extreme 
growing success rate of action comedies coming out of Hollywood, it’s 
safe to say that Aries Spears will be in demand for quite some time.

events.title: Fleet Accessibility Mornings
events.description: Early access to galleries at 9 a.m. On the third Saturday of every month, we open our doors one hour before the museum opens to the public and screen a documentary with house lights on and the soundtrack volume low. And if you want to stay and enjoy the museum all day? You can! Accessibility Morning features a special film screening of a documentary at 10 a.m. with house lights on and the soundtrack volume low. The Fleet Science Center strives to be an inclusive place where people of all needs and abilities are welcome and respectfully accommodated every day. Science is for everyone. Admission is free for one visitor and an accompanying guest. Regular admission rates apply for additional visitors. Noise-canceling headsets are available for use during your visit to the Fleet Science Center. Please inquire at the ticket counter or at the Heikoff Giant Dome Theater. For more information regarding accessibility at the Fleet Science Center, please visit our Accessibility page.

events.title: Online: Saturday Study Group
events.description: We are continuing our Study group online for the time being. We are now meeting online in the San Diego Python Discord server! Here is the link to join: https://discord.gg/8GpWuVvdbQ There is a “meetup” voice/video channel that we will be using! We may start meeting in person soon! This will be discussed at the meetup. We are considering having some online, some in-person. Decisions, decisions! Every Saturday we meet up with the larger Python community (including San Diego PyLadies (https://www.meetup.com/sd-pyladies), Open BioInformatics San Diego (https://www.meetup.com/Open-Bioinformatics-SanDiego/) and San Diego Artificial Intelligence (https://www.meetup.com/SanDiegoAI/)) to chat, learn, and hang out. Our study group is for everyone on their Python journey, whether you’re just starting out, an experienced pro, or anywhere in between. There’s no formal instruction, just a bunch of folks who like to code. Sometimes we talk about a specific subject, sometimes answer questions, whatever! Python Community Code of Conduct (http://www.pythonsd.org/coc): Python is much more than a software language. Python is a vibrant community made up of members from around the globe with a diverse set of skills, personalities, and experiences. It is through these differences that our community experiences great successes and continued growth. Overall, we’re good to each other. We contribute to this community not because we have to, but because we want to.

events.title: Pokémon
events.description: The Pokémon Trading Card Game is a tabletop game that involves collecting, trading and playing with Pokémon themed playing cards. It has its own set of rules but uses many motifs and ideas derived from the Pokémon video games. There are Pokémon cards for every species & type of Pokémon as well as Trainer cards featuring characters, items and other themes of the franchise (each with a different use) and Energy cards to power various actions.

events.title: CEDH – Bronze Event
events.description: TopDeck Championship Series – Bronze Event A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition. *Event May be downgraded to based on attendance*

events.title: Modern
events.description: Modern is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards Cards from Core Set Eighth Edition and Mirrodin through today No maximum deck size, as long as you can shuffle your deck unassisted Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and Wastes), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age block Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forest are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block.

events.title: Diving deeper into software development discussions
events.description: • What we’ll do Come join in discussions about Vim, Emacs, Linux, Elixir, Erlang, OTP, Lisp, Haskell, Ruby, Javascript, Functional Programming, Object Oriented Programming, Blockchain, HashGraph, Distributed Systems, Decentralized Systems, and whatever peaks your interest. • What to bring A laptop, good questions, exciting ideas, an open mind and a positive attitude. • Where to meet Meet by the corn hole game in the back and look for others with laptops. Ask for others that are there for the software discussion group. • Alternate location Some nights the Rabbit Hole has live music. I was not able to pin down an exact schedule, just sometimes we would show up and there would be a band but most times there was not. As an alternate location on live music nights the discussion group could be moved just down the street to Lestats coffee shop. They have great coffee and a nice atmosphere for hanging out and discussing software. • Important to know This is a self organizing open discussion group that is meant to be organic, with no main speaker or leader. It is just a time and a place for like minded people to come together to talk about software. • Background When I was staying in SD (Jan-Aug 2018) I was teaching software development at UCSD and the Meetup gave my cohort a space to dive further into topics that the curriculum did not allow time for. When the class was over and I went back to traveling full time, I thought it would be an interesting experiment to leave the Meetup going and see if people would still gather to discuss software without someone to lead it. I liked the idea of a group that did not require some one to always lead it. A lot off Meetups I have been to before were always one or two people doing a lot of work to organize and put on talks but everyone else just shows up to consume and doesn’t really participate that much. Kinda like going to a movie, in a way. The idea for this group is that it is what people make it. You get out of it what you put into it. It’s great that people are showing up still and I hope you have some great discussions. When I get back to San Diego (planning on a handful of times a year) and there is a meeting I will stop by and hang out and invite my cohort of previous students.

events.title: ARIES SPEARS
events.description:
Ever since New York native Aries Spears was 14 years old, he has been
a force to be reckoned with in the comedy scene throughout America. His
quick wit, charisma and ferociously aggressive style of comedy have 
earned him critical acclaim, high accolades, and above all, a busy 
schedule. From being a regular on Fox’s Mad TV, starring in feature 
films, appearing on a number of national talk shows, and continually 
touring the country where he continually sells out each and every city, 
Aries’ talents are widespread as he just passed the 3 million spins 
played on Pandora and his podcast is one of the "Musts" in the US. 

This
past summer, Aries guest starred in CNN’s “See It Loud” new summer 
series on the history of Black Television which was one of their hit 
docuseries of 2023, alongside Amanda Seales,Da’Vinchi, Debbie Allen, 
Deon Cole, Desus & Mero, Gabrielle Union, Jimmie Walker, Judge 
GregMathis, Kevin Frazier, Loni Love, Lynn Whitfield, Mo’Nique, Naturi 
Naughton, Omari Hardwick,Ray J, Ruben Studdard, Sherri Shepherd, Tatyana
Ali, Tiffany Pollard, Tisha Campbell, Vivica A. Fox and many more. 
Trailer: https://tinyurl.com/AriesCNNSeeItLoud 

As
a principal cast member on Fox’s hit sketch comedy show Mad TV, Aries 
brought a fresh, hip style to the already-edgy program from the third 
through the tenth season. The producers made full use of many of 
Aries’s talents by calling on him frequently to create new hilarious 
characters and write sketches where he thrived for eight seasons. 

Some
of the many recurring characters that Aries is famous for on the show 
include: Belma Buttons, Bill Cosby, Jesse Jackson, Mike Tyson, Walter 
(Crackheads), Reggie (Erascist), Dollar Bill Montgomery, Shaquille 
O'Neal, The Klumps, Michael Jackson, Sisqo, Evander Holyfield, El 
Diablo Negro and more. Aries also boasts a number of uproarious 
impersonations on the show that include James Brown, Al Pacino, and his
childhood idol Eddie Murphy. The Aries-branded sketch “Talkin’ 
American” was Mad TV’s most popular bit, keeping fans tuned in and 
boosting Mad’s ratings impressively on Saturday night. 

Aries has
also found great success in the world of feature films. At the age of 
17, after being spotted in a comedy club, Aries landed a part in the 
movie Home of Angels which eventually led to a starring role alongside 
John Leguizamo in TriStar’s The Pest followed by the notable role as 
Cuba Gooding, Jr.’s brother in Columbia’s blockbuster hit, Jerry 
Maguire.

Aries’ fierce momentum paved the way from features to tv 
and his eight seasons on Mad TV . Aries continues to spread his comedic
wings with the development of feature film projects -- in addition to 
TV -- which he is currently involved in writing. He is presently 
developing a feature project based on an original character that he 
created on Mad TV, as well as a thriller about a young black man 
immersed in hip-hop culture. Additionally, Aries has made appearances 
on many talk shows including: Loveline, Vibe, Keenen Ivory Wayans, Soul 
Train and Late Friday. He has also lent his voice to an animated 
character on C-Bear and Jamal and had a recurring stint on Fox’s The 
Best Damn Sports Show Period, where he created some of the show’s 
funniest characters during his short time there. 

Aries maintains a
strong connection to his African roots and is involved in many 
charitable events, including a number of organizations that help abused
women. He feels blessed to have been able to achieve everything that 
he has, but he in no way intends on stopping there. Given the extreme 
growing success rate of action comedies coming out of Hollywood, it’s 
safe to say that Aries Spears will be in demand for quite some time. 

No refunds or exchanges.
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest. 
Ages 21+

events.title: Benson Boone: Wanted Man Tour 2026
events.description: Benson Boone is bringing Wanted Man Tour 2026 to Pechanga Arena San Diego on August 15. Benson Boone continues to emerge as one of pop’s most compelling new voices, blending raw emotional delivery with polished, radio-ready production. Rising to prominence after gaining traction on platforms like TikTok and following a brief stint on American Idol, Boone quickly carved out his own lane with breakout hits such as “Ghost Town” and “In the Stars.” His music resonates with a younger audience through its vulnerability, often centered on themes of heartbreak, self-reflection, and personal growth. With a rapidly expanding global fanbase and consistent chart performance, Boone has transitioned from viral sensation to a legitimate force in the modern pop landscape. His dynamic vocal range and piano-driven sound have positioned him as an artist with both commercial appeal and long-term staying power, as he continues to release new music and tour internationally. Tickets on sale Friday, April 3, 2026 at 11 AM PT. For more information about Pre-Sale access, visit BensonBoone.com.

events.description: Join us for the 49th annual America’s Finest City Half Marathon & 5K! San Diego’s most scenic half marathon – run from the Cabrillo National Monument atop the tip of Point Loma, along San Diego Bay, Harbor Island, Embarcadero, past the Star of India, through downtown San Diego to a rousing finish in historic Balboa Park. The 5K delivers a fast, flat, and fun course through the heart of Balboa Park – perfect for runners of all levels.

events.title: Cars and Coffee
events.description: Cars and Coffee is where beautiful vehicles and their fabulous owners come together to show off their collection and to share in their mutual interest. Along with this, spectators usually join in for the sights and sounds. These car enthusiasts gather in the parking lot just outside the Museum for our monthly informal car meet, and we welcome up to 200 cars – all years, makes, models, colors, styles, and customizations. It’s completely free. We provide coffee and donuts. The Museum opens early at 8:00am with a special admission price of $10 before 10:00am.

events.title: Tianguis
events.description: We invite you to be part of the Tianguis de la Raza. Every Third Sunday of the month (except January and February) This artisan market’s mission is to create a space that connects local crafters and community members to promote an autonomous economy and community building. There are always arts and crafts, natural self-care products, home goods, and more. Plus, there will be fun activities for the entire family, including: Live Music and Children’s art workshops and more! Come out to connect, build community and support local art.

events.title: A Space for All of Us
events.description: Sensory-Friendly Quiet Mornings at the Museum The Museum’s sensory-friendly quiet hours are held every Sunday from 10 a.m. to 11 a.m. This time is dedicated to creating a calmer, quieter museum experience. We remain open to the public during this time, and all are welcome to visit the Museum. During this time, specific exhibit features in the main Museum building will be turned off to minimize audio and visual stimulation. Noise-blocking headphones are free for check-out at the main Admissions desk and the entrance to the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibition, Exhibit features in the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibit will remain on, and all exhibits will be fully turned on at 11 a.m. Admission purchased during sensory-friendly quiet hours is the same as standard admission and applies to the entire day. Please contact publicprograms@museumofus.org with any questions.

Whether you’re a seasoned player or new to the format, Casual Commander offers a welcoming environment where fun and creativity take center stage.

events.title: Rockets Adventures
events.description: When: Every Monday at 6:00pm-9:00pm Entry Fee (Weekly): $5 Character Creation Fee: $5 How do you join? -Anyone can join! In order to join a player must pay a $5 Character creation fee or use a pre-made character. Players will be able to attend every week or whenever they feel like it. What is a Character Creation fee? -All characters for DnD at Rockets will have to be made through our DND Beyond account. Players will have access to bonus expansions this way. Characters will be kept on the TC’s Rockets account and be made in-store as well. How does it work? -There will be a set amount of Dungeon Masters provided by Rockets. Each DM will adventure with 5 players. All stories will be provided by Rockets along with in game accessories. What else is there? -Every week you’ll receive raffle tickets with your entry fee. Raffles will vary every week!

events.title: Argentine Tango Class
events.description: All levels, all ages class taught by Eduardo Santana every Monday. There is a fee of $20 per class. If you would like to attend a class, wear shoes that allow for pivoting.

events.title: Scared to speak in front a group? We’ll help you!
events.description: A fun, supportive environment to practice your public speaking skills – for all levels of speakers! This is for YOU – who want to improve + polish their communication. This is for beginners who are looking for a comfortable place to start. And, this is also for seasoned speakers looking to tune up for a presentation. This is a recurring weekly meeting. https://www.meetup.com/palomar-airport-toastmasters/

events.title: Breakfast with some San Diego Digital Designers
events.description: Hello, digital designers! Let’s get together again and welcome new (and familiar) faces in and around the SD digital design scene. There are plenty of fancy design events, this is meant to be a more casual, personal, and intimate meeting of designers. Introvert- and extrovert-friendly. Conversation, not networking. Priority will be given to SDDD members (joining is easy — assuming you are in fact a digital designer in San Diego — and free at sddd.org). Again, this is totally casual. No agenda, no presentation. Everyone pays for their own meal, grabs a seat at the table, and brings their friendly faces. Let’s chat about trends, workplace issues, design tools and approaches, job opportunities, and digital culture. Hit up the chat here in Meetup — or in #events in Slack — with any questions at all. See you there!

events.title: San Diego Tech Coffee Online
events.description: The San Diego Tech Coffee Online is an informal gathering of people interested in startups, technology and/or business, modeled after the weekly in person San Diego Tech Coffee gatherings in San Diego, CA. This is an experiment to see if the related communities would value getting together online, sharing projects for which they need feedback and asking questions of other members. Join us online at – https://us02web.zoom.us/j/558502650?pwd=YjViRUZxNy9nV2daMDZkMlBiRW9zZz09 Or, Dial in via Phone: 253-215-8782 Meeting ID: 558 502 650 Passcode: 744238 In between our Tech Coffee, check out this list of resources for startup and tech people in San Diego: http://bit.ly/2HvvF7X-SanDiegoTechCommunity More Tech Coffee related information: www.techcoffeeonline.com Past Tech Coffee Online Notes – https://docs.google.com/document/d/1OckcmzWaXvGtW5YYe-lxFf5QBD2i8skGlbHi8LvscUM/edit# Tech Coffee On Discord (Virtual Coffee Shop for Networking, Jobs, Events) – https://discord.gg/PnRR5wC Tech Coffee on Slack (Discussion) – https://sandiegotechcoffee.slack.com/ Tech Coffee on Telegram (Earn Crypto!) – https://t.me/techcoffeeonline

events.title: San Diego Tech Coffee – Lestat’s-Normal Heights
events.description: The San Diego Tech Coffee is coming up on its 13th year of being the largest weekly gathering of entrepreneurs in San Diego. There’s no pitching, only networking, sharing, brainstorming, mentoring, etc. And we love it when people want to share what they are working on! There is no agenda, which means you can’t be late – come when you can, leave when you need to. Very informal. New to the San Diego startup community? Or just want to be better connected? Check out the Startup San Diego Startup Ecosystem Resources List – https://startupsd.org/resources/

events.title: Tuesday Team Up
events.description: The Group is growing and we are excited to announce the launch of Tuesday Team Up at Maya Moon Collective at 3349 Adams Ave, San Diego, CA 92116 at 9:00 am. This new chapter is more than just a gathering. It is a chance to expand our movement of empowerment, inspiration, and support into the heart of San Diego. Whether you cannot always make it to Feel Good Fridays in Encinitas or you are ready to double down on connection by joining both, Tuesday Team Up was created for you. It is a place where authentic conversations spark new opportunities, where encouragement turns into action, and where people come together to lift each other higher both personally and professionally. Our vision is to create spaces throughout San Diego where everyone feels they belong, where ideas become reality, and where community becomes the foundation for growth. Tuesday Team Up is another step toward that vision and we would love for you to be part of it.

events.title: The Group (North Park) – San Diego’s Best Non-Networking Networking Event
events.description: June An is the visionary founder of The Group, a community born from the triumphs and trials of his own entrepreneurial journey. As a seasoned entrepreneur, June understands the personal and professional challenges that can often feel overwhelming. His journey has taken him through moments of helplessness and hopelessness, which fueled his passion to create a space where others don’t have to navigate these challenges alone. Inspired by his experiences, June founded The Group with a clear mission: to empower individuals to overcome obstacles and pursue their dreams with renewed hope and determination. The Group is more than just a community; it’s a place where dreams are nurtured, resilience is strengthened, and people come together to achieve their full potential—both personally and professionally. Let The Group inspire you to live your dreams with purpose and confidence. https://www.thegroupcoastal.com/

events.title: Resident Free Tuesday
events.description: Tuesday 10:00 a.m.–5:00 p.m. The San Diego Museum of Art offers free admission to San Diego County residents (with ID) on the third Tuesday of every month. A valid ID is required for admission and certain restrictions apply as Resident Free Tuesdays tend to be busy. We ask that you not bring food, beverages, backpacks, or other oversized items. Plan for your visit and see our top tips for visiting the Museum.

location.venue: The San Diego Museum of Art

events.title: Community Open Studio
events.description: Monthly, Third Tuesdays 10:00 a.m.–noon Museum Art Studio Join us for an in-studio art making experience. Come explore art making in the SDMA Museum Art Studio! Join us and create work using mediums such as drawing, painting, and mixed media and explore your creativity. Materials will be provided, or you can bring your own. No oil paints or solvents please! This is a pay-what-you-wish event. Please RSVP here to save your spot. Registration is limited to 20 people per session. Meet at the House of Hospitality, near the Prado restaurant entrance, at 9:50 a.m. and look for the OPEN STUDIO sign. Artists under the age of 18 must be accompanied by an adult for the duration of the Community Open Studio. Museum admission not included.

events.description: San Diego Tech Coffee’s Tech Beer is held at the “Garten” of Deft Brewing every 3rd Tuesday of the month until October. Join the enthusiastic crowd of startup founders, tech entrepreneurs, crypto enthusiasts, developers and others involved in the San Diego startup community to socialize, network and enjoy each other and talk about what you’re doing at our monthly Tech Beer gathering. There will be great beer, mead, wine and wood fired pizza to enjoy along with the great people and conversations. So, there’s no excuse not to hoist a good beverage while enjoying great pizza with good company at this month’s SD Tech Beer! RSVP now while you’re here! RSVP: EventShip| Meetup

events.title: CEDH
events.description: A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition.

events.title: Legacy
events.description: Legacy is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards No maximum deck size Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used Allows cards from all legal sets unless otherwise banned for power level reasons. With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, and Forest), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age blockâ€”Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forestâ€”are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block. Legacy decks may consist of cards from allÂ MagicÂ card sets, plus the following cards: Sewers of Estark, Windseeker Centaur, and Nalathni Dragon (Mana Crypt would also be included were it not currently bannedâ€”see below). Cards from expansions and special sets (likeÂ From the Vault,Â Magic: The Gatheringâ€”Commander,Â Duel Decks,Â Conspiracy, etc.) are legal in the Legacy format on the date of release of the expansion or special set.

House in the Gaslamp Quarter delivers one of the most interactive live music experiences in downtown San Diego. Our high-energy dueling piano show features talented entertainers performing on grand pianos while the crowd sings along, requests songs, and becomes part of the show. Whether you’re planning a night out in the Gaslamp Quarter, celebrating a birthday, or simply looking for live music in San Diego on a Tuesday night, The Shout! House delivers a one-of-a-kind nightlife experience. PIANO PLAYERS: TBD TABLE RESERVATIONS Reservations available from 7:00pm – 7:45pm. (No reservations accepted after 7:45pm.) There is no reservation fee for guaranteed seating. Optional VIP upgrades are available for an additional fee. Guests included in reservations are not charged cover upon arrival. RESERVATIONS VIP PACKAGES WALK-INS FREE cover all night every Tuesday. FREE cover every night with college or military ID* *State-issued ID also required. PARKING $12 Flat-Rate Parking for 3 hours at Horton Plaza Parking Garage w/ validation. Regular rates apply after 3 hours. Ask your server for validation sticker. The Best Dueling Piano Show in San Diego When it comes to live music and nightlife in San Diego, The Shout! House is one of the Gaslamp Quarter’s most exciting destinations. Our famous dueling piano show features two talented performers playing face-to-face grand pianos in an electrifying, request-driven performance that keeps the energy high all night long. House is one of the most unique private event venues in the Gaslamp Quarter. Enjoy live entertainment, great food, craft cocktails, and a high-energy atmosphere your guests will never forget. Reserve your table today and Laugh, Sing & Shout the night away.

events.title: VibeCraft Power Hour
events.description: VibeCraft is a recurring, collaborative working session where ideas turn into experiments and experiments turn into real builds. Each session is shaped by the group. We review what is emerging, explore new directions, and decide together what is worth building next. This is not a presentation or a passive meetup. It is an active, hands-on environment where members contribute ideas, react to each other’s work, and influence the direction of future sessions. Expect open discussion, live demos, shared whiteboards, and practical exploration. Sessions may include live walkthroughs of tools, automations, and AI agents, discussions around product and company design, rapid demos from members, and breakdowns of different approaches to building with modern AI systems. Topics evolve over time, but the focus remains the same: learning by doing, building in public, and exploring a high-tech, high-touch future grounded in curiosity, collaboration, and thoughtful design. Come ready to participate, share what you are building, and help shape what we explore next as a VibeCraft community.

events.title: Applied Underwriters Invitational 25th Chamber Golf Classic
events.description: Each year, the Chamber Golf Classic brings San Diego’s business community together for one of the most anticipated days on the calendar. It’s a day of golf, connection, and friendly competition — all set on one of the region’s top courses. The day kicks off with an 8:00 a.m. four-person scramble, fueled by breakfast burritos, and wraps up with lunch and an awards ceremony. EVENT DETAILS Date: Wednesday, August 19, 2026 Time: Shotgun start beginning at 8:00 am Location: Maderas Golf Club, 17750 Old Coach Road, Poway, 92064 | Click for map/directions Parking: Parking is readily available at Maderas Contact: Monica Negrete | 619-544-1343 | mnegrete@sdchamber.org THANK YOU SPONSORS Numerous sponsorship opportunities are available for this event. Please contact Sherman Stocker at sstocker@sdchamber.org or (619) 544-1354 for more details. TITLE SPONSOR DONATE A RAFFLE PRIZE Gain instant visibility in front of leading businesses and their key decision-makers by donating a raffle prize for the tournament. In appreciation, your company will be listed as a contributor on the Chamber’s Golf Classic webpage, as well as recognized during the tournament awards reception. Our only request is that the prize be substantial enough to motivate our participants. We are unable to accept “2-for-1,” “percentage off,” or similar offers. We are looking for prizes that are exciting for our participants while showcasing your organization in a positive light. All donations or E-Gift Cards must be received by August 13th. Donate a prize: Please contact Monica at mnegrete@sdchamber.org. Items can be mailed to: Chamber Golf Classic San Diego Regional Chamber of Commerce 402 West Broadway, Suite 1000 San Diego, CA 92101

source.name: San Diego Regional Chamber of Commerce

events.title: San Diego Tech Coffee Meetup
events.description: Del Mar Highlands Sky Deck The San Diego Tech Coffee is in its 14th year of being the largest weekly gathering of entrepreneurs and tech people in San Diego. Stop by for networking, sharing, brainstorming, mentoring, etc. And we love it when people want to share what they are working on! There is no agenda, which means you can’t be late – come when you can, leave when you need to. Very informal. New to the San Diego startup community? Or just want to be better connected? Check out the Startup San Diego “Startup Ecosystem Resource List” at https://startupsd.org/resources/.

location.address: 12841 El Camino Real suite 208, San Diego, CA

events.title: 1 Million Cups
events.description: 1 Million Cups is a weekly event for entrepreneurs, by entrepreneurs — built on the belief that conversations over coffee can accelerate innovation. Each week, two founders take the stage to pitch their business for six minutes, followed by 20 minutes of honest, constructive feedback from a room full of fellow entrepreneurs, business professionals, students, and curious minds. This isn’t a pitch competition — it’s a collaborative environment where ideas are refined, challenges are unpacked, and next steps become clearer. Attendees are encouraged to ask thoughtful questions, offer insights, and share resources that help presenters grow. Whether you’re launching your first startup, scaling your next venture, or just looking to get plugged into your local entrepreneurial ecosystem, 1 Million Cups is where you’ll find the energy, perspective, and community you need to move forward. Come for the coffee. Stay for the conversation. Leave with a better business.

events.title: Startup Quarters Curated Conversations
events.description: Startup Quarters is a weekly gathering held every Wednesday, discussing in breakout groups, Curated Conversations where founders and builders share ideas and work through challenges in real time. Designed to create momentum through consistent connection, it brings together multiple startup-focused interactions in one place, fostering ongoing mentorship, collaboration, and meaningful conversations that build week over week.

events.title: Business Networking Meeting
events.description: Join us on the first and third Wednesday of each month for a dynamic professional networking experience! Our group is dedicated to building strong business connections and fostering growth. Each meeting offers an opportunity to learn more about our members, exchange insights, and create valuable networking opportunities to help our businesses thrive. To enhance our networking experience, we plan to invite guest speakers and share upcoming events that members may find valuable. Additionally, twice a year, we will host an open event to welcome new members and expand our network. For our meeting location, group guidelines, and a list of industries we’re looking to welcome, visit: Gather & Grow Networking – Ansir Cowork

Experience the joy of building unique and flavorful decks, creating unforgettable gaming moments, and forging lasting friendships. Whether you’re a seasoned player or new to the format, Casual Commander offers a welcoming environment where fun and creativity take center stage.

events.title: Let’s Cowork in PB!
events.description: Tired of working from your actual home, and want a change of scenery? New to San Diego and want to meet new people? Come join us at our weekly coworking session. It’s a pretty casual affair with new people every week, some regulars, and some just passing through. We take over a few tables at Second Nature in Pacific Beach and do a bit of work and a bit of getting to know each other. Come and go as you please, but do remember to bring your laptop (charged as plugs are few). It’s free to join, but ideally grab some coffee or lunch & tip the servers. A note about RSVPs: This group aims to help people meet in a small group. The max amount of people for this event is 12 people. We close the RSVP list on Mondays if there is a waitlist, and if you can’t make it we do kindly ask that you change your status as soon as you know to give someone else a chance to attend. Those on the waitlist will receive a note on the day of the meetup letting them know if there is space or not. If you sign up, but do not attend, you will be marked as a No Show. People with 5 No Shows will be removed from the group.

events.title: Carlsbad Coworking
events.description: We are all writers. Whether you’re writing copy for your next launch, tackling those client emails, drafting your newsletter, or finally updating your resume – bring your laptop and get it done. We meet every other week, 2:30-4:30pm at Wildland in Carlsbad. Structure: – 30 min: Intros & set your intention (what are you working on today?) – 60 min: Focused work time (no distractions, everyone’s heads down) – 30 min: Share progress, get feedback, ask questions This is for anyone who writes as part of their work: founders, marketers, consultants, creatives, job searchers. If you’re tired of procrastinating alone, come procrastinate with us (and then actually get stuff done). Details: This event is FREE. Please support Wildland by grabbing a coffee, tea, or treat while you’re there. RSVP to secure your spot. Refund policy Paid tickets are non-refundable and all sales are final.

events.title: Warhammer
events.description: Warhammer is a tabletop miniature wargame with a medieval fantasy theme where players use miniature models to represent a battlefield filled with buildings, trees, hills, and other terrain features.

events.title: Pokémon
events.description: The Pokémon Trading Card Game is a tabletop game that involves collecting, trading and playing with Pokémon themed playing cards. It has its own set of rules but uses many motifs and ideas derived from the Pokémon video games. There are Pokémon cards for every species & type of Pokémon as well as Trainer cards featuring characters, items and other themes of the franchise (each with a different use) and Energy cards to power various actions.

events.title: Pauper
events.description: In this Magic format, all cards must have been printed at common rarity in a Magic set or product. Common promo cards are only legal if the card meets that qualification. If a common version of a particular card was ever released in Magic, any version of that card is legal in this format. Other than that, the usual rules for constructed decks apply: Minimum of 60 cards in the main deck (There is no maximum size for main decks) Up to 15 cards in your sideboard, if used No more than 4 of any individual card in the main deck and sideboard combined (with the exception of basic lands)

events.title: Standard Showdown
events.description: The Standard format uses the most recently released Magic sets. Standard is a dynamic format where you build decks and play using cards in your collection from recently released Magic sets. Evolving gameplay and fresh strategies make it one of the most fun and popular ways to play Magic.

events.title: San Diego Arduino Enthusiasts
events.description: Please join us at our monthly meeting. 7pm on the third Wednesday. The Escape Room Steal & Escape graciously allow us to use their space. Please enter through the door under the “A | ROYALE WEAR” sign. Lat/Lon 32°39’35.6″N 117°06’09.1″W Google Maps: https://maps.app.goo.gl/zGrMUfKtBgo1rtnc8 Tonight — We will continue with the development of the low-power PCB arduino project: demonstrate programming the WeAct STM32G431CB boards and as time allows: 2) drive the OLED display, 3) Explore low power modes of the STM32G431CB Bring your WeAct dev kit! What we’re about The purpose of this groups is to share ideas, experiences, projects, and code for Arduino-based electronics.

events.title: COLUM TYRRELL
events.description:
After beginning his career in his home country of Ireland, Colum Tyrrell moved to New York City and quickly became one of the fastest rising comics in the scene. He is a regular at The Comedy Cellar, The Stand Comedy Club and Stand Up New York. A staple in the New York Comedy Podcast scene he can be heard on The Legion of Skanks, You Know What Dude, Are You Garbage? and Matt and Shane's Secret Podcast. He launched The Colum Tyrrell Podcast in 2022 which is growing to be one of the more popular shows in New York and he recently made his television debut on The Tonight Show with Jimmy Fallon. Catch him now before he blows up and becomes a household name.

Ages 21+

events.title: Open Web Application Security Project San Diego (OWASP-SD) – Monthly Meeting
events.description: What we’re about The Open Web Application Security Project (OWASP) is a 501(c)(3) worldwide not-for-profit charitable organization focused on improving the security of software. Our mission is to make software security visible, so that individuals and organizations worldwide can make informed decisions about true software security risks. Over 30,000 Subscribers – Over 140 Projects – Over 190 Chapters ———————————– If you wish to present at OWASP San Diego, you can apply here: https://goo.gl/forms/eCbZU8Kn3mxIjxMt1 If you wish to apply to our Slack channel, you can apply here: https://goo.gl/forms/mNwzJZKs47iK5usG2

events.title: CEDH
events.description: A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition.

events.title: Modern
events.description: Modern is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards Cards from Core Set Eighth Edition and Mirrodin through today No maximum deck size, as long as you can shuffle your deck unassisted Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and Wastes), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age block Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forest are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block.
