events.title: Dueling Pianos San Diego Thursday Night | Live Music at The Shout! House
events.description: THURSDAY NIGHT – Doors 7pm | Pianos 8pm | 21+ Looking for the best dueling pianos show in San Diego on Thursday night? Every Thursday, The Shout! House in the Gaslamp Quarter delivers one of the most interactive live music experiences in downtown San Diego. Our high-energy dueling piano show features talented entertainers performing on grand pianos while the audience sings along, requests songs, and becomes part of the show. Whether you’re planning a night out in the Gaslamp Quarter, celebrating a birthday, or searching for live music in San Diego on Thursday night, The Shout! House offers a one-of-a-kind nightlife experience filled with music, laughter, and unforgettable moments. SHOUT ENTERTAINERS: TBD TABLE RESERVATIONS Reservations available from 7:00pm – 7:45pm. No reservations accepted after 7:45pm. Reservation fee: $5 per guest for guaranteed seating. Guests included in reservations are not charged cover upon arrival. RESERVATIONS VIP PACKAGES WALK-INS Welcome! FREE cover every night with college or military ID* *State-issued ID also required. PARKING $12 Flat-Rate Parking for 3 hours at Horton Plaza Parking Garage w/ validation. Regular rates apply after 3 hours. Ask your server for validation sticker. The Best Dueling Piano Show in San Diego When it comes to live music and nightlife in San Diego, The Shout! House is one of the Gaslamp Quarter’s most exciting destinations. Our renowned dueling piano show features two talented performers playing face-to-face grand pianos in a high-energy, request-driven performance that keeps the crowd entertained all night long. Expect sing-alongs, comedy, audience participation, staff dancing on stage, and music ranging from classic rock anthems to today’s biggest hits. No two shows are ever the same — the audience helps create the experience. Private Events & Group Parties Planning a birthday party, corporate event, bachelor or bachelorette celebration, or group night out in San Diego? The Shout! House is one of the most unique private event venues in the Gaslamp Quarter. Enjoy live entertainment, delicious food, craft cocktails, and a high-energy atmosphere your guests will never forget. Reserve your table today and Laugh, Sing & Shout the night away.

events.title: [ONLINE] EE Business Networking @Lunch (free!)
events.description: Why go it alone? It only takes one person to change our lives personally or professionally. Who will you meet today at “EE“? See additional info and direct links below… Exceptional Entrepreneurs is one of the USA’s largest business networking organizations – and one of the best values in business networking. At EE: There is no membership required. There is no attendance requirement. There is no business profession or category exclusivity. ALL business professions are welcome to attend! Meeting Agenda: First 30 minutes: check-in & breakout rooms open. Remaining 60 minutes: structured agenda with everyone in main room, including introductions by participants + more! While meetings end on time (for those who need to go), we also open breakout rooms again for those wanting to meet with other attendees afterwards (at least 30 minutes, usually longer.. fyi). Interested in being the featured speaker for this event? Information and registration is at: http://EEspeaker.com Info to join this EE business networking meeting: ####################################### http://nroi.us/eeonline (the shortlink above will remain updated with the current Zoom link) Passcode: 7772021 ####################################### Limited to the first 100 participants. We also cross-livestream through the EE FB Group. No participant limit on FB, however it’s view-only mode. Please feel free to leave your contact info in the comments of the livestream when listening along: https://www.facebook.com/groups/networkingeesd The meetings (including chat) will be recorded to the cloud – and made available temporarily afterwards. Information on recordings will be provided to attendees on Zoom and Facebook. Exceptional Entrepreneurs was founded November, 2008 in San Diego, California USA by David & Gina Demangos. Since then, “EE” has become one of the USA’s largest business networking organizations, due to the Demangos’ vision and leadership to provide “exceptional” opportunities for connecting local professionals. David Demangos is an eXp real estate agent in San Diego. For more information on David, please see: Awesome San Diego Real Estate: http://www.awesomesandiegorealestate.com “Effective networking is a learned skill. Exceptional Entrepreneurs are highly trusted professionals who share ideas and know that in order to receive you must first be willing to give.” – David Demangos Thank you for your continuing support of “EE”! See you at the next event.

events.title: Casual Commander – (12pm » 6pm)
events.description: Gather your friends, select your legendary commander, and unleash powerful spells and creatures in epic multiplayer battles. Experience the joy of building unique and flavorful decks, creating unforgettable gaming moments, and forging lasting friendships. Whether you’re a seasoned player or new to the format, Casual Commander offers a welcoming environment where fun and creativity take center stage.

events.title: Nat at Night
events.description: On Fridays, the San Diego Natural History Museum is open until 11 PM just for you. Enjoy access to all open exhibitions and our rooftop deck for good views and great vibes. The rooftop is open to all visitors and serves beer, wine, non-alcoholic drinks, and food from Wolf in the Woods. Take a selfie with a megalodon shark, see scientists at work in our Paleo Center, admire glittering gems, get a close up look at spiders and snakes, and so much more. Best of all, get a look at our brand-new exhibition, Washed Ashore: Art to Save the Sea and grab a drink in the dive bar while chatting with a scientist. On select dates, channel your inner artist with Sketchparty, get competitive during trivia, or unwind while listening to the rooftop DJ. Hungry for more? The Craft Taco, located inside the Atrium, is a vibrant taco restaurant offering a delectable fusion of flavors and artisanal creations. They offer made-to-order tacos featuring signature blue corn tortillas, delectable burritos and bowls, and a variety of drinks and coffees. Admission is half-price after 5 PM, and parking is free after 6 PM. You can buy your tickets online in advance or buy them at the door the night of. As always, members of The Nat and Explorer pass holders enjoy free general admission. Fill your summer with wonder at The Nat. With weekly Nat at Night, brunches, weekend family programming, and so much more, membership just makes sense. Not to mention, you’re directly supporting nature conservation in our region. Join us today.

events.title: Online: Saturday Study Group
events.description: We are continuing our Study group online for the time being. We are now meeting online in the San Diego Python Discord server! Here is the link to join: https://discord.gg/8GpWuVvdbQ There is a “meetup” voice/video channel that we will be using! We may start meeting in person soon! This will be discussed at the meetup. We are considering having some online, some in-person. Decisions, decisions! Every Saturday we meet up with the larger Python community (including San Diego PyLadies (https://www.meetup.com/sd-pyladies), Open BioInformatics San Diego (https://www.meetup.com/Open-Bioinformatics-SanDiego/) and San Diego Artificial Intelligence (https://www.meetup.com/SanDiegoAI/)) to chat, learn, and hang out. Our study group is for everyone on their Python journey, whether you’re just starting out, an experienced pro, or anywhere in between. There’s no formal instruction, just a bunch of folks who like to code. Sometimes we talk about a specific subject, sometimes answer questions, whatever! Python Community Code of Conduct (http://www.pythonsd.org/coc): Python is much more than a software language. Python is a vibrant community made up of members from around the globe with a diverse set of skills, personalities, and experiences. It is through these differences that our community experiences great successes and continued growth. Overall, we’re good to each other. We contribute to this community not because we have to, but because we want to.

events.title: REMOTE: Pyladies/San Diego PUG Co-Study Group
events.description: We are continuing our Study group online for the time being. We are now meeting online in the San Diego Python Discord server! Here is the link to join: https://discord.gg/8GpWuVvdbQ There is a “meetup” voice/video channel that we will be using! The SD Python User Group Meetup is https://www.meetup.com/pythonsd/ – there is a Saturday Study group hosted by the SD Python Meetup group. Every Saturday we meet up with the larger Python community to chat, learn, and hang out. Our study group is for everyone on their Python journey, whether you’re just starting out, you’re an experienced pro, or anywhere in between. There’s no formal instruction, just a bunch of folks who like to code. We have a great time–come join us! Free feel to show up at any time and stay as long as you want. Hope to see you there! Python Community Code of Conduct (https://www.python.org/psf/codeofconduct/): Python is much more than a software language. Python is a vibrant community made up of members from around the globe with a diverse set of skills, personalities, and experiences. It is through these differences that our community experiences great successes and continued growth. Overall, we’re good to each other. We contribute to this community not because we have to, but because we want to.

events.title: In Person – San Diego 3D Printing Monthly Meetup
events.description: Hi All, This is our in-person meeting on the 4th saturday of the month.(2nd Saturday will continue to be online) We will meet at SD Colab 3.0 makerspace(on Mission Gorge Place). Members from all background’s come by and talk about how 3D printing has changed their lives for the better. For myself I like to make artistic objects with some functionality, others are building custom printers and exploring custom wearables. Sometimes people will bring their printers to share. Anything is possible so let’s chat! Do you have a printer? What do you want to make? Just curious and want to chat with similar interests? Stop in and get your Saturday morning motivation amped up with a cup of coffee, and make something! Disclaimer: We take photos during the event of people and equipment to share in our community. Join our discord to continue the chat. http://discord.SanDiego3DPrinting.com

events.title: Hack and Race-Prep Autonomous Cars
events.description: San Diego Robotics DIYRobocars Race-Prep Day, Hosted by UCSD. Please keep in mind that at the Saturdays meeting we expect you to have your robocar built and software installed. I hope you will understand that for us time at the track is limited to ever other week for 2 hours. We would like to use it for development as much as feasible. Please complete your robot build before coming to the Saturdays meetup. See the 2nd Wednesday of month Beginner/Builder Meetup and our slack channel. Then bring your bot and computer to the track. Please sign-up and come for our monthly Beginners and Builders Meetup before coming to the Saturdays Meetup. Agenda: 10 :00 am – 1200 pm. Training and racing We have built a double lane track with a yellow dashed center-line. Expect a track roughly 30×15 feet, with a concrete floor and duct tape lines. Don’t worry if you are still working things out with the software. We will be on hand to help all get going. This is a great chance to gather training data for you robot. Still figuring out hardware and/or configuring the software on the bot and laptop? Go to one of the 2nd Wed of month Beginner/Builder Meetups. There will be tables, power, and wifi. Please bring your laptops and bot and be ready to hack. A special thanks to UCSD for hosting us. Thank you. SD DIYRobocars. *** Checkout http://www.donkeycar.com for the build information that many of us used to get started. Checkout http://docs.donkeycar.com for parts lists, hardware and software setup instructions. Self-steering car from SD group: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=nxhsv5CViBg more sample driving from San Diego meetup: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=WecNKymdg3k some sample driving from Oakland DIYRobocars meetup: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sQn5PA6nl3I

Other members only have time to create when they have a spare moment in their busy lives. We all have one thing in common; our artists are visionaries, driven to awaken within a world seldom experienced, through the expertise of their gentile hands.

events.title: Natsu Nights
events.description: A Summer Night Market Series Set inside the Japanese Friendship Garden & Museum, this night market series transforms the garden into a different atmosphere. Explore layered landscapes as you move through the space after dark, and let the garden become a welcoming place where you can gather, linger, and spend time outside the usual routine. Wander winding pathways, sip on boba, and explore a rotating mix of themed vendors and activities that change throughout the series. Whether you’re here to romanticize your evenings, unwind from work, or hang out with friends, Natsu Nights is a summer must. The Garden will have extended hours in the upper garden on select Saturdays from June through August, so you can experience the outdoors in perfect summer weather. Discounted admission is $5 from 5pm–8pm (upper garden only), with vendors on site from 12pm–8pm. June 27 | Kawaii Trinketcore Night July 25 | Crafty Cuties Market August 22 | Enchanted Curio Market

events.title: Pokémon
events.description: The Pokémon Trading Card Game is a tabletop game that involves collecting, trading and playing with Pokémon themed playing cards. It has its own set of rules but uses many motifs and ideas derived from the Pokémon video games. There are Pokémon cards for every species & type of Pokémon as well as Trainer cards featuring characters, items and other themes of the franchise (each with a different use) and Energy cards to power various actions.

The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition. *Event May be downgraded to based on attendance*

events.title: Modern
events.description: Modern is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards Cards from Core Set Eighth Edition and Mirrodin through today No maximum deck size, as long as you can shuffle your deck unassisted Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and Wastes), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age block Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forest are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block.

events.title: Dueling Pianos San Diego Saturday Night | Live Music at The Shout! House
events.description: SATURDAY NIGHT – Doors 6pm | Pianos 6:30pm | 21+ Looking for the best dueling pianos show in San Diego on Saturday night? Every Saturday, The Shout! House in the Gaslamp Quarter delivers one of the most exciting live music experiences in downtown San Diego. Our high-energy dueling piano show features talented entertainers performing on grand pianos while the audience sings along, requests songs, and becomes part of the show. Whether you’re celebrating a birthday, planning a night out with friends, or searching for live music in San Diego on Saturday night, The Shout! House offers a one-of-a-kind nightlife experience filled with music, comedy, sing-alongs, and unforgettable energy. SHOUT ENTERTAINERS: TBD TABLE RESERVATIONS Reservations available from 6:00pm – 6:30pm. (No reservations accepted after 6:30pm.) Reservation fee: $20 per guest for guaranteed seating. Guests included in reservations are not charged cover upon arrival. RESERVATIONS VIP PACKAGES WALK-INS Walk-ins are always welcome. FREE cover every night with college or military ID* *State-issued ID also required. The Best Dueling Piano Show in San Diego When it comes to live music and nightlife in San Diego, The Shout! House is one of the Gaslamp Quarter’s most exciting destinations. Our famous dueling piano show features two talented performers playing face-to-face grand pianos in a high-energy, request-driven performance that keeps the crowd entertained all night long. Expect sing-alongs, comedy, audience participation, staff dancing on stage, and music ranging from classic rock anthems to today’s biggest hits. No two shows are ever the same — the audience helps create the experience. Private Events & Group Parties Planning a birthday party, corporate event, bachelor or bachelorette celebration, or group night out in San Diego? The Shout! House is one of the most unique private event venues in the Gaslamp Quarter. Enjoy live entertainment, great food, craft cocktails, and a high-energy atmosphere your guests will never forget. Reserve your table today and Laugh, Sing & Shout the night away.

events.title: CHRIS O'CONNOR
events.description:
Chris O'Connor is a Philadelphia native comic now living in Austin who is a co-creator, writer, and star for the hit Netflix series Tires. Chris wrote and acted for the sketch series Gilly and Keeves. Furthermore, he also starred in a pair of viral videos that garnered more than 4 million views ("Samesies" and "Man hears for the first time”). In addition, he is one half of the podcast Stuff Island. His work has been featured on Comedy Central, SiriusXM, TeamCoco, Funny or Die, Buzzfeed, iTunes, Spotify, Huffington Post, and Barstool Sports.

Ages 21+
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest.
Doors for open for seating approximately 30 minutes before late showtimes. Please note, doors are subject to prior show endtime and may change without notice, beyond our control.
No refunds or exchanges.

events.title: BIG TOMATO COMEDY FESTIVAL
events.description:
Featuring the Best Emerging Comedians of the Festival!

The Big Tomato Comedy Festival Showcase highlights the top-performing, highest-rated comedians from this year’s fest; hand-selected by industry professionals for their originality, stage presence, and standout material.

Hosted at Mic Drop Comedy San Diego in collaboration with Big Pine Comedy Festival, these showcases bring audiences an unforgettable night of comedy featuring tomorrow’s headliners, today.

Each performer you’ll see has been evaluated by club owners, bookers, producers, and managers throughout the festival. These are the comics who rose to the top through exceptional writing, delivery, and connection with the room.

What to Expect

✔️ A curated lineup of the festival’s strongest comedians
✔️ Fresh, original material refined through professional feedback
✔️ High-energy performances in an intimate showroom setting
✔️ A chance to discover rising talent before they break nationally

This is where future touring comics, club regulars, and breakout performers take the stage.

To Submit to Big Tomato Comedy Festival Visit:  https://www.bigpinecomedyfesti...

events.title: A Space for All of Us
events.description: Sensory-Friendly Quiet Mornings at the Museum The Museum’s sensory-friendly quiet hours are held every Sunday from 10 a.m. to 11 a.m. This time is dedicated to creating a calmer, quieter museum experience. We remain open to the public during this time, and all are welcome to visit the Museum. During this time, specific exhibit features in the main Museum building will be turned off to minimize audio and visual stimulation. Noise-blocking headphones are free for check-out at the main Admissions desk and the entrance to the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibition, Exhibit features in the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibit will remain on, and all exhibits will be fully turned on at 11 a.m. Admission purchased during sensory-friendly quiet hours is the same as standard admission and applies to the entire day. Please contact publicprograms@museumofus.org with any questions.

location.address: 1350 El Prado, Balboa Park, San Diego, CA

events.title: Art Mart
events.description: SDCAMA art shows display the living art of today; you can hold it in your hands and take it into your life. Many of our artist members are full time professionals that have been plying their trade for decades, have won competitions throughout the U.S., and are world renowned. Other members only have time to create when they have a spare moment in their busy lives. We all have one thing in common; our artists are visionaries, driven to awaken within a world seldom experienced, through the expertise of their gentile hands.

events.title: Little Italy San Diego Tech Coffee
events.description: We’re bringing back Tech Coffee to the greater downtown area after a few year hiatus. What better spot for Tech Coffee than Little Italy, the coffee capital of San Diego! Join us at Portal Coffee overlooking the beautiful Waterfront Park and San Diego Harbor. There’s lots of available street parking along Kettner between Fir and Beech St just 1-3 blocks from Portal Coffee. See the Parking Map attached image. We’re also one block from the MTA Trolley and 2 blocks from Amtrak and the Coaster. Come by for some networking, sharing, brainstorming, and mentoring. And we love it when people want to share what they are working on! There is no agenda, which means you can’t be late – come when you can, leave when you need to. Very informal. New to the San Diego startup community? Or just want to be better connected? Check out the SD Tech Scene calendar – https://sdtechscene.org

events.title: Rockets Adventures
events.description: When: Every Monday at 6:00pm-9:00pm Entry Fee (Weekly): $5 Character Creation Fee: $5 How do you join? -Anyone can join! In order to join a player must pay a $5 Character creation fee or use a pre-made character. Players will be able to attend every week or whenever they feel like it. What is a Character Creation fee? -All characters for DnD at Rockets will have to be made through our DND Beyond account. Players will have access to bonus expansions this way. Characters will be kept on the TC’s Rockets account and be made in-store as well. How does it work? -There will be a set amount of Dungeon Masters provided by Rockets. Each DM will adventure with 5 players. All stories will be provided by Rockets along with in game accessories. What else is there? -Every week you’ll receive raffle tickets with your entry fee. Raffles will vary every week!

events.title: AI Insights Roundtable
events.description: Twitter: https://twitter.com/hashtag/AIIR 07:00 pm to 08:15 pm Usually, the 4th Monday of the month. Sometimes remote through Zoom, sometimes in person at La Mesa Cowork. Join for a dynamic and collaborative exploration of Artificial Intelligence! This isn’t just another lecture – it’s your chance to connect with fellow innovators, share your AI journey, and discover practical ways to revolutionize your business and boost productivity. 8 seats are available at the round table. Everyone will get an opportunity to share their AI interest or experience. Have you been experimenting with AI tools to streamline your workflow? Curious about how others are leveraging AI for business growth? This is the place to share your insights and learn from the community! Additional details: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Q-mjrkLc-U8

events.description: A fun, supportive environment to practice your public speaking skills – for all levels of speakers! This is for YOU – who want to improve + polish their communication. This is a recurring weekly meeting. https://www.meetup.com/palomar-airport-toastmasters/

events.title: San Diego Tech Coffee Online
events.description: The San Diego Tech Coffee Online is an informal gathering of people interested in startups, technology and/or business, modeled after the weekly in person San Diego Tech Coffee gatherings in San Diego, CA. This is an experiment to see if the related communities would value getting together online, sharing projects for which they need feedback and asking questions of other members. Join us online at – https://us02web.zoom.us/j/558502650?pwd=YjViRUZxNy9nV2daMDZkMlBiRW9zZz09 Or, Dial in via Phone: 253-215-8782 Meeting ID: 558 502 650 Passcode: 744238 In between our Tech Coffee, check out this list of resources for startup and tech people in San Diego: http://bit.ly/2HvvF7X-SanDiegoTechCommunity More Tech Coffee related information: www.techcoffeeonline.com Past Tech Coffee Online Notes – https://docs.google.com/document/d/1OckcmzWaXvGtW5YYe-lxFf5QBD2i8skGlbHi8LvscUM/edit# Tech Coffee On Discord (Virtual Coffee Shop for Networking, Jobs, Events) – https://discord.gg/PnRR5wC Tech Coffee on Slack (Discussion) – https://sandiegotechcoffee.slack.com/ Tech Coffee on Telegram (Earn Crypto!) – https://t.me/techcoffeeonline

events.title: San Diego Tech Coffee – Lestat’s-Normal Heights
events.description: The San Diego Tech Coffee is coming up on its 13th year of being the largest weekly gathering of entrepreneurs in San Diego. There’s no pitching, only networking, sharing, brainstorming, mentoring, etc. And we love it when people want to share what they are working on! There is no agenda, which means you can’t be late – come when you can, leave when you need to. Very informal. New to the San Diego startup community? Or just want to be better connected? Check out the Startup San Diego Startup Ecosystem Resources List – https://startupsd.org/resources/

events.title: Tuesday Team Up
events.description: The Group is growing and we are excited to announce the launch of Tuesday Team Up at Maya Moon Collective at 3349 Adams Ave, San Diego, CA 92116 at 9:00 am. This new chapter is more than just a gathering. It is a chance to expand our movement of empowerment, inspiration, and support into the heart of San Diego. Whether you cannot always make it to Feel Good Fridays in Encinitas or you are ready to double down on connection by joining both, Tuesday Team Up was created for you. It is a place where authentic conversations spark new opportunities, where encouragement turns into action, and where people come together to lift each other higher both personally and professionally. Our vision is to create spaces throughout San Diego where everyone feels they belong, where ideas become reality, and where community becomes the foundation for growth. Tuesday Team Up is another step toward that vision and we would love for you to be part of it.

events.title: The Group (North Park) – San Diego’s Best Non-Networking Networking Event
events.description: June An is the visionary founder of The Group, a community born from the triumphs and trials of his own entrepreneurial journey. As a seasoned entrepreneur, June understands the personal and professional challenges that can often feel overwhelming. His journey has taken him through moments of helplessness and hopelessness, which fueled his passion to create a space where others don’t have to navigate these challenges alone. Inspired by his experiences, June founded The Group with a clear mission: to empower individuals to overcome obstacles and pursue their dreams with renewed hope and determination. The Group is more than just a community; it’s a place where dreams are nurtured, resilience is strengthened, and people come together to achieve their full potential—both personally and professionally. Let The Group inspire you to live your dreams with purpose and confidence. https://www.thegroupcoastal.com/

events.title: CEDH
events.description: A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition.

events.title: Legacy
events.description: Legacy is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards No maximum deck size Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used Allows cards from all legal sets unless otherwise banned for power level reasons. With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, and Forest), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age blockâ€”Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forestâ€”are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block. events.title: VibeCraft Power Hour
events.description: VibeCraft is a recurring, collaborative working session where ideas turn into experiments and experiments turn into real builds. Each session is shaped by the group. We review what is emerging, explore new directions, and decide together what is worth building next. This is not a presentation or a passive meetup. It is an active, hands-on environment where members contribute ideas, react to each other’s work, and influence the direction of future sessions. Expect open discussion, live demos, shared whiteboards, and practical exploration. Sessions may include live walkthroughs of tools, automations, and AI agents, discussions around product and company design, rapid demos from members, and breakdowns of different approaches to building with modern AI systems. Topics evolve over time, but the focus remains the same: learning by doing, building in public, and exploring a high-tech, high-touch future grounded in curiosity, collaboration, and thoughtful design. Come ready to participate, share what you are building, and help shape what we explore next as a VibeCraft community.

events.title: San Diego Tech Coffee Meetup
events.description: Del Mar Highlands Sky Deck The San Diego Tech Coffee is in its 14th year of being the largest weekly gathering of entrepreneurs and tech people in San Diego. Stop by for networking, sharing, brainstorming, mentoring, etc. And we love it when people want to share what they are working on! There is no agenda, which means you can’t be late – come when you can, leave when you need to. Very informal. New to the San Diego startup community? Or just want to be better connected? Check out the Startup San Diego “Startup Ecosystem Resource List” at https://startupsd.org/resources/.

location.address: 12841 El Camino Real suite 208, San Diego, CA

events.title: 1 Million Cups
events.description: 1 Million Cups is a weekly event for entrepreneurs, by entrepreneurs — built on the belief that conversations over coffee can accelerate innovation. Each week, two founders take the stage to pitch their business for six minutes, followed by 20 minutes of honest, constructive feedback from a room full of fellow entrepreneurs, business professionals, students, and curious minds. This isn’t a pitch competition — it’s a collaborative environment where ideas are refined, challenges are unpacked, and next steps become clearer. Attendees are encouraged to ask thoughtful questions, offer insights, and share resources that help presenters grow. Whether you’re launching your first startup, scaling your next venture, or just looking to get plugged into your local entrepreneurial ecosystem, 1 Million Cups is where you’ll find the energy, perspective, and community you need to move forward. Come for the coffee. Stay for the conversation. Leave with a better business.

events.title: Startup Quarters Curated Conversations
events.description: Startup Quarters is a weekly gathering held every Wednesday, discussing in breakout groups, Curated Conversations where founders and builders share ideas and work through challenges in real time. Designed to create momentum through consistent connection, it brings together multiple startup-focused interactions in one place, fostering ongoing mentorship, collaboration, and meaningful conversations that build week over week.

events.title: Let’s Cowork in PB!
events.description: Tired of working from your actual home, and want a change of scenery? New to San Diego and want to meet new people? Come join us at our weekly coworking session. It’s a pretty casual affair with new people every week, some regulars, and some just passing through. We take over a few tables at Second Nature in Pacific Beach and do a bit of work and a bit of getting to know each other. Come and go as you please, but do remember to bring your laptop (charged as plugs are few). It’s free to join, but ideally grab some coffee or lunch & tip the servers. A note about RSVPs: This group aims to help people meet in a small group. The max amount of people for this event is 12 people. We close the RSVP list on Mondays if there is a waitlist, and if you can’t make it we do kindly ask that you change your status as soon as you know to give someone else a chance to attend. Those on the waitlist will receive a note on the day of the meetup letting them know if there is space or not. If you sign up, but do not attend, you will be marked as a No Show. People with 5 No Shows will be removed from the group.

events.description: Warhammer is a tabletop miniature wargame with a medieval fantasy theme where players use miniature models to represent a battlefield filled with buildings, trees, hills, and other terrain features.

events.title: Pokémon
events.description: The Pokémon Trading Card Game is a tabletop game that involves collecting, trading and playing with Pokémon themed playing cards. It has its own set of rules but uses many motifs and ideas derived from the Pokémon video games. There are Pokémon cards for every species & type of Pokémon as well as Trainer cards featuring characters, items and other themes of the franchise (each with a different use) and Energy cards to power various actions.

events.title: Pauper
events.description: In this Magic format, all cards must have been printed at common rarity in a Magic set or product. Common promo cards are only legal if the card meets that qualification. If a common version of a particular card was ever released in Magic, any version of that card is legal in this format. Other than that, the usual rules for constructed decks apply: Minimum of 60 cards in the main deck (There is no maximum size for main decks) Up to 15 cards in your sideboard, if used No more than 4 of any individual card in the main deck and sideboard combined (with the exception of basic lands)

events.title: Standard Showdown
events.description: The Standard format uses the most recently released Magic sets. Standard is a dynamic format where you build decks and play using cards in your collection from recently released Magic sets. Evolving gameplay and fresh strategies make it one of the most fun and popular ways to play Magic.

events.title: Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain Discussion
events.description: Join us for an engaging discussion on the latest advancements in Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain technology. Our event will cover topics such as Machine Learning, Ethereum, Smart Contracts, and how these innovations are shaping the future of Decentralized Systems & Applications. Whether you are a tech enthusiast, developer, or industry professional, this event will offer valuable insights into the intersection of AI and Blockchain. In this meetup, we will dive deep into the potential of leveraging AI within Blockchain networks, the impact of Cryptocurrency on decentralized systems, and the evolution of Bitcoin in the digital landscape. From understanding the basics of Blockchain to exploring real-world applications of Smart Contracts, this event is designed to cater to individuals at all levels of expertise. Don’t miss this opportunity to network with like-minded individuals and gain valuable knowledge about the dynamic world of Artificial Intelligence and Blockchain.

events.title: CEDH
events.description: A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition.

events.title: Modern
events.description: Modern is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards Cards from Core Set Eighth Edition and Mirrodin through today No maximum deck size, as long as you can shuffle your deck unassisted Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and Wastes), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age block Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forest are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block.

events.title: Nat at Night
events.description: On Fridays, the San Diego Natural History Museum is open until 11 PM just for you. Enjoy access to all open exhibitions and our rooftop deck for good views and great vibes. The rooftop is open to all visitors and serves beer, wine, non-alcoholic drinks, and food from Wolf in the Woods. Take a selfie with a megalodon shark, see scientists at work in our Paleo Center, admire glittering gems, get a close up look at spiders and snakes, and so much more. Best of all, get a look at our brand-new exhibition, Washed Ashore: Art to Save the Sea and grab a drink in the dive bar while chatting with a scientist. On select dates, channel your inner artist with Sketchparty, get competitive during trivia, or unwind while listening to the rooftop DJ. Hungry for more? The Craft Taco, located inside the Atrium, is a vibrant taco restaurant offering a delectable fusion of flavors and artisanal creations. They offer made-to-order tacos featuring signature blue corn tortillas, delectable burritos and bowls, and a variety of drinks and coffees. Admission is half-price after 5 PM, and parking is free after 6 PM. You can buy your tickets online in advance or buy them at the door the night of. As always, members of The Nat and Explorer pass holders enjoy free general admission. Fill your summer with wonder at The Nat. With weekly Nat at Night, brunches, weekend family programming, and so much more, membership just makes sense. Not to mention, you’re directly supporting nature conservation in our region. Join us today.

events.title: MOJO BROOKZZ
events.description:
Dyon “Mojo” Brooks is an actor, writer, producer, and nationally acclaimed stand-up comedian that can currently be seen as the male lead for Tyler Perry’s MISS GOVERNOR on Netflix alongside the legendary Terri J Vaughn and Jo Marie Payton, the series held a multiple-week Top 10 for following its debut. As a comic, Brooks is celebrated by fans across the country, having sold out every show on his nationwide “Unhand Me Now” Tour in 2024, as well as selling out each stop on his current fourth national headlining tour (The “I Know You F*ckin’ Lying” Tour). The tour has sold over 86,000 tickets and will conclude at the end January of 2026 with stops at multiple large-capacity theatres across the nation including the historic Chicago Theatre, the Orpheum, and two shows at New York’s Town Hall to name a few. From February to May of 2026, Brooks will again tour with Mike Epps for the “We Them Ones” Arena Tour playing to audiences of 10,000+ every weekend once again. Brooks will embark on another national theatre and arena tour as a headliner playing even larger venues than any other time in his career. The demand, fandom, and passion for Brooks is palpable across not only the country, but internationally as he will be touring across London and the EU in Summer 2026 as well.

December of 2025 marked the recording of Mojo’s taping for his debut one-hour Stand-Up Special, shot in front of three sold out crowds at Atlanta’s Center Stage Theatre. The special was directed by Mike Lavin who has previously directed the likes of Shane Gillis, Nate Bargatze, and Jim Gaffigan to name a few. 

Brooks was named as one of Variety’s 10 Comics to Watch for 2025 where he was recognized and honored at the historic Just For Laughs Montreal. The prestigious list includes such notable alumni as Kevin Hart, Amy Schumer, Patton Oswalt, Tiffany Haddish, and Kumail Nanjiani to name just a few.

Additionally, Dyon can be seen on the most recent Season [21] of Nick Cannon’s WILD N OUT , having been hand selected by Cannon who is a fan of Brooks viral sketch comedy videos that can be found across his social media pages , which have amassed millions of views and over 28 million likes. Brooks counts more than 1.7 million Instagram followers, 2.3 million Facebook followers, and over 1.5 million TikTok followers. 

 A Chicago native, Brooks frequently collaborates with the Chicago Bears and contributed to a midwest Emmy win for his writing efforts across commercial and digital work, which the team continues to collaborate with him on. Additionally, Brooks has lent his comedic acting  talents to music videos including Lil Durk’s hit Sad Songs . When not entertaining audiences, Brooks gives back to his community in Chicago, dedicating his time and resources to support youth development programs, demonstrating his commitment to making a positive impact beyond the stage and screen.

No refunds or exchanges.
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest. 
 Ages 21+

events.title: MOJO BROOKZZ
events.description:
Dyon “Mojo” Brooks is an actor, writer, producer, and nationally acclaimed stand-up comedian that can currently be seen as the male lead for Tyler Perry’s MISS GOVERNOR on Netflix alongside the legendary Terri J Vaughn and Jo Marie Payton, the series held a multiple-week Top 10 for following its debut. As a comic, Brooks is celebrated by fans across the country, having sold out every show on his nationwide “Unhand Me Now” Tour in 2024, as well as selling out each stop on his current fourth national headlining tour (The “I Know You F*ckin’ Lying” Tour). The tour has sold over 86,000 tickets and will conclude at the end January of 2026 with stops at multiple large-capacity theatres across the nation including the historic Chicago Theatre, the Orpheum, and two shows at New York’s Town Hall to name a few. From February to May of 2026, Brooks will again tour with Mike Epps for the “We Them Ones” Arena Tour playing to audiences of 10,000+ every weekend once again. Brooks will embark on another national theatre and arena tour as a headliner playing even larger venues than any other time in his career. The demand, fandom, and passion for Brooks is palpable across not only the country, but internationally as he will be touring across London and the EU in Summer 2026 as well.

December of 2025 marked the recording of Mojo’s taping for his debut one-hour Stand-Up Special, shot in front of three sold out crowds at Atlanta’s Center Stage Theatre. The special was directed by Mike Lavin who has previously directed the likes of Shane Gillis, Nate Bargatze, and Jim Gaffigan to name a few. 

Brooks was named as one of Variety’s 10 Comics to Watch for 2025 where he was recognized and honored at the historic Just For Laughs Montreal. The prestigious list includes such notable alumni as Kevin Hart, Amy Schumer, Patton Oswalt, Tiffany Haddish, and Kumail Nanjiani to name just a few.

Additionally, Dyon can be seen on the most recent Season [21] of Nick Cannon’s WILD N OUT , having been hand selected by Cannon who is a fan of Brooks viral sketch comedy videos that can be found across his social media pages , which have amassed millions of views and over 28 million likes. Brooks counts more than 1.7 million Instagram followers, 2.3 million Facebook followers, and over 1.5 million TikTok followers. 

 A Chicago native, Brooks frequently collaborates with the Chicago Bears and contributed to a midwest Emmy win for his writing efforts across commercial and digital work, which the team continues to collaborate with him on. Additionally, Brooks has lent his comedic acting  talents to music videos including Lil Durk’s hit Sad Songs . When not entertaining audiences, Brooks gives back to his community in Chicago, dedicating his time and resources to support youth development programs, demonstrating his commitment to making a positive impact beyond the stage and screen.

No refunds or exchanges.
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest. 
 Ages 21+

events.title: Online: Saturday Study Group
events.description: We are continuing our Study group online for the time being. We are now meeting online in the San Diego Python Discord server! Here is the link to join: https://discord.gg/8GpWuVvdbQ There is a “meetup” voice/video channel that we will be using! We may start meeting in person soon! This will be discussed at the meetup. We are considering having some online, some in-person. Decisions, decisions! Every Saturday we meet up with the larger Python community (including San Diego PyLadies (https://www.meetup.com/sd-pyladies), Open BioInformatics San Diego (https://www.meetup.com/Open-Bioinformatics-SanDiego/) and San Diego Artificial Intelligence (https://www.meetup.com/SanDiegoAI/)) to chat, learn, and hang out. Our study group is for everyone on their Python journey, whether you’re just starting out, an experienced pro, or anywhere in between. There’s no formal instruction, just a bunch of folks who like to code. Sometimes we talk about a specific subject, sometimes answer questions, whatever! Python Community Code of Conduct (http://www.pythonsd.org/coc): Python is much more than a software language. Python is a vibrant community made up of members from around the globe with a diverse set of skills, personalities, and experiences. It is through these differences that our community experiences great successes and continued growth. Overall, we’re good to each other. We contribute to this community not because we have to, but because we want to.

events.title: REMOTE: Pyladies/San Diego PUG Co-Study Group
events.description: We are continuing our Study group online for the time being. We are now meeting online in the San Diego Python Discord server! Here is the link to join: https://discord.gg/8GpWuVvdbQ There is a “meetup” voice/video channel that we will be using! The SD Python User Group Meetup is https://www.meetup.com/pythonsd/ – there is a Saturday Study group hosted by the SD Python Meetup group. Every Saturday we meet up with the larger Python community to chat, learn, and hang out. Our study group is for everyone on their Python journey, whether you’re just starting out, you’re an experienced pro, or anywhere in between. There’s no formal instruction, just a bunch of folks who like to code. We have a great time–come join us! Free feel to show up at any time and stay as long as you want. Hope to see you there! Python Community Code of Conduct (https://www.python.org/psf/codeofconduct/): Python is much more than a software language. Python is a vibrant community made up of members from around the globe with a diverse set of skills, personalities, and experiences. It is through these differences that our community experiences great successes and continued growth. Overall, we’re good to each other. We contribute to this community not because we have to, but because we want to.

events.title: Pokémon
events.description: The Pokémon Trading Card Game is a tabletop game that involves collecting, trading and playing with Pokémon themed playing cards. It has its own set of rules but uses many motifs and ideas derived from the Pokémon video games. There are Pokémon cards for every species & type of Pokémon as well as Trainer cards featuring characters, items and other themes of the franchise (each with a different use) and Energy cards to power various actions.

events.title: CEDH – Bronze Event
events.description: TopDeck Championship Series – Bronze Event A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition. *Event May be downgraded to based on attendance*

events.title: Modern
events.description: Modern is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards Cards from Core Set Eighth Edition and Mirrodin through today No maximum deck size, as long as you can shuffle your deck unassisted Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, Forest, and Wastes), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age block Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forest are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block.

events.title: Diving deeper into software development discussions
events.description: • What we’ll do Come join in discussions about Vim, Emacs, Linux, Elixir, Erlang, OTP, Lisp, Haskell, Ruby, Javascript, Functional Programming, Object Oriented Programming, Blockchain, HashGraph, Distributed Systems, Decentralized Systems, and whatever peaks your interest. • What to bring A laptop, good questions, exciting ideas, an open mind and a positive attitude. • Where to meet Meet by the corn hole game in the back and look for others with laptops. Ask for others that are there for the software discussion group. • Alternate location Some nights the Rabbit Hole has live music. I was not able to pin down an exact schedule, just sometimes we would show up and there would be a band but most times there was not. As an alternate location on live music nights the discussion group could be moved just down the street to Lestats coffee shop. They have great coffee and a nice atmosphere for hanging out and discussing software. • Important to know This is a self organizing open discussion group that is meant to be organic, with no main speaker or leader. It is just a time and a place for like minded people to come together to talk about software. • Background When I was staying in SD (Jan-Aug 2018) I was teaching software development at UCSD and the Meetup gave my cohort a space to dive further into topics that the curriculum did not allow time for. When the class was over and I went back to traveling full time, I thought it would be an interesting experiment to leave the Meetup going and see if people would still gather to discuss software without someone to lead it. I liked the idea of a group that did not require some one to always lead it. A lot off Meetups I have been to before were always one or two people doing a lot of work to organize and put on talks but everyone else just shows up to consume and doesn’t really participate that much. Kinda like going to a movie, in a way. The idea for this group is that it is what people make it. You get out of it what you put into it. It’s great that people are showing up still and I hope you have some great discussions. When I get back to San Diego (planning on a handful of times a year) and there is a meeting I will stop by and hang out and invite my cohort of previous students.

events.title: MOJO BROOKZZ
events.description:
Dyon “Mojo” Brooks is an actor, writer, producer, and nationally acclaimed stand-up comedian that can currently be seen as the male lead for Tyler Perry’s MISS GOVERNOR on Netflix alongside the legendary Terri J Vaughn and Jo Marie Payton, the series held a multiple-week Top 10 for following its debut. As a comic, Brooks is celebrated by fans across the country, having sold out every show on his nationwide “Unhand Me Now” Tour in 2024, as well as selling out each stop on his current fourth national headlining tour (The “I Know You F*ckin’ Lying” Tour). The tour has sold over 86,000 tickets and will conclude at the end January of 2026 with stops at multiple large-capacity theatres across the nation including the historic Chicago Theatre, the Orpheum, and two shows at New York’s Town Hall to name a few. From February to May of 2026, Brooks will again tour with Mike Epps for the “We Them Ones” Arena Tour playing to audiences of 10,000+ every weekend once again. Brooks will embark on another national theatre and arena tour as a headliner playing even larger venues than any other time in his career. The demand, fandom, and passion for Brooks is palpable across not only the country, but internationally as he will be touring across London and the EU in Summer 2026 as well.

December of 2025 marked the recording of Mojo’s taping for his debut one-hour Stand-Up Special, shot in front of three sold out crowds at Atlanta’s Center Stage Theatre. The special was directed by Mike Lavin who has previously directed the likes of Shane Gillis, Nate Bargatze, and Jim Gaffigan to name a few. 

Brooks was named as one of Variety’s 10 Comics to Watch for 2025 where he was recognized and honored at the historic Just For Laughs Montreal. The prestigious list includes such notable alumni as Kevin Hart, Amy Schumer, Patton Oswalt, Tiffany Haddish, and Kumail Nanjiani to name just a few.

Additionally, Dyon can be seen on the most recent Season [21] of Nick Cannon’s WILD N OUT , having been hand selected by Cannon who is a fan of Brooks viral sketch comedy videos that can be found across his social media pages , which have amassed millions of views and over 28 million likes. Brooks counts more than 1.7 million Instagram followers, 2.3 million Facebook followers, and over 1.5 million TikTok followers. 

 A Chicago native, Brooks frequently collaborates with the Chicago Bears and contributed to a midwest Emmy win for his writing efforts across commercial and digital work, which the team continues to collaborate with him on. Additionally, Brooks has lent his comedic acting  talents to music videos including Lil Durk’s hit Sad Songs . When not entertaining audiences, Brooks gives back to his community in Chicago, dedicating his time and resources to support youth development programs, demonstrating his commitment to making a positive impact beyond the stage and screen.

No refunds or exchanges.
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest. 
 Ages 21+

events.title: NACYA MARREIRO
events.description:
Hailing from Portugal and now based in Los Angeles, Nacya Marreiro 
is a multilingual stand-up comedian and actress with a bold, 
international voice. Fluent in Portuguese, Spanish, English, and basic
French, she brings sharp cultural insight and fearless honesty to both
stage and screen.

Nacya is the creator of The Riff , an improv stand-up show selected for the New York Comedy Festival 2024 . Her acclaimed one-woman show Blood in the Gill was featured at the 2025 New York Comedy Festival at the Triad Theater , while her stand-up hour “Pretty Privilege” runs monthly at The Comedy Store . Trained by renowned acting coach Julie Areola , Nacya’s acting credits include Adrianna in the BET film Hands , Gigi in The Contract (BET) , Valentine in Moving Valentine (Amazon Prime) , and Rita in the award-winning comedy short Down Way to Mexico .

Ages 21+
Fee applies if transferring confirmed ticket purchase to another guest.
Doors for open for seating approximately 30 minutes before late showtimes. Please note, doors are subject to prior show endtime and may change without notice, beyond our control.
No refunds or exchanges.

events.title: A Space for All of Us
events.description: Sensory-Friendly Quiet Mornings at the Museum The Museum’s sensory-friendly quiet hours are held every Sunday from 10 a.m. to 11 a.m. This time is dedicated to creating a calmer, quieter museum experience. We remain open to the public during this time, and all are welcome to visit the Museum. During this time, specific exhibit features in the main Museum building will be turned off to minimize audio and visual stimulation. Noise-blocking headphones are free for check-out at the main Admissions desk and the entrance to the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibition, Exhibit features in the Cannibals: Myth & Reality exhibit will remain on, and all exhibits will be fully turned on at 11 a.m. Admission purchased during sensory-friendly quiet hours is the same as standard admission and applies to the entire day. Please contact publicprograms@museumofus.org with any questions.

events.title: NURSES COMEDY BREAK
events.description:
Get ready for a side-splitting night of comedy that brings the wild and 
wonderful world of nursing to life! From hectic hospital shifts to the quirks of 
patient care, this show delivers a dose of humor that every nurse—and 
anyone who’s ever been in a hospital—can appreciate.

Featuring stand-up routines from real nurses and seasoned comedians, 
Nurses Comedy Break is the perfect escape from the daily grind. Whether 
you’re on the front lines or just love a good laugh, this show will have
you in stitches—no sutures required!

Join us at Mic Drop Comedy for a night where the only prescription is 
laughter! 

Ages 21+

events.title: Little Italy San Diego Tech Coffee
events.description: We’re bringing back Tech Coffee to the greater downtown area after a few year hiatus. What better spot for Tech Coffee than Little Italy, the coffee capital of San Diego! Join us at Portal Coffee overlooking the beautiful Waterfront Park and San Diego Harbor. There’s lots of available street parking along Kettner between Fir and Beech St just 1-3 blocks from Portal Coffee. See the Parking Map attached image. We’re also one block from the MTA Trolley and 2 blocks from Amtrak and the Coaster. Come by for some networking, sharing, brainstorming, and mentoring. And we love it when people want to share what they are working on! There is no agenda, which means you can’t be late – come when you can, leave when you need to. Very informal. New to the San Diego startup community? Or just want to be better connected? Check out the SD Tech Scene calendar – https://sdtechscene.org

events.title: Rockets Adventures
events.description: When: Every Monday at 6:00pm-9:00pm Entry Fee (Weekly): $5 Character Creation Fee: $5 How do you join? -Anyone can join! In order to join a player must pay a $5 Character creation fee or use a pre-made character. Players will be able to attend every week or whenever they feel like it. What is a Character Creation fee? -All characters for DnD at Rockets will have to be made through our DND Beyond account. Players will have access to bonus expansions this way. Characters will be kept on the TC’s Rockets account and be made in-store as well. How does it work? -There will be a set amount of Dungeon Masters provided by Rockets. Each DM will adventure with 5 players. All stories will be provided by Rockets along with in game accessories. What else is there? -Every week you’ll receive raffle tickets with your entry fee. Raffles will vary every week!

There’s no pitching, only networking, sharing, brainstorming, mentoring, etc. And we love it when people want to share what they are working on! There is no agenda, which means you can’t be late – come when you can, leave when you need to. Very informal. New to the San Diego startup community? Or just want to be better connected? events.title: Tuesday Team Up
events.description: The Group is growing and we are excited to announce the launch of Tuesday Team Up at Maya Moon Collective at 3349 Adams Ave, San Diego, CA 92116 at 9:00 am. This new chapter is more than just a gathering. It is a chance to expand our movement of empowerment, inspiration, and support into the heart of San Diego. Whether you cannot always make it to Feel Good Fridays in Encinitas or you are ready to double down on connection by joining both, Tuesday Team Up was created for you. It is a place where authentic conversations spark new opportunities, where encouragement turns into action, and where people come together to lift each other higher both personally and professionally. Our vision is to create spaces throughout San Diego where everyone feels they belong, where ideas become reality, and where community becomes the foundation for growth. Tuesday Team Up is another step toward that vision and we would love for you to be part of it.

events.title: The Group (North Park) – San Diego’s Best Non-Networking Networking Event
events.description: June An is the visionary founder of The Group, a community born from the triumphs and trials of his own entrepreneurial journey. As a seasoned entrepreneur, June understands the personal and professional challenges that can often feel overwhelming. His journey has taken him through moments of helplessness and hopelessness, which fueled his passion to create a space where others don’t have to navigate these challenges alone. Inspired by his experiences, June founded The Group with a clear mission: to empower individuals to overcome obstacles and pursue their dreams with renewed hope and determination. The Group is more than just a community; it’s a place where dreams are nurtured, resilience is strengthened, and people come together to achieve their full potential—both personally and professionally. Let The Group inspire you to live your dreams with purpose and confidence. https://www.thegroupcoastal.com/

events.title: San Diego’s NFT, Digital Art and Cocktails Meetup
events.description: Join Botanica’s NFT Meetup Group for an evening of digital art and cocktails. Discover the fascinating world of NFTs and how they are revolutionizing the arts and entertainment industry. Connect with fellow enthusiasts, artists, and collectors as we explore new frontiers in creativity and technology, while enjoying cocktails and bites. During this event, we will have a showcase of stunning digital artwork created by talented artists in the NFT space as well as a meet & greet with a local NFT expert. Let’s engage in lively discussions about the impact of NFTs on the art world and exchange ideas on how to navigate this emerging market & the opportunities around it. As you mingle with like-minded individuals, enjoy a variety of specially crafted cocktails inspired by the wonders of digital art. Our mixologists have carefully curated a menu that embodies the spirit and vibrancy of this exciting movement. Whether you are an artist, collector, or simply curious about NFTs, this meetup is the perfect opportunity to delve into the world of digital art and meet other local folks that care about the same things!

events.title: CEDH
events.description: A high-powered variant of the Magic: The Gathering Commander or Elder Dragon Highlander (EDH) format. CEDH is distinguished by its emphasis on efficiency, powerful card interactions, and a competitive approach to deck building and gameplay. This format allows for the use of nearly all Magic: The Gathering cards, encouraging players to construct decks that aim for maximum potency and consistency. The goal is often to win quickly through combos or to establish overwhelming control, making it appealing to players who enjoy a high level of strategic complexity and competition.

events.title: Legacy
events.description: Legacy is a constructed format and therefore adheres to the following constructed rules: Minimum of sixty cards No maximum deck size Up to fifteen cards in your sideboard, if used Allows cards from all legal sets unless otherwise banned for power level reasons. With the exception of basic lands (Plains, Island, Swamp, Mountain, and Forest), your combined deck and sideboard may not contain more than four of any individual card, counted by its English card title equivalent. Note: The five snow-covered lands from the Ice Age blockâ€”Snow-Covered Plains, Snow-Covered Island, Snow-Covered Swamp, Snow-Covered Mountain, and Snow-Covered Forestâ€”are also basic lands. Snow-covered lands are only permitted in formats that allow expansions from the Ice Age block. Legacy decks may consist of cards from allÂ MagicÂ card sets, plus the following cards: Sewers of Estark, Windseeker Centaur, and Nalathni Dragon (Mana Crypt would also be included were it not currently bannedâ€”see below). Cards from expansions and special sets (likeÂ From the Vault,Â Magic: The Gatheringâ€”Commander,Â Duel Decks,Â Conspiracy, etc.) are legal in the Legacy format on the date of release of the expansion or special set.

events.title: VibeCraft Power Hour
events.description: VibeCraft is a recurring, collaborative working session where ideas turn into experiments and experiments turn into real builds. Each session is shaped by the group. We review what is emerging, explore new directions, and decide together what is worth building next. This is not a presentation or a passive meetup. It is an active, hands-on environment where members contribute ideas, react to each other’s work, and influence the direction of future sessions. Expect open discussion, live demos, shared whiteboards, and practical exploration. Sessions may include live walkthroughs of tools, automations, and AI agents, discussions around product and company design, rapid demos from members, and breakdowns of different approaches to building with modern AI systems. Topics evolve over time, but the focus remains the same: learning by doing, building in public, and exploring a high-tech, high-touch future grounded in curiosity, collaboration, and thoughtful design. Come ready to participate, share what you are building, and help shape what we explore next as a VibeCraft community.

events.title: 1 Million Cups
events.description: 1 Million Cups is a weekly event for entrepreneurs, by entrepreneurs — built on the belief that conversations over coffee can accelerate innovation. Each week, two founders take the stage to pitch their business for six minutes, followed by 20 minutes of honest, constructive feedback from a room full of fellow entrepreneurs, business professionals, students, and curious minds. This isn’t a pitch competition — it’s a collaborative environment where ideas are refined, challenges are unpacked, and next steps become clearer. Attendees are encouraged to ask thoughtful questions, offer insights, and share resources that help presenters grow. Whether you’re launching your first startup, scaling your next venture, or just looking to get plugged into your local entrepreneurial ecosystem, 1 Million Cups is where you’ll find the energy, perspective, and community you need to move forward. Come for the coffee. Stay for the conversation. Leave with a better business.

events.title: Let’s Cowork in PB!
events.description: Tired of working from your actual home, and want a change of scenery? New to San Diego and want to meet new people? Come join us at our weekly coworking session. It’s a pretty casual affair with new people every week, some regulars, and some just passing through. We take over a few tables at Second Nature in Pacific Beach and do a bit of work and a bit of getting to know each other. Come and go as you please, but do remember to bring your laptop (charged as plugs are few). It’s free to join, but ideally grab some coffee or lunch & tip the servers. A note about RSVPs: This group aims to help people meet in a small group. The max amount of people for this event is 12 people. We close the RSVP list on Mondays if there is a waitlist, and if you can’t make it we do kindly ask that you change your status as soon as you know to give someone else a chance to attend. Those on the waitlist will receive a note on the day of the meetup letting them know if there is space or not. If you sign up, but do not attend, you will be marked as a No Show. People with 5 No Shows will be removed from the group.

events.title: Carlsbad Coworking
events.description: We are all writers. Whether you’re writing copy for your next launch, tackling those client emails, drafting your newsletter, or finally updating your resume – bring your laptop and get it done. We meet every other week, 2:30-4:30pm at Wildland in Carlsbad. Structure: – 30 min: Intros & set your intention (what are you working on today?) – 60 min: Focused work time (no distractions, everyone’s heads down) – 30 min: Share progress, get feedback, ask questions This is for anyone who writes as part of their work: founders, marketers, consultants, creatives, job searchers. If you’re tired of procrastinating alone, come procrastinate with us (and then actually get stuff done). Details: This event is FREE. Please support Wildland by grabbing a coffee, tea, or treat while you’re there. RSVP to secure your spot. Refund policy Paid tickets are non-refundable and all sales are final.

events.title: Pokémon
events.description: The Pokémon Trading Card Game is a tabletop game that involves collecting, trading and playing with Pokémon themed playing cards. It has its own set of rules but uses many motifs and ideas derived from the Pokémon video games. There are Pokémon cards for every species & type of Pokémon as well as Trainer cards featuring characters, items and other themes of the franchise (each with a different use) and Energy cards to power various actions.

events.title: Pauper
events.description: In this Magic format, all cards must have been printed at common rarity in a Magic set or product. Common promo cards are only legal if the card meets that qualification. If a common version of a particular card was ever released in Magic, any version of that card is legal in this format. Other than that, the usual rules for constructed decks apply: Minimum of 60 cards in the main deck (There is no maximum size for main decks) Up to 15 cards in your sideboard, if used No more than 4 of any individual card in the main deck and sideboard combined (with the exception of basic lands)

events.title: Standard Showdown
events.description: The Standard format uses the most recently released Magic sets. Standard is a dynamic format where you build decks and play using cards in your collection from recently released Magic sets. Evolving gameplay and fresh strategies make it one of the most fun and popular ways to play Magic.

events.title: DARREN CARTER
events.description:
Darren Carter, also known as "The Party Starter," is an American 
stand-up comedian and actor born in Fresno, California. He has been 
entertaining audiences for over 20 years with his unique style of 
comedy, which blends observational humor and physical comedy.

Darren
Carter's hilarious Instagram Reels, TikTok videos, YouTube Shorts, and 
podcast have helped him amass over 300,000 followers worldwide.

Carter's
comedy career began in the 1990s when he performed at open-mic nights 
in Los Angeles. He quickly gained recognition for his energetic stage 
presence and relatable humor, which led to appearances on popular TV 
shows such as "The Tonight Show with Jay Leno," "Comedy Central 
Presents," and "Premium Blend."

Over the years, Carter has 
released several comedy albums, including "Shady Side" and "That 
Ginger's Crazy." He has also performed live all over the country, 
including at The Comedy Store, The Laugh Factory, and The Improv.

Fresh
off the heels of his Dry Bar Comedy Special, "Calm Down," Darren Carter
is set to be in the upcoming film "Born Again," slated for release this
year.

In addition to his comedy work, Carter has also acted in a 
number of films and TV shows, including "Be Cool," "Who Made The Potatoe
Salad," and "Love Chronicles." He also hosts his own podcast, "Pocket 
Party," where he interviews fellow comedians and shares stories from his
own life.

Carter is known for his high-energy performances, 
infectious laughter, and ability to connect with audiences of all ages 
and backgrounds. He continues to tour and perform regularly, bringing 
laughter and joy to fans across the country.

When Carter is home 
in Los Angeles, he can be found going to Guitar Center with his teenage 
son and watching him play every instrument in the building until they 
get kicked out.

events.description:
JT
Tomlinson is a Los Angeles-based stand-up comedian whose blend of 
neurotic self-deprecation and witty observational comedy makes him an 
intriguing and insightful joke-teller. Drawing from his experiences as a
recovered addict, former fat person, and current twenty-something, JT’s
comedy is simultaneously silly and wise.

JT
has recently been on the road opening for Emily Catalano. When in LA, 
he performs weekly with Don’t Tell Comedy in Los Angeles, and recently 
launched his own Don’t Tell chapters in Long Beach and Palm Springs. He 
has appeared at the World-Famous Comedy Store, the Hollywood Improv, and
Westside Comedy Theater, and in 2023 began hosting at the Irvine Improv
and Oxnard Levity Live. His stand-up and sketch comedy videos have 
garnered over 50 million views across platforms.

events.title: Ansir Cowork: First Friday Open Coworking and Lunch
events.description: Experience the collaborative energy of Ansir Cowork’s vibrant workspace, designed to inspire innovation and focus. Whether you’re a freelancer, entrepreneur, or remote worker, join us for a day filled with networking opportunities, complimentary amenities, and a supportive environment. Don’t miss this chance to elevate your workday, forge new connections, and make your first Friday even more productive. Let’s grab lunch. We’re in the heart of the Convoy Asian Food District so there are plenty of restaurants to choose from. We’re a 3 minute walk from the nearest restaurants. Lunch is 11:30 at restaurant Steamy Piggy (or whatever we decide as a group) Please RSVP to join First Friday Coworking.

events.title: UX Speakeasy Cafe – Coworking Fridays
events.description: ✨ First Fridays of each month ✨ Looking for a perspective change? Want to connect with other UX Designers over coffee and pixels? Join us for UX Speakeasy Cafe—a casual monthly co-working meetup for UXers of all levels and disciplines. Bring your laptop, grab a drink, and settle in for a few hours of working alongside fellow designers. There’s no formal agenda—just shared energy, spontaneous design chats, feedback swaps, and a vibe check from the community. 🗓️ When: First Fridays of each month 📍 Where: Rotating cafe’s 💻 What to Bring: Your laptop, good vibes ☕ Why: Stay inspired, stay connected

events.title: [ONLINE] EE Business Networking @Lunch (free!)
events.description: Why go it alone? It only takes one person to change our lives personally or professionally. Who will you meet today at “EE“? See additional info and direct links below… Exceptional Entrepreneurs is one of the USA’s largest business networking organizations – and one of the best values in business networking. At EE: There is no membership required. There is no attendance requirement. There is no business profession or category exclusivity. ALL business professions are welcome to attend! Meeting Agenda: First 30 minutes: check-in & breakout rooms open. Remaining 60 minutes: structured agenda with everyone in main room, including introductions by participants + more! While meetings end on time (for those who need to go), we also open breakout rooms again for those wanting to meet with other attendees afterwards (at least 30 minutes, usually longer.. fyi). Interested in being the featured speaker for this event? Information and registration is at: http://EEspeaker.com Info to join this EE business networking meeting: ####################################### http://nroi.us/eeonline (the shortlink above will remain updated with the current Zoom link) Passcode: 7772021 ####################################### Limited to the first 100 participants. We also cross-livestream through the EE FB Group. No participant limit on FB, however it’s view-only mode. Please feel free to leave your contact info in the comments of the livestream when listening along: https://www.facebook.com/groups/networkingeesd The meetings (including chat) will be recorded to the cloud – and made available temporarily afterwards. Information on recordings will be provided to attendees on Zoom and Facebook. Exceptional Entrepreneurs was founded November, 2008 in San Diego, California USA by David & Gina Demangos. Since then, “EE” has become one of the USA’s largest business networking organizations, due to the Demangos’ vision and leadership to provide “exceptional” opportunities for connecting local professionals. David Demangos is an eXp real estate agent in San Diego. For more information on David, please see: Awesome San Diego Real Estate: http://www.awesomesandiegorealestate.com “Effective networking is a learned skill. Exceptional Entrepreneurs are highly trusted professionals who share ideas and know that in order to receive you must first be willing to give.” – David Demangos Thank you for your continuing support of “EE”! See you at the next event.
